## PAGE 00944: Replaced and renamed elements

- page_id: `227158130`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227158130/Replaced+and+renamed+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-05-12T09:57:35.019+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual > UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.1 > Model migration > Elements mapping
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The replaced elements mean that their names and/or metaclasses are changed. Pay attention, that some elements from UPDM 2.1 are replaced to one element in UAF 1.1.

| UPDM 2.1 | UAF 1.1 | Comments |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Activity Part Of Capability [Dependency] | Maps To Capability [Abstraction] | Maps To Capability relationship denotes that an Activity contributes to providing a Capability. |
| Activity Performed By Performer [Dependency]Is Capable Of Performing [Dependency] | Is Capable To Perform [Abstraction] | Is Capable To Perform relationship defines the traceability between the Capable Elements to the Activities that they can perform. |
| Actual Organization Relationship | Actual Resource Relationship | Actual Resource Relationship is an abstract element that details the Actual Organizational Resources that are able to carry out an Actual Responsibility. |
| Actual Property | Actual Measurement | Actual Measurement is an actual value that is applied to a Measurement. |
| Capability Of Performer [Dependency] | Exhibits [Abstraction] | Exhibits relationship that exists between a capable element and a Capability that it meets under specific environmental conditions. |
| Capability Property | Capability Role |  |
| ClimateLight Condition | Environment | A definition of the environmental factors in which something exists or functions. The definition of an Environment element can be further defined using EnvironmentKind. |
| Deployed MilestoneNo Longer Used MilestoneOut Of Service MilestoneIncrement MilestoneActual Project Milestone | Actual Project Milestone | Actual Project Milestone is an event with a start date in an Actual Project from which progress is measured. Actual Project Milestone can have such kinds:In Service.Deployed.No Longer Used.Out Of Service.Other. |
| EnergyMateriel | Natural Resource | Natural Resource is any type of physical resource that occurs in nature such as oil, water, gas, or coal. |
| Exchange Element | Data ElementInformation Element | If the Exchange Element was conveyed by Resource Interaction it is migrated to Data Element. The Data Element is formalized representation of data that is managed by or exchanged between systems.If the Exchange Element was conveyed by Operational Exchange or wasn't conveyed, it is migrated to Information Element. The Information Element is an item of information that flows between Operational Performers and is produced and consumed by the Operational Activities that the Operational Performers are capable to perform (see Is Capable To Perform). |
| Functional Standard | Standard | A ratified and peer-reviewed specification that is used to guide or constrain the architecture. A Standard may be applied to any element in the architecture. |
| Function Edge [Activity Edge] | Functional Control Flow [Control Flow]Functional Object Flow [Object Flow] | Function Edge is an abstract grouping for Function Control Flow and Function Object Flow.Function Object Flow connects Function Actions using Pins.Function Control Flow connects Function Actions directly. |
| Project | Actual Project | Actual Project defines a time-limited endeavor to provide a specific set of Actual Resources that meet specific Capability needs. |
| Individual Person Role | Actual Post | An actual, specific post, is an instance of a Post "type" - e.g., "President of the United States of America" where the Post would be president. |
| Location | Actual Location | Actual Location is an Actual State that describes a physical location, for example using text to provide an address, Geo-coordinates, etc. |
| Location Type | Location | Location element denote a specification of the generic area in which a location holder is required to be located. |
| Logical Architecture | Operational Architecture | Operational Architecture is an element used to denote a model of the architecture, described from the business operational perspective. |
| Measure | Actual Measurement Set | Actual Measurement Set denote a set of Actual Measurements. |
| Measure TypeSecurity Attributes Group | Measurement Set | Measurement Set denote a set of Measurements. |
| Milestone Sequence | Milestone Dependency | Milestone Dependency relationship is relationship between two Actual Project Milestones that denotes one Actual Project Milestone follows from another. |
| Needline | Operational Connector | Operational Connector is a Connector that goes between Operational Roles representing a need to exchange resources. It can carry a number of Operational Exchanges. |
| Node Operation | Operational Method | Operational Method is a behavioral feature of a Operational Performer whose behavior is specified in an Operational Activity. |
| Node Parent | Operational Agent | An abstract element grouping Operational Architecture and Operational Performer. |
| Node Port | Operational Port | Operational Port is used of a Operational Performer or Operational Architecture in the context of another Operational Performer or Operational Architecture. The whole-part relationship is created. |
| Node Role | Operational Role | Usage of a Operational Performer or Operational Architecture in the context of another Operational Performer or Operational Architecture. Creates a whole-part relationship. |
| Operational Activity Edge [Activity Edge] | Operational Control Flow [Control Flow]Operational Object Flow [Object Flow] | Operational Activity Edge is an abstract grouping for Operational Control Flow and Operational Object Flow.Operational Object Flow connects Operational Activity Actions using Pins.Operational Control Flow connects Operational Activity Actions directly. |
| Organizational Project RelationshipProject Ownership | Responsible For | Responsible For relationship is relationship between an Actual Responsible Resource and an Actual Responsibility or Actual Project. It defines the duties that the Actual Responsible Resource is Responsible For. |
| Organization Type | Organization | Organization element denote a group of organizational resources (Persons, Posts, Organizations and Responsibilities) associated for a particular purpose. |
| NodePerformerSecurity Domain | Operational Performer | Operational Performer denote a logical entity that Is Capable To Perform Operational Activities which produce, consume and process resources. |
| Person Type | Post | Post denote a type of job title or position that a person can fill (e.g. Lawyer, Solution Architect, Machine Operator or Chief Executive Officer). |
| Physical Architecture | Resource Architecture | Resource Architecture is an element used to denote a model of the Architecture, described from the System Resource perspective. |
| Project Type | Project | Project is an element that describes types of time-limited endeavours that are required to meet one or more Capability needs. |
| Property | Measurement | Measurement is a property of an element representing something in the physical world, expressed in amounts of a unit of measure. |
| RequestService | Operational PortResource Port | Operational Port is used of a Operational Performer or Operational Architecture in the context of another Operational Performer or Operational Architecture. The whole-part relationship is created.If Request port belongs to System Resource it is migrated to Resource Port.If Request port belongs to Operational Performer it is migrated to Operational Port.Resource Port is an interaction point for a System Resource through which it can interact with the outside environment and which is defined by a Resource Connector. If Service port belongs to System Resource it is migrated to Resource Port.If Service port belongs to Operational Performer it is migrated to Operational Port. |
| Resource Interaction | Resource Exchange |  |
| Resource Interaction Item | Resource Exchange Item | Resource Exchange Item is an abstract grouping for elements that defines the types of elements that can be exchanged between System Resources and conveyed by a Resource Exchange. |
| Resource Interface | Resource Connector | Resource Connector is a channel for exchange between two Resource Roles. |
| Resource State Machine | Resource State Description | A state machine describing the behavior of a System Resource, depicting how the System Resource responds to various events and the actions. |
| Resource Operation | Resource Method |  |
| Role Type | Responsibility | Responsibility element is the type of duty required of a Person or Organization. |
| Service Access | Capability ConfigurationService Specification | Capability Configuration represents a composite structure the physical and human resources (and their interactions) in an enterprise, assembled to meet a Capability.Service Specification defines the specification of a set of functionality provided by one element for the use of others. |
| Service Attribute | Flow Property | Flow Property is a SysML element with already defined direction that is inout. A Flow Property signifies a single flow element that can flow to/from a Block. A flow property’s values are either received from or transmitted to an external Block. Flow properties are defined directly on Blocks or Flow Specifications that are those specifications which type the Flow Ports. Flow properties enable item flows across connectors connecting parts of the corresponding block types, either directly (in case of the property is defined on the Block) or via Flow Ports. |
| Service Channel | Resource ConnectorOperational Connector | If Service Channel connects Ports which owner is System Resource it is be migrated to Resource Connector.If Service Channel connects Ports which owner is Operational Performer it is be migrated to Operational Connector. |
| Service Level Value Set | Provided Service Level | Provided Service Level is a sub type of Actual Service that details a specific service level delivered by the provider. |
| Service State Machine | Service State Description | Service State Description is a state machine describing the behavior of a Service Specification, depicting how the Service Specification responds to various events and the actions. |
| Skill | Competence | Competence element is a specific set of abilities defined by knowledge, skills and aptitude. |
| Skill Of Person Type | Provides Competence | Provides Competence is a dependency relationship that asserts that an Actual Organizational Resource provides a specific set of Competencies. |
| System | Resource Artifact | Resource Artifact element is a type of man-made object that contains no human beings (i.e. satellite, radio, petrol, gasoline, etc.). |
| System Resource | Resource Performer | The Resource Performer covers the Resource Architecture, , Known Resource.Physical Resource |
| Service Operation | Service Method | A behavioral feature of a Service Specification whose behavior is specified in a Service Function. |
| Technical Standard | Standard | Standard is a ratified and peer-reviewed specification that is used to guide or constrain the architecture. A Standard may be applied to any element in the architecture. |
| Trustline | Operational Exchange | Operational Exchange asserts that a flow can exist between Operational Performers (i.e. flows of information, people, materiel, or energy). |
| Vision | Enterprise Vision | An Enterprise Vision describes the future state of the enterprise, without regard to how it is to be achieved. |
| Activity Part Of Project [Dependency]Overlap [Dependency] | Dependency | The Dependency relationship is used instead of stereotyped dependencies such as Activity Part Of Project and Overlap. |
| Association Of Information [Association]Entity Relationship [Association] | Association | The Association relationship is used instead of stereotyped associations such as Association Of Information and Entity Relationship. |
| Asynchronous Message [Signal] | Signal | The Signal element is used instead of stereotyped signal element Asynchronous Message. |
| Condition Property [Property]Entity Attribute [Property] | Property | The Property is used instead of stereotyped properties such as Condition Property and Entity Attribute. |
| Design Rule [Constraint] | Constraint | The Constraint is used instead of stereotyped constraint Design Rule. |
| Geo Political Extent [Instance Specification] | Instance Specification | The Instance Specification is used instead of stereotyped instance specification Geo Political Extent . |
| Logical Data Model [Data Model]Physical Data Model [Data Model] | Data Model | The Data Model is used instead of stereotyped data models such as Logical Data Model and Physical Data Model. |
| Operational Event Trace [Interaction]Resource Event Trace [Interaction]Service Interaction [Interaction] | Interaction | The Interaction is used instead of stereotyped interactions such as Operational Event Trace, Resource Event Trace, and Service Interaction. |
| Operational State [State]Resource State [State] | State | The State is used instead of stereotyped states such as Operational State and Resource State. |
| Project Activity Edge [Activity Edge] | Activity Edge | The Activity Edge is used instead of stereotyped activity edge Project Activity Edge. |
| Service Description [Package] | Package | The Package is used instead of stereotyped package Service Description. |
| Service Level Value [Slot] | Slot | The Slot is used instead of stereotyped slot Service Level Value. |
| Service Message Handler [Reception] | Reception | The Reception is used instead of stereotyped reception Service Message Handler. |
| UPDM Element | UAF Element |  |

The following table describes properties which are renamed in UAF 1.1 profile.

| Element | Property UPDM 2.1 [name] | Property UAF 1.1 [name] |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Viewpoint | languagesmethods | languagemethod |

Interaction [Interaction]</p></td><td colspan="1">Interaction</td><td colspan="1">The Interaction is used instead of stereotyped interactions such as Operational Event Trace, Resource Event Trace, and Service Interaction.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p>Operational State [State]</p><p>Resource State [State]</p></td><td colspan="1">State</td><td colspan="1">The State is used instead of stereotyped states such as Operational State and Resource State.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Project Activity Edge [Activity Edge]</td><td colspan="1">Activity Edge</td><td colspan="1">The Activity Edge is used instead of stereotyped activity edge Project Activity Edge.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Service Description [Package]</td><td colspan="1">Package</td><td colspan="1">The Package is used instead of stereotyped package Service Description.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Service Level Value [Slot]</td><td colspan="1">Slot</td><td colspan="1">The Slot is used instead of stereotyped slot Service Level Value.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Service Message Handler [Reception]</td><td colspan="1"><p>Reception</p></td><td colspan="1">The Reception is used instead of stereotyped reception Service Message Handler.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">UPDM Element</td><td colspan="1">UAF Element</td><td colspan="1"><br /></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p><p>The following table describes properties  which are renamed in UAF 1.1 profile.</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><thead><tr><th colspan="1"><p>Element</p></th><th><p>Property UPDM 2.1 [name]</p></th><th><p>Property UAF 1.1 [name]</p></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td colspan="1">Viewpoint</td><td colspan="1"><p>languages</p><p>methods </p></td><td colspan="1"><p>language</p><p>method</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

**Description**

The **Requirements Domain** (Rq) represent requirements, their properties, and relationships (trace, verify, satisfy, refine) between each other and to UAF architectural elements.

**Implementation**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Requirement Diagram' space='SYSMLP2024xR3'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Requirement Table' space='SYSMLP2024xR3'] .

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227157089 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00953: Resource Assignment Wizard

- page_id: `227157089`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227157089/Resource+Assignment+Wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-22T11:48:01.692+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Dialogs
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Resource Assignment Wizard** is used to define resource for the Version Of Configuration in specified time frame.

To open the **Resource Assignment Wizard**

1. Open a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-8 Systems Evolution Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] (DoDAF), SV-8 (MODAF), NSV-8 (NAF), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Pr Configuration Management' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] (NAF 4.0), or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Evolution diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] (UAF)
2. On the diagram pane, right-click the Version Of Configuration and select Resource . The Resource Assignment Wizard opens.

The wizard consists of three steps:

truenone

##### Resource selection

In this step, you can select the existing or create newresource/ActualResource that will be assigned for Version Of Configuration.

If you select or create new resource, the corresponding Actual Resource will be created automatically.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create a resource for the Version Of Configuration

1. In the Select Resource dialog switch on the Creation Mode .
2. Select package where you want to create a resource.
3. Click the Create button, choose a resource category Security , Resources , or Business , and select the desired resource.
4. In the Specification window of the newly created resource, type its name.

##### Start date specification

In this step, you can set the start date (or select the date from the Timeline) when selected Actual Resource is being used in Version Of Configuration.

The Actual Project Milestone with Kind InService is created. Resource is assigned to the Actual Project Milestone.

For more information on setting the day and time, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Date and Time Settings dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR3']**Date and Time Settings** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### End date specification

In this step, you can set the end date (or select the date from the Timeline) when selected Actual Resource is being used in Version Of Configuration.

The Actual Project Milestone with Kind OutOfService is created. Resource is assigned to the Actual Project Milestone.

For more information on setting the day and time, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Date and Time Settings dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR3']**Date and Time Settings** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>Resource Assignment Wizard</strong> is used to define resource for the Version Of Configuration in specified time frame.</p><p>To open the <strong>Resource Assignment Wizard</strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Open a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-8 Systems Evolution Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-8]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (DoDAF), SV-8 (MODAF), NSV-8 (NAF), <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Pr Configuration Management" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Pr]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (NAF 4.0), or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Resources Evolution diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Resource Evolution]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (UAF)</li><li>On the diagram pane, right-click the Version Of Configuration and select <strong>Resource</strong>.<br />The <strong>Resource Assignment Wizard</strong> opens.</li></ol><p>The wizard consists of three steps:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7906868e-8eaf-4211-b25d-81da0fd7f96b"><ac:parameter ac:name="outline">true</ac:parameter><ac:parameter resource, the corresponding Actual Resource will be created automatically.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_assignment_wizard_step1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Assignment Wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>To create a a resource.</li><li>Click the <strong>Create</strong> button, choose a resource category <strong>Security</strong>, <strong>Resources</strong>, or <strong>Business</strong>, and select the desired resource.</li><li>In the Specification window of the newly created resource, type its name.</li></ol><h3>Start date specification</h3><p>In this step, you can set the start date (or select the date from the Timeline) when selected Actual Resource is being used in Version Of Configuration.</p><p>The Actual Project Milestone with Kind InService is created. Resource is assigned to the Actual Project Milestone.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d535bfaa-fd31-4790-942a-80eecc5bd7ca"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more information on setting the day and time, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Date and Time Settings dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:link-body><strong>Date and Time Settings</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_assignment_wizard_step2.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Assignment Wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3>End date specification</h3><p>In this step, you can set the end date (or select the date from the Timeline) when selected Actual Resource is being used in Version Of Configuration.</p><p>The Actual Project Milestone with Kind OutOfService is created. Resource is assigned to the Actual Project Milestone.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b2cdb224-4e8d-419f-b79d-f0ff69b67ebf"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more information on setting the day and time, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Date and Time Settings dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:link-body><strong>Date and Time Settings</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_assignment_wizard_step3.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Assignment Wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p>

- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227157968/Resource+Exchange
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T11:03:03.779+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

Asserts that a flow can exist between Resource Performers (i.e. flows of data, people, materiel, or energy).

**Architecture** **Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Information Flow

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="OMGBoldBulletKeepNextParagraph"><strong>Description</strong></p><p>Asserts that a flow can exist between Resource Performers (i.e. flows of data, people, materiel, or energy).</p><p><strong>Architecture</strong> <strong>Framework</strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</span></p><p><strong>Extension</strong></p><p>UML Information Flow</p><p><br /></p>
````

## PAGE 00957: Resource Exchange Item

- page_id: `227157969`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227157969/Resource+Exchange+Item
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T11:02:52.955+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

An abstract grouping for elements that defines the types of elements that can be exchanged between Resource Performers and conveyed by a Resource Exchange.

**Architecture** **Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="OMGBoldBulletKeepNextParagraph"><strong>Description</strong></p><p>An abstract grouping for elements that defines the types of elements that can be exchanged between Resource Performers and conveyed by a Resource Exchange.</p><p><strong>Architecture</strong> <strong>Framework</strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</span></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227157970 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00958: Resource Exchange Kind

- page_id: `227157970`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227157970/Resource+Exchange+Kind
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T11:02:41.683+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

Enumeration of the possible kinds of resource exchange applicable to a ResourceExchange. Its enumeration literals are:

- ResourceCommunication - Indicates that the ResourceExchange associated with the ResourceExchangeKind is an implementation of the logical flow of data between Resources.
- ResourceMovement - Indicates that the ResourceExchange associated with the ResourceExchangeKind is an implementation of the logical flow of Resources between Resources.
- ResourceEnergyFlow - Indicates that the ResourceExchange associated with the ResourceExchangeKind is an implementation of the logical flow of natural resources between Resources.
- GeoPoliticalExtentExchange - Indicates that the ResourceExchange associated with the ResourceExchangeKind is an implementation of logical flow where GeoPoliticalExtents (i.e., Borders) flow from one place to another.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="OMGBoldBulletKeepNextParagraph"><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">Enumeration of the possible kinds of resource exchange applicable to a ResourceExchange. Its enumeration literals are:</p><ul><li>ResourceCommunication - Indicates that the<span> </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">ResourceExchange<span> </span></span>associated with the<span> </span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">ResourceExchangeKind<span> </span></span>is an implementation of the logical flow of data between Resources.</li><li>ResourceMovement - Indicates that the<span> </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">ResourceExchange<span> </span></span>associated with the<span> </span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">ResourceExchangeKind<span> </span></span>is an implementation of the logical flow of Resources between Resources.</li><li>ResourceEnergyFlow - Indicates that the<span> </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">ResourceExchange<span> </span></span>associated with the<span> </span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">ResourceExchangeKind<span> </span></span>is an implementation of the logical flow of natural resources between Resources.</li><li>GeoPoliticalExtentExchange - Indicates that the<span> </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">ResourceExchange<span> </span></span>associated with the<span> </span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">ResourceExchangeKind<span> </span></span>is an implementation of logical flow where GeoPoliticalExtents (i.e., Borders) flow from one place to another.</li></ul><p><strong>Architecture Framework</strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227157093 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00959: Resource Exchange Manager dialog

- page_id: `227157093`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227157093/Resource+Exchange+Manager+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-04-25T14:29:47.971+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Dialogs
- labels: ['resource-interaction-manager-dialog']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The**Resource Exchange Manager**dialog enables the analysis and management of the Resource Exchange data. You can:

- Edit existing Resource Exchanges by adding new Resource Exchange Items
- Realize or ignore existing Resource Exchanges on selected relationship
- Change the Resource Exchange Item [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_resource_exchange_item.png']
- Review producing and consuming Function pairs and remove the ones that are not needed
- Review a list of diagrams that display the Realizing Elements of the selected Exchange

To open the **Resource Exchange Manager** dialog

1. Select Resource Connector, Association,Function Control Flow , Function Object Flow, or Resource Message in the diagram pane.
2. On the smart manipulator, click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_exchange_manager.png'] . The Resource Exchange Manager dialog opens.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- The Affected Diagrams button opens the list of diagrams that are affected by the selected Realized Resource Exchange.
- The All Affected Diagrams button opens the list of diagrams that are affected by all the Realized Exchanges in the Resource Exchange Manager dialog.

To learn more about previewing affected diagrams, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Preview Affected Diagrams dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR3'].

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extensions**

Class

## PAGE 00961: Resource Information Role

- page_id: `227157972`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227157972/Resource+Information+Role
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T11:02:22.820+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A usage of Resource Information that exists in the context of a Resource Asset. It also allows the representation of the whole-part aggregation of Resource Information elements.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

Property

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">A usage of Resource Information that exists in the context of a Resource Asset. It also allows the representation of the whole-part aggregation of Resource Information elements.</p><p><strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Architecture Framework</span></strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</span></p><p><strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Extension</span></strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Property</span></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227157973 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00962: Resource Interface

- page_id: `227157973`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227157973/Resource+Interface
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T11:02:13.952+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A declaration that specifies a contract between the Resource Performers it is related to and any other Resource Performers it can interact with. It is also intended to be an implementation of a specification of an Interface in the Business and/or Service layer.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Class

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="OMGBoldBulletKeepNextParagraph"><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">A declaration that specifies a contract between the Resource Performers it is related to and any other Resource Performers it can interact with. It is also intended to be an implementation of a specification of an Interface in the Business and/or Service layer. </p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Architecture Framework</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</span></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Extension</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">UML Class</p><p><br /></p>
````

## PAGE 00965: Resource Mitigation

- page_id: `227157976`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227157976/Resource+Mitigation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T11:01:31.838+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A set of security measures intended to address specific cyber risks. Comprises a subset of Tailored Security Controls that are used to protect the asset at resource (Resource Role).

**Architecture** **Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Class

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<p class="OMGBoldBulletKeepNextParagraph"><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">A set of security measures intended to address specific cyber risks. Comprises a subset of Tailored Security Controls that are used to protect the asset at resource (Resource Role).</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Architecture</strong> <strong>Framework</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</span></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Extension</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">UML Class</p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227157980 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00969: Resource Role

- page_id: `227157980`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227157980/Resource+Role
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T11:00:28.601+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

728086762

728086766

728086764

**Description**

Usage of a Resource Performer in the context of another Resource Performer. Creates a whole-part relationship.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Property

728086761

###### **Resource Role Kind mapping table by OMG specification**

For each Resource Role created under the Resource Performer (which is a context), a type (which is Resource Performer) should be assigned. Depending on a type, an appropriate Role Kind is assigned for each Resource Role according to the rules provided in the following table.

| Role Kind | Type | Context |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Artifact Component | Resource Artifact | Resource Performer |
| Component | Software | Resource Performer |
| Equipment | Physical Resource | Resource Performer |
| Hosted Software | Software | Resource Performer |
| Human Resource | Organizational Resource | Resource Performer |
| Natural Resource Component | Natural Resource | Resource Performer |
| Other | Resource Performer | Resource Performer |
| Part | Resource Performer | Resource Performer |
| Platform | Resource Performer | Resource Performer |
| Post Role | Post | Resource Performer |
| Responsibility Role | Responsibility | Resource Performer |
| System | Resource Performer | Resource Performer |
| Sub Organization | Organization | Resource Performer |
| Sub System Part | Resource Performers | Resource Performer |
| Used Configuration | Capability Configuration | Resource Performer |
| Used Physical Architecture | Resource Architecture | Resource Performer |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Creates a whole-part relationship.</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Architecture Framework</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</span></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Extension</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">UML Property</p><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d8b5a443-4b88-4085-8ce0-d4dad2adb50a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">728086761</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h4><strong>Resource Role Kind mapping table by OMG specification</strong></h4><p>For each Resource Role created under the Resource Performer (which is a context), a type (which is Resource Performer) should be assigned. ### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A services that a Resource Performer provides to support higher level Services or Operational Activities. Employee provisioning, backup and recovery, storage, self-service help desk are examples of Resource Services.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extensions**

Class

## PAGE 00972: Resource Signal

- page_id: `227157983`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227157983/Resource+Signal
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T11:00:03.906+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A ResourceSignal is a specification of a kind of communication between resources (Resource Performers) in which a reaction is asynchronously triggered in the receiver without a reply.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

Signal

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Description </strong></p><p>A ResourceSignal is a specification of a kind of communication between resources (Resource Performers) in which a reaction is asynchronously triggered in the receiver without a reply.</p><p><strong>Architecture Framework</strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</span></p><p><strong>Extension</strong></p><p>Signal</p>
````

The Resources (Rs) domain defines the solution architectures to implement operational requirements. It captures a solution architecture consisting of resources, e.g. organizational, software, artifacts, capability configurations, natural resources.

## PAGE 00975: Resources Connectivity

- page_id: `227156660`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227156660/Resources+Connectivity
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:55:51.804+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Resources
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

914248742

914248744

914248743

**Description**

The Resources Connectivity (Rs-Cn) table summarizes the exchanges between resources of information, systems, personnel, natural resources, etc., and the functions that produce and consume them. Measurements can optionally be included.

The Resources Connectivity Internal diagram addresses the internal composition and interaction of systems.

The intended usage of the The Resources Connectivity (Rs-Cn) view includes:

- Summarizing system resource exchanges.
- Interface management.
- Comparing interoperability characteristics of solution options.
- Detailed definition of resource flows.

**Implementation**

The Resources Connectivity (Rs-Cn) is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Internal Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Connectivity matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] . It represents exchanges between resources.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Role-based Connectivity Table' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] . It represents exchanges between Resource Roles (resource usages).
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Connectivity Table' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- Reports (.xlsx): - Resources Connectivity Table Excel Spreadsheet. - Resources Role-based Connectivity Table Excel Spreadsheet.

**Sample**

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

It represents exchanges between resources.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Resources Role-based Connectivity Table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Resources Role-based Connectivity table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Resources Connectivity Table.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li>Reports (.xlsx):<br />- Resources Connectivity Table Excel Spreadsheet.<br />- Resources Role-based Connectivity Table Excel Spreadsheet.</li></ul><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="resources_connectivity.png"><ri:page <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227156681 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00976: Resources Constraints

- page_id: `227156681`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227156681/Resources+Constraints
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:02:13.974+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Resources
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

914505518

914505520

914505519

**Description**

The Resources Constraints (Rs-Ct) domain defines limitations, constraints, and performance parameters for resources, their interactions, performed functions, and data. It specifies traditional textual rules/non-functional requirements. The addition of SysML parametrics provide a computational means of defining resource constraints within a specific context.

The intended usage of the Resources Constraints (Rs-Ct) view includes:

- Definition of implementation logic.
- Identification of resource constraints.

**Implementation**

The Resources Constraints (Rs-Ct) view is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Constraints table' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Constraints Definition diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] .
- Resources Constraints Excel Spreadsheet (.xlsx) report.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Resources Constraints table

**Constraint owner**

You can choose where all the constraints will be stored through the **Project Options**.

To select a constraint owner

1. In the main menu, click Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. Go to General > UAF , and in the Default Constraint Owners property group, choose a needed owner.

914505517

**Related elements**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227157097 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00977: Resources Forecast Creation Wizard

- page_id: `227157097`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227157097/Resources+Forecast+Creation+Wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-20T13:31:36.143+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Dialogs
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1622433108

1622433119

1622433109

The **Resources Forecast Creation Wizard** is used to create forecast tables.

The same wizard is used for the following tables:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating the SV-9 table' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SvcV-9 table' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating StdV-2 table' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating NSV-9 table' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating NTV-2 table' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating the SV-9 table' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating TV-2 table' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating P1 Technology Forecast table' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating A8 Standards Forecast table' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Standards Roadmap table' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Forecast table' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Personnel Forecast diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

To open the **Resources Forecast Creation Wizard**

- Do one of the following:
  - Press Ctrl+N.
  - From the main menu, select Diagrams > Create Diagram .
  - Right-click the <view name> Package and select Create Diagram > <diagram name> .
  - In the main toolbar, click Create Diagram and in the opened window, search for the needed diagram. The Resources Forecast Creation Wizard opens.

The wizard consists of three steps:

truenone

##### Diagram name and owning package specification

In this step, you can select the existing or create a new owner for the created diagram. You can also type the diagram name.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Resources selection

In this step, choose the needed resources or create new resources. The selected resources will be represented as Resources Forecast table rows.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The resources (Capability Configuration, Competence, Known Resource, Natural Resource, Organization, Person, Post, Project, Protocol, protocol Stack, Resource Architecture, Resource Artifact, Resource Mitigation, Responsibility, Security Enclave, Software, Standard, System, Technology) are offered depending on the domain.

To create a resource

1. In the Select Resources step, click the Creation Mode .
2. Select the package where you want to create a resource.
3. Click the Create button.
4. From the list, select the resource type.
5. In the Specification window of the newly created resource, type its name.

##### Actual Strategic Phases selection

In this step, you can select existing or create new Actual Strategic Phases which will be represented as Resources Forecast table columns.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create an Actual Strategic Phase

1. In the Select Actual Strategic Phase step, click the Creation Mode .
2. Select the package where you want to create a resource.
3. Click the Create button.
4. In the Specification window of the newly created Actual Strategic Phase, type its name.

1622433106

**Related diagrams**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating the SV-9 table' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SvcV-9 table' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating StdV-2 table' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

Forecast Creation Wizard</strong></p><hr /><ul><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>Press Ctrl+N.</li><li>From the main menu, select <strong>Diagrams</strong> &gt; <strong>Create Diagram</strong>.</li><li>Right-click the <strong>&lt;view name&gt;</strong> Package and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>&lt;diagram name&gt;</strong>.</li><li>In the main toolbar, click <strong>Create Diagram</strong> and in the opened window, search for the needed diagram.<br />The <strong>Resources Forecast Creation </strong><strong>Wizard</strong> opens.</li></ul></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>The wizard consists of three steps:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7906868e-8eaf-4211-b25d-81da0fd7f96b"><ac:parameter ac:name="outline">true</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="style">none</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h3>Diagram name and owning package specification</h3><p>In this step, you can select the existing or create a new owner for the created diagram. You can also type the diagram name.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_forecast_creation_wizard_1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Resources Forecast Creation Wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3>Resources selection</h3><p>In this step, choose the needed resources or create new resources. The selected resources will be represented as Resources Forecast table rows.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_forecast_creation_wizard_2.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Resources Forecast Creation Wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f8550f47-2413-4909-b52c-3689c4a71ffe"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The resources  (Capability Configuration, Competence, Known Resource, Natural Resource, Organization, Person, Post, Project, Protocol, protocol Stack, Resource Architecture, Resource Artifact, Resource Mitigation, Responsibility, Security Enclave, Software, Standard, System, Technology) are offered depending on the domain.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create a resource</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <strong>Select Resources</strong> step, click the <strong>Creation Mode</strong>.</li><li>Select the package where you want to create a resource.</li><li>Click the <strong>Create</strong> button.</li><li>From the list, select the resource type.</li><li>In the Specification window of the newly created resource, type its name.</li></ol><h3>Actual Strategic Phases selection</h3><p>In this step, you can select existing or create new Actual Strategic Phases which will be represented as Resources Forecast table columns.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_forecast_creation_wizard_3.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Resources Forecast Creation Wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>To create an Actual Strategic Phase</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <strong>Select Actual Strategic Phase </strong>step, click the <strong>Creation Mode</strong>.</li><li>Select the package where you want to create a resource.</li><li>Click the <strong>Create</strong> button.</li><li>In the Specification window of the newly created Actual Strategic Phase, type its name.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f5104d22-a3a4-4e2f-8aff-3bfb42843e2a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1622433106</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related diagrams</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating the SV-9 table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-9]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating SvcV-9 table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SvcV-9]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating StdV-2 table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[StdV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating NSV-9 table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[NSV-9]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating NTV-2 table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[NTV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating the SV-9 table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[MODAF. SV-9]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating TV-2 table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[MODAF. TV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating P1 Technology Forecast table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P1 Technology Forecast]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating A8 Standards Forecast table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[A8 Standards Forecast]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Standards Roadmap" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Resources Forecast table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Resources Forecast]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Personnel Forecast diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Personnel Forecast]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227156688 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00978: Resources Information

- page_id: `227156688`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227156688/Resources+Information
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:01:10.387+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Resources
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

915064863

915064865

915064864

**Description**

The Resources Information (Rs-If) domain shows the information perspective on resource architecture. It allows analysis of an architecture’s information and data definition aspect, without consideration of implementation-specific issues.

**Cameo Data Modeler plugin integration**

You can use the Entity Relationship diagram for logical and physical data modeling in the data model diagrams. It allows for using the information engineering notation within this view.

**Implementation**

The Resources Information (Rs-If) view is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Information diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] . It allows analysis of an architecture’s data definition aspect, without consideration of implementation-specific or product-specific issues. Another purpose is to provide a common dictionary of data definitions to consistently express models wherever logical-level resource information elements are included in the descriptions
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Information diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] . It defines the structure of the various kinds of system or service data that are utilized by the systems or services in the Architectural Description. A Physical Data Model diagram is used to describe how the information represented in the Logical Data Model diagram is actually implemented. While the mapping between the logical and physical data models is relatively straightforward, the relationship between the components of each model (e.g., entity types in the logical model versus relational tables in the physical model) is frequently one-to-many or many-to-many.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Entity Relationship Diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] . It supports the information engineering notation.

**Samples**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Logical Data Model

915064862

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Information' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Information Model' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Information Model Kind' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Entity Relationship Diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

Another purpose is to provide a common dictionary of data definitions to consistently express models wherever logical-level resource information elements are included in the descriptions</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Resources Information diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Physical Data Model]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. It defines the structure of the various kinds of system or service data that are utilized by the systems or services in the Architectural Description. A Physical Data Model diagram is used to describe how the information represented in the Logical Data Model diagram is actually implemented. While the mapping between the logical and physical data models is relatively straightforward, the relationship between the components of each model (e.g., entity types in the logical model versus relational tables in the physical model) is frequently one-to-many or many-to-many.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating Entity Relationship Diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Entity Relationship Diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227156764 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00979: Resources Motivation

- page_id: `227156764`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227156764/Resources+Motivation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-01-31T11:34:46.132+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Resources
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The **Resources Motivation** (Rq-Mv) view provides a central reference for a set of stakeholder needs expressed as requirements, their relationship (via traceability) to more detailed requirements, and the solution described by the architecture that will meet those requirements. It is used to represent requirements, their properties, and relationships (trace, verify, satisfy, refine) both to each other and to UAF architectural elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The <strong>Resources Motivation</strong> (Rq-Mv) view provides a central reference for a set of stakeholder needs expressed as requirements, their relationship (via traceability) to more detailed requirements, and the solution described by the architecture that will meet those requirements. It is used to represent requirements, their properties, and relationships (trace, verify, satisfy, refine) both to each other and to UAF architectural elements.</p>
````

**Description**

The Resources Parameters (Rs-Pm) domain depicts the measurements of resources.

The intended usage of the Resources Parameters (Rs-Pm) domain includes:

- Definition of performance characteristics and measurements.
- Identification of non-functional requirements.

**Implementation**

The Resources Parameters (Rs-Pm) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Environment diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Typical Measurements table' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with hierarchical Typical Measurements table' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Actual Measurements table' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with hierarchical Actual Measurements table' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Parametric diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- Actual Measurements (.xlsx).

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227156696 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00981: Resources Processes

- page_id: `227156696`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227156696/Resources+Processes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:48:37.460+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Resources
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

915545085

915545088

915545087

**Description**

The Resources Processes (Rs-Pr) domain describes the functions that are normally conducted in the course of implementing operational activities in support of capabilities. It describes the functions, their inputs/outputs, function actions and flows between them.

The Resources Processes (Rs-Pr) domain addresses human and system functionality.

The primary purposes of The Resources Processes (Rs-Pr) domain are to:

- Develop a clear description of the necessary data flows that are input (consumed) by and output (produced) by each resource.
- Ensure that the functional connectivity is complete (i.e., that a resource’s required inputs are all satisfied).
- Ensure that the functional decomposition reaches an appropriate level of detail.

The intended usage of the The Resources Processes (Rs-Pr) domain includes:

- Description of task workflow.
- Identification of functional system requirements.
- Functional decomposition of systems.
- Relate human and system functions.

**Implementation**

The Resources Processes (Rs-Pr) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Processes diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Processes Flow diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] .
- Resources Processes Flow (BPD) diagram. It is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD) and describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the business work. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed business activities.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Implementation Matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] .

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a979f6d7-af01-48b6-9f7e-c715dbded8fa"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">915545085</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="70e49422-fca3-4ed3-b7f3-f2f7f93799e1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">915545088</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="02932502-4b06-482e-90ec-e7a36c2a7695"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">915545087</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The Resources Processes (Rs-Pr) domain describes the functions that are normally conducted in the course of implementing operational activities in support of capabilities. It describes the functions, their inputs/outputs, function actions and flows between them.</p><p>The Resources Processes (Rs-Pr) domain addresses human and system functionality.</p><p>The primary purposes of The Resources Processes (Rs-Pr) domain are to:</p><ul><li>Develop a clear description of the necessary data flows that are input (consumed) by and output (produced) by each resource.</li><li>Ensure that the functional connectivity is complete (i.e., that a resource’s required inputs are all satisfied).</li><li>Ensure that the functional decomposition reaches an appropriate level of detail.</li></ul><p>The intended usage of the The Resources Processes (Rs-Pr) domain includes:</p><ul><li>Description of task workflow.</li><li>Identification of functional system requirements.</li><li>Functional decomposition of systems.</li><li>Relate human and system functions.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The Resources Processes (Rs-Pr) domain is represented by:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Resources Processes diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Resources Processes diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Resources Processes Flow diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Resources Processes Flow diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Resources Processes Flow (BPD) diagram. It is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD) and describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">business </span>work. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">business </span>activities.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Implementation Matrix" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link>.</li></ul><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image ac:title="Resource Processes" ac:alt="Resource Processes"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_processes.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Resources Processes" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>An example of the process diagram</h6></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_process_flow.png"><ri:page ## PAGE 00982: Resources Roadmap

- page_id: `227156707`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227156707/Resources+Roadmap
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:14:51.688+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Resources
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

917589031

917589033

917589032

**Description**

The **Resources Roadmap** (Rs-Rm) domain shows the resource structure changes over time. It shows the structure of several resources mapped against a timeline.

**Implementation**

The Resources Roadmap (Rs-Rm) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Evolution diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3']. It shows the resource structure changes over time. The intended usage of the Resources Evolution diagram includes:
  - Development of incremental acquisition strategy.
  - Planning technology insertion.

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Forecast table' space='UAF12P2024xR3']. It shows the technology forecast. This diagram defines the underlying current and expected supporting technologies. Expected supporting technologies are those that can be reasonably forecast given the current state of technology, and expected improvements / trends. It provides a summary of emerging technologies and skills that impact the architecture. The intended usage of the Resources Forecast table includes:
  - Forecasting technology readiness against time.
  - HR Trends Analysis.
  - Recruitment Planning.
  - Planning technology insertion.
  - Input to options analysis.
- Resources Evolution report (.docx).

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the resources forecast

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the resources evolution

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227156715 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00983: Resources Sequences

- page_id: `227156715`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227156715/Resources+Sequences
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:01:54.133+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Resources
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

915260432

915260434

915260433

**Description**

The Resources Sequences (Rs-Sq) domain expresses the time-ordered examination of the interactions between resources (roles). Each event-trace diagram should have an accompanying description that defines the particular scenario or situation.

The Resources Sequences (Rs-Sq) domain is valuable for moving to the next level of detail from the initial solution design, to help define a sequence of functions and system data interfaces, and to ensure that each participating resource or resource role has the necessary information it needs, at the right time, to perform its assigned functionality.

The intended usage of The Resources Sequences (Rs-Sq) view includes:

- Analysis of resource events impacting operation.
- Behavioral analysis.
- Identification of non-functional system requirements.

**Implementation**

The Resources Sequences (Rs-Sq) view is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Sequences diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] that is based on interaction.
- Resources Sequences (BPD) diagram. It is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD). It describes an organization's sequence or flow of activities that show how the business works. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed business activities.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

915260431

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

## PAGE 00984: Resources States

- page_id: `227156721`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227156721/Resources+States
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-02T10:30:50.619+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Resources
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

917825059

917825061

917825060

**Description**

The Resources States (Rs-St) domain shows the state-based behavior of a resource. It represents how the resource responds to various events and actions by changing the state. Each transition specifies an event and an action.

The intended usage of the The Resources States (Rs-St) domain includes:

- Definition of states, events and state transitions (behavioral modeling).
- Identification of constraints.

**Implementation**

The Resources States (Rs-St) domain is represented by a Resource States diagram that is based on UML State Machine diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Resources States diagram

917825058

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Performer' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

**Related diagrams**

ac:name="id">917825060</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The Resources States (Rs-St) domain shows the state-based behavior of a resource. It represents how the resource responds to various events and actions by changing the state. Each transition specifies an event and an action.</p><p>The intended usage of the The Resources States (Rs-St) domain includes:</p><ul><li>Definition of states, events and state transitions (behavioral modeling).</li><li>Identification of constraints.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The Resources States (Rs-St) domain is represented by a Resource States diagram that is based on UML State Machine diagram.</p><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image ac:title="Resource States" ac:alt="Resource States"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_states.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Resources States" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>An example of the Resources States diagram</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="21676753-91f6-477f-828b-2421bf7f3a95"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">917825058</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Performer" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource State Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Allocate Activities to State Internal Behaviors" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related diagrams</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" ri:content-title="State Machine diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

## PAGE 00985: Resources Structure

- page_id: `227156723`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227156723/Resources+Structure
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:05:07.392+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Resources
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

917923917

917923919

917923918

**Description**

The Resources Structure (Rs-Sr) domain references the resource structure, connectors and interfaces in a specific context. It defines the physical resources, e.g. capability configuration(s)/system(s) and interactions necessary to implement a specific set of Operational Performer(s). Can be used to represent communications networks and pathways that link communications resources and provides details regarding their configuration.

**Implementation**

The Resources Structure (Rs-Sr) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Implementation Matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] .

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227156741 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00986: Resources Taxonomy

- page_id: `227156741`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227156741/Resources+Taxonomy
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:22:47.734+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Resources
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

918156776

918156778

918156777

**Description**

The Resources Taxonomy (Rs-Tx) domain shows the taxonomy of types of resources and the flows of resources among them.

The intended usage of the Resources Taxonomy (Rs-Tx) domain includes:

- Definition of system concepts.
- Definition of system options.
- System resource flow requirements capture.
- Capability integration planning.
- System integration management.
- Operational planning (capability and performer definition).

The Resources Taxonomy (Rs-Tx) domain is used in two complementary ways:

- Describe the resource flows exchanged between resources in the architecture.
- Describe a solution, or solution option, in terms of the components of capability and their physical integration on platforms and other facilities.

**Implementation**

The Resources Taxonomy (Rs-Tx) domain in represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Taxonomy diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] . It addresses the composition and interaction of systems.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Implementation Matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Taxonomy table' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] . This table can be used to create and describe major domain elements faster.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Resources Taxonomy diagram

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Resources Taxonomy table

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227156756 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00987: Resources Traceability

- page_id: `227156756`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227156756/Resources+Traceability
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:51:46.664+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Resources
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

918383161

918383164

918383163

**Description**

The Resources Traceability (Rs-Tr) domain shows the traceability between operational activities and functions that implements them. It depicts the mapping of functions to operational activities and thus identifies the transformation of an operational need into a purposeful function performed by a resource or solution.

The intended usage of the Resources Traceability (Rs-Tr) domain includes:

- Tracing functional system requirements to user requirements.
- Tracing solution options to requirements.
- Identification of overlaps or gaps.

**Implementation**

The Resources Traceability (Rs-Tr) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Functions to Operational Activities Mapping Matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources to Operational Activities Mapping matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources to Capabilities Mapping matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] .

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Functions to Operational Activities Mapping Matrix

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Resources to Operational Activities Mapping matrix

918383160

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Function' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Activity' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Function' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

**Related procedures**
