<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227157774 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00988: ResourcesBToSSync 1

- page_id: `227157774`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227157774/ResourcesBToSSync+1
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-04-26T07:57:41.487+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Validation rules > Correctness validation rules
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Abbreviation**

ResourcesBToSSync[1]

**Description**

No compatible Resource Interface. Thisvalidation rule checks whether Resource Ports and Resource Interfaces are compatible with flows between the Resource Roles. The missing Ports and Interfaces can be handled automatically using solvers.

**Severity**

warning****

**Constrained Element**

Diagram****

**Solvers**

- Create Resource Port - creates a new Resource Port typed by a compatible Resource Interface.
- Choose Compatible Resource Interface - changes an existing Resource Port type to a compatible Resource Interface.
- Add Missing Flow Property - adds a new Flow Property to a Resource Interface that types a Resource Port.
- Reverse Direction of ResourcePort - changes the Resource Port direction, thus changing the Flow Property direction of a Resource Interface that types a Resource Port.

**Example**

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

###### The Resource Port*iTriage*is created using Behavior to Structure Synchronization solvers.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Abbreviation</strong></p><p>ResourcesBToSSync[1]</p><p><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7216feb7-efde-4071-86ed-30df4d49b0fe">Description</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="63e12ed2-ff4d-407b-9819-9808bb8dd540">No compatible Resource Interface</ac:inline-comment-marker>. This </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">validation rule checks whether Resource Ports and Resource Interfaces are compatible with flows between the Resource Roles. The missing Ports and Interfaces can be handled automatically using solvers.</span></p><p><strong>Severity</strong></p><p>warning<strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>Constrained Element</strong></p><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="d88639d5-40f1-443d-8985-e8e9b51ab799">Diagram</ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>Solvers</strong></p><ul><li><strong>Create Resource Port </strong>- creates a new Resource Port typed by a compatible Resource Interface.</li><li><strong>Choose Compatible Resource Interface </strong>- changes an existing Resource Port type to a compatible Resource Interface.</li><li><strong>Add Missing Flow Property </strong>- adds a new Flow Property to a Resource Interface that types a Resource Port.</li><li><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Reverse Direction <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="70f0e072-f1d9-4655-a463-9a4400f792a0">of <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Resource </span>Port</ac:inline-comment-marker> </strong>- changes the Resource Port direction, thus changing the Flow Property direction of a Resource Interface that types a Resource Port.</li></ul><p><strong>Example</strong></p><p><strong><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="UAF_behavior_to_structure_resources_ports_and_interfaces_validation_rule_process.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="ResourcesBToSSync 1" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(128,128,128);">The Resource Port<span> </span></span><em style="text-align: center;">iTriage</em><span style="color: rgb(128,128,128);"><span> </span>is created</span> using Behavior to Structure Synchronization solvers.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227157776 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00989: ResourcesBToSSync 2

- page_id: `227157776`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227157776/ResourcesBToSSync+2
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-04-26T07:58:36.053+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Validation rules > Correctness validation rules
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Abbreviation**

ResourcesBToSSync[2]

**Description**

No compatible Resource Connector.The second validation rule checks whether a Resources Connector exists between the two compatible Resources Ports owned by the Resource Roles. The missing Resources Connectors can be handled automatically using solvers.

**Severity**

warning****

**Constrained Element**

Diagram****

**Solvers**

**Create ResourcesConnector** - creates a missing ResourcesConnector between the two compatible ResourcesPorts.

**Example**

###### **[IMAGE alt='' src='']**Creating a Resources Connector that is missing in the Resources Internal Connectivity Diagram.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The missing Resources Connectors can be handled automatically using solvers.</span></p><p><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Severity</strong></p><p>warning<strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>Constrained Element</strong></p><p>Diagram<strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>Solvers</strong></p><p><strong>Create <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Resources </span>Connector</strong> - creates a missing <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Resources </span>Connector between the two compatible <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Resources </span>Ports. </p><p><strong>Example</strong></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="UAF_behavior_to_structure_resources_connectors_validation_rule_process.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="ResourcesBToSSync 2" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></strong><span style="color: rgb(128,128,128);">Creating a Resources Connector that is missing in the Resources Internal Connectivity Diagram.</span></h6>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227157778 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00990: ResourcesBToSSync 3

- page_id: `227157778`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227157778/ResourcesBToSSync+3
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-15T15:46:01.942+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Validation rules > Correctness validation rules
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Abbreviation**

ResourcesBToSSync[3]

**Description**

No compatible Resource Exchange realization. The third validation rule checks realized Resources Exchanges in Activities and help to ensure the correct Resources Exchanges are realized on Resources Connectors that connect Resources Ports typed by compatible Resources Interface.Themissing Exchanges can be handled automatically using solvers.

**Severity**

warning****

**Constrained Element**

Diagram****

**Solvers**

- Realize Missing Resource Exchange - r ealizes existing compatible Resource Exchange found in the behavior model on the Resources Connector .
- Open Resource Exchange Manager - opens the Resource Exchange Manager dialog.
- Change Exchange Realization Compatibility Checking - opens the Project Options dialog to turn on/turn off the Exchange realization checking.

**Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Realizing a missing Resource Exchange on the Connector in the Resources Internal Connectivity Diagram.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Abbreviation</strong></p><p>ResourcesBToSSync[3]</p><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">No compatible Resource Exchange realization. The third validation rule checks realized Resources Exchanges in Activities and help to ensure the correct Resources Exchanges are realized on Resources Connectors that connect Resources Ports typed by compatible Resources Interface.<span> </span></span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span> </span>missing Exchanges can be handled automatically using solvers.</span></p><p><strong>Severity</strong></p><p>warning<strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>Constrained Element</strong></p><p>Diagram<strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>Solvers</strong></p><ul><li><strong>Realize Missing Resource Exchange</strong> - <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">r</span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">ealizes existing compatible Resource Exchange found in the behavior model on the Resources Connector</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. </span></li><li><strong>Open <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="51a81ba7-c982-4882-ba93-f54dfb5d2fc8">Resource Exchange Manager</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong> - opens the <strong>Resource Exchange Manager</strong> dialog.</li><li><strong>Change Exchange Realization Compatibility Checking</strong> - opens the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog to turn on/turn off the Exchange realization checking. </li></ul><p><strong>Example</strong></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="UAF_behavior_to_structure_resources_connectors_validation_rule_process (1).png"><ri:page ri:content-title="ResourcesBToSSync 3" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="4d386544-5508-408a-99c2-114155023b62">Realizing a missing Resource Exchange on the Connector in the Resources Internal Connectivity Diagram.</ac:inline-comment-marker></h6>
````

## PAGE 00992: Responsible For

- page_id: `227157986`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227157986/Responsible+For
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T10:59:17.718+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

An abstraction relationship between an Actual Responsible Resource and an Actual Responsibility or Actual Project. It defines the duties that the Actual Responsible Resource is Responsible For.

**Architecture** **Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

Abstraction

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">An abstraction relationship between an Actual Responsible Resource and an Actual Responsibility or Actual Project. It defines the duties that the Actual Responsible Resource is Responsible For.</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Architecture</strong> <strong>Framework</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</span></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Extension</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">Abstraction</p><p><br /></p>
````

## PAGE 00993: Responsible Role Kind

- page_id: `227157987`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`

- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

Enumeration of the possible kinds of ResponsibleRole. Its enumeration literals are:

- Manager - Indicates that the ResourceExchange associated with the ResourceExchangeKind is an implementation of logical flow.
- ResponsibleOwner - Indicates that the ResourceExchange associated with the ResourceExchangeKind is an implementation of logical flow.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227157351 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00994: Reusing Measurement Sets for Actual Property Set creation

- page_id: `227157351`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227157351/Reusing+Measurement+Sets+for+Actual+Property+Set+creation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-04-09T10:34:24.332+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures > Measurements
- labels: ['reuse-measurement-sets-for-actual-objects']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When creating a <viewpoint name>* Role, you can quickly reuse the measurements of their type (Asset). For your convenience, the information about the recommended Actual Property Sets is displayed in the Specification window of the element for which you are creating the Actual Property Set.

You can conveniently reuse the values of Measurements or Measurement Sets when creating the following Actual Property Set:

- <viewpoint name>* Role

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The type measurements in the Actual Measurements compartment are displayed *Italic*.

*** <viewpoint name>**- Operational, Resources, Services, Strategic.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>When creating a &lt;viewpoint name&gt;* Role, you can quickly reuse the measurements of their type (Asset). For your convenience, the information about the recommended Actual Property Sets is displayed in the Specification window of the element for which you are creating the Actual Property Set.</p><p>You can conveniently reuse the values of Measurements or Measurement Sets when creating the following Actual Property Set:</p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(124,127,147);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">&lt;viewpoint name&gt;*</span> </span>Role</li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Reusing measurements structure owned by roles type.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Reusing Measurement Sets for Actual Property Set creation" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="83d4c9a2-d883-498c-b5ad-54430acd2301"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The type measurements in the Actual Measurements compartment are displayed <em>Italic</em>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><hr /><p><strong>* &lt;viewpoint name&gt;</strong><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><span> </span>- Operational, Resources, Services, Strategic.</span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227157988 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00995: Risk

- page_id: `227157988`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227157988/Risk
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T10:58:54.926+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A statement of the impact of an event on Assets. It represents a constraint on an Asset in terms of adverse effects, with an associated measure. The measure is used to capture the extent to which an entity is threatened by a potential circumstance or event. Risk is typically a function of:

- the adverse impacts that would arise if the circumstance or event occurs;
- the likelihood of occurrence. Software related security risks are those risks that arise from the loss of confidentiality, integrity, or availability of information or information systems.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>A statement of the impact of an event on Assets. It represents a constraint on an Asset in terms of adverse effects, with an associated measure. The measure is used to capture the extent to which an entity is threatened by a potential circumstance or event. Risk is typically a function of:</p><ul><li>the adverse impacts that would arise if the circumstance or event occurs;</li><li>the likelihood of occurrence. Software related security risks are those risks that arise from the loss of confidentiality, integrity, or availability of information or information systems.</li></ul><p><strong>Architecture Framework</strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227157989 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00996: Role Kind

- page_id: `227157989`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227157989/Role+Kind
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T10:58:11.561+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

Enumeration of the possible kinds of roles that a ResourceRole may play in the context of a ResourcePerformer. Its enumeration literals are:

- Part - Indicates that the ResourceRole associated with the ResourceRoleKind is a kind of a ResourcePerformer that is used as a part of another ResourcePerformer.
- Component - Indicates that the ResourceRole associated with the ResourceRoleKind is a kind of Software that is used in the context of a ResourcePerformer.
- UsedConfiguration - Indicates that the ResourceRole associated with the ResourceRoleKind is a kind of existing CapabilityConfiguration that is used in the context of a ResourcePerformer.
- HumanResource - Indicates that the ResourceRole associated with the ResourceRoleKind is a kind of human resource that is used in the context of a ResourcePerformer.
- Platform - Indicates that the ResourceRole associated with the ResourceRoleKind is a kind of a ResourcePerformer that represents a platform (e.g. vessel, aircraft, etc.) that is used in the context of a SystemsResource.
- System - Indicates that the ResourceRole associated with the ResourceRoleKind is a kind of assembly of ResourcePerformers that is used in the context of another ResourcePerformer.
- SubOrganization - Indicates that the ResourceRole associated with the ResourceRoleKind is a kind of Organization that is typically the parent of another - e.g. a squadron may be part of a batallion, that is used in the context of a ResourcePerformer.
- PostRole - Indicates that the ResourceRole associated with the ResourceRoleKind is a kind of Post that is used in the context of a ResourcePerformer.
- ResponsibilityRole - Indicates that the ResourceRole associated with the ResourceRoleKind is a kind of Responsibility associated with a role that is used in the context of a ResourcePerformer.
- Equipment - Indicates that the ResourceRole associated with the ResourceRoleKind is a kind of man made resource that is used to accomplish a task or function in the context of a ResourcePerformer.
- SubSystemPart - Indicates that the ResourceRole associated with the ResourceRoleKind is a kind of subsystem (represented as a ResourcePerformers) is is part of another ResourcePerformer.
- UsedPhysicalArchitecture - Indicates that the ResourceRole associated with the ResourceRoleKind is a kind of existing PhysicalArchitecture that is used in the context of a ResourcePerformer.
- HostedSoftware - Indicates that the ResourceRole associated with the ResourceRoleKind is a kind of software that is used in the context of a ResourcePerformer.
- ArtifactComponent - Indicates that the ResourceRole associated with the ResourceRoleKind is a kind of non human resource that is used as a component in the context of a ResourcePerformer.
- NaturalResourceComponent - Indicates that the ResourceRole associated with the ResourceRoleKind is a kind of natural resource that is used as a component in the context of a ResourcePerformer.
- Other - Indicates that the ResourceRole associated with the ResourceRoleKind is another kind of RoleKind that is not on the enumerated list.

**Architecture** **Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="OMGBoldBulletKeepNextParagraph"><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">Enumeration of the possible kinds of roles that a ResourceRole may play in the context of a ResourcePerformer. Its enumeration literals are:</p><ul><li>Part - Indicates that the ResourceRole associated with the ResourceRoleKind is a kind of a ResourcePerformer that is used as a part of another ResourcePerformer.</li><li>Component - Indicates that the ResourceRole associated with the ResourceRoleKind is a kind of Software that is used in the context of a ResourcePerformer.</li><li>UsedConfiguration - Indicates that the ResourceRole associated with the ResourceRoleKind is a kind of existing CapabilityConfiguration that is used in the context of a ResourcePerformer.</li><li>HumanResource - Indicates that the ResourceRole associated with the ResourceRoleKind is a kind of human resource that is used in the context of a ResourcePerformer.</li><li>Platform - Indicates that the ResourceRole associated with the ResourceRoleKind is a kind of a ResourcePerformer that represents  a platform (e.g. vessel, aircraft, etc.) that is used in the context of a SystemsResource.</li><li>System - Indicates that the ResourceRole associated with the ResourceRoleKind is a kind of assembly of ResourcePerformers that is used in the context of another ResourcePerformer.</li><li>SubOrganization - Indicates that the ResourceRole associated with the ResourceRoleKind is a kind of  Organization that is typically the parent of another - e.g. a squadron may be part of a batallion, that is used in the context of a ResourcePerformer.</li><li>PostRole - Indicates that the ResourceRole associated with the ResourceRoleKind is a kind of Post that is used in the context of a ResourcePerformer.</li><li>ResponsibilityRole - Indicates that the ResourceRole associated with the ResourceRoleKind is a kind of Responsibility associated with a role that is used in the context of a ResourcePerformer.</li><li>Equipment - Indicates that the ResourceRole associated with the ResourceRoleKind is a kind of man made resource that is used to accomplish a task or function in the context of a ResourcePerformer.</li><li>SubSystemPart - Indicates that the ResourceRole associated with the ResourceRoleKind is a kind of  subsystem (represented as a ResourcePerformers) is is part of another ResourcePerformer.</li><li>UsedPhysicalArchitecture - Indicates that the ResourceRole associated with the ResourceRoleKind is a kind of existing PhysicalArchitecture that is used in the context of a ResourcePerformer.</li><li>HostedSoftware - Indicates that the ResourceRole associated with the ResourceRoleKind is a kind of software that is used in the context of a ResourcePerformer.</li><li>ArtifactComponent - Indicates that the ResourceRole associated with the ResourceRoleKind is a kind of non human resource that is used as a component in the context of a ResourcePerformer.</li><li>NaturalResourceComponent - Indicates that the ResourceRole associated with the ResourceRoleKind is a kind of natural resource that is used as a component in the context of a ResourcePerformer.</li><li>Other - Indicates that the ResourceRole associated with the ResourceRoleKind is another kind of RoleKind that is not on the enumerated list.</li></ul><p><strong>Architecture</strong> <strong>Framework</strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227157990 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00997: Rule

- page_id: `227157990`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227157990/Rule
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T10:57:58.442+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

An abstract grouping for all types of constraint (i.e. an Operational Constraint could detail the rules of accountancy best practice).

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Constraint

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">An abstract grouping for all types of constraint (i.e. an Operational Constraint could detail the rules of accountancy best practice).</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Architecture Framework</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</span></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Extension</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">UML Constraint</p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=236847195 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00998: Rule Kind

- page_id: `236847195`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/236847195/Rule+Kind
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-06-17T09:55:06.891+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

Enumeration of the possible kinds of Rules applicable to constraints. Its enumeration literals are:

- **StructuralAssertion**– Indicates that the Rule associated with the RuleKind is a statement that details that something of importance either exists as a concept of interest or exists in relationship to another thing of interest.
- **ActionAssertion**– Indicates that the Rule associated with the RuleKind is a statement that concerns some dynamic aspect.
- **Derivation**– Indicates that the Rule associated with the RuleKind is a statement that details a Rule derived from another Rule.
- **Contract**– Indicates that the Rule associated with the RuleKind is a statement that details a consent among parties regarding the terms and conditions of activities that said parties participate in.
- **Constraint**– Indicates that the Rule associated with the RuleKind is a statement that details a limitation, e.g., business rule, restraint, or operational limitation.
- **Guidance**– Indicates that the Rule associated with the RuleKind is a statement that details an authoritative statement intended to lead or steer the execution of actions.
- **SecurityPolicy**– Indicates that the Rule associated with the RuleKind is a statement that details a constraint that specifies policy for information handling, physical security, encryption, etc.
- **Caveat**– Indicates that the Rule associated with the RuleKind is a statement that details alternate conditions under which the rule is not valid.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227156057 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00999: S1 Service Taxonomy

- page_id: `227156057`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227156057/S1+Service+Taxonomy
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-02T10:30:36.152+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Service viewpoint
- labels: ['s1-service-taxonomy', 's1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

68648442

68648444

68648443

**Description**

The S1 Service Taxonomy view specifies a hierarchy of services. The elements in the hierarchy are service specifications (rather than service implementations), and the relationships between the elements are specializations – i.e. one service is a special type of another. The purpose of an S1 is to provide a governance structure for a Service-Oriented Architecture. Along with S3-Service Interfaces, it specifies a standard library of service specifications for an enterprise, which service implementers are expected to conform to.

A service is an implementation-independent specification of a packaged element of functionality and/or capability. There is potential for confusion between services and capabilities. The key indicator of a service is that it provides a standard interface to consumers. This means that services may be used as “wrappers” for one or more capability in order to provide a standard method of access to the capability. A well-designed service taxonomy provides a set of specifications for capability providers to adhere to. Services may have attributes and constraints (service policy) defined against them. Attributes are inherited by specialized services. Where an attribute is specified for a service, implementations of that service shall specify their values for the attribute.

The S1 view may be used to:

- SOA governance.
- Identification of services.
- Service planning.
- Service audit.
- Service gap analysis.
- Providing reference services for architectures.
- Tailoring generic services for specific applications.

**Implementation**

The S1 view can be represented using:

- S1 Service Taxonomy diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='S1.png']
- C1-S1 Capability to Services Mapping matrix. [ATTACHMENT filename='S1_matrix.png']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with S1 Services to Service Contracts Mapping matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] . The Services to Service Contracts Mapping matrix depicts mapping between the Services or their specializations and the Service Contracts they govern.

68648441

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Interface' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227156081 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01000: S2 Service Internal Structure

- page_id: `227156081`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227156081/S2+Service+Internal+Structure
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-02T10:30:36.510+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Service viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Creating S2 Service Internal Structure diagram

To create an S2 Service Internal Structure diagram

1. Create Service Roles .
2. Associate Service Roles using Service Connectors.
3. Create Service Exchanges flowing via Service Connectors.

##### Creating Service Exchange in S2 Service Internal Structure diagram

To create a Service Exchangein the S2 Service Internal Structure diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Service Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR3']**Realized Service Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Service Connector, and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='smart_manipulator_sevice_exchange.png'] .
  - On the diagram palette, click [ATTACHMENT filename='service_exchange.png'] and then click the Service Connector.
2. Specify the Service Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Service Functions.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR3']**Service Exchange Manager** dialog to analyze and manage the Service Exchangedata.

##### Showing or hiding realized Service Exchanges

You can choose to either show or hide the Service Exchange Item on the element realizing Service Exchanges.

To show/hide the realized Service Exchange Items

1. On the diagram, click the element for which you want to show or hide the realized exchange.
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulator toolbar' space='MD2024xR3'] :
  1. If theService Exchange Item is not displayed , the tooltip for the button is [ATTACHMENT filename='services_show_hide.png'] Show Realized Service Exchanges . Click it to hide the Service ExchangeItem.
  2. If the Service ExchangeItem is displayed , the tooltip for the button is[IMAGE alt='' src=''] Hide Realized **Service Exchanges** . Click it to hide the Service ExchangeItem. 
If theelement realizes only one Service Exchange, the Service ExchangeItem is displayed/hidden instantly with the click of a button.
3. If the element realizes multiple Service Exchanges, the Service Exchange Items dialog opens. In the dialog, do the following:
  1. Select the check boxes for the Service Exchanges whose Service Exchange Item you want to be displayed.
  2. Clear the check boxes for the Service Exchanges whose Service ExchangeItem you want to be hidden. To select all check boxes, click the**Select All**button. To clear all check boxes, click the**Clear All**button.
  3. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='show_service_exchanges.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227156065 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01001: S2 Service Structure

- page_id: `227156065`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227156065/S2+Service+Structure
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-29T08:59:48.846+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Service viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The S2 Viewpoint identifies and specifies how services are structured to create higher-aggregated services. To provide high-level views, dependencies to other services, nodes, and resources, as well as service interfaces and service functions can be represented.

**Implementation**

The S2 view can be represented using the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating S2 diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3'].

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the S2 Service Structure diagram.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

To provide high-level views, dependencies to other services, nodes, and resources, as well as service interfaces and service functions can be represented.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The S2 view can be represented using the<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating S2 diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ S2 Service Structure diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><strong>Sample</strong> </span></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="S2.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="S2 Service Structure" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6><span style="color: rgb(128,128,128);">An example of the S2 Service Structure diagram.</span></h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227156100 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01002: S3 Service Interfaces

- page_id: `227156100`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227156100/S3+Service+Interfaces
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-02T10:30:36.647+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Service viewpoint
- labels: ['s3-service-interfaces', 's3']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

68833919

68833921

68833920

**Description**

The purpose of the S3 Service Interfaces view is to define the interfaces presented by a service. A service presents one or more interfaces to consumers (a “consumer” being any agent capable of using the service – a person, an organization, a system or another service). In this case, the architect specifies provided interfaces. A service may also be capable of using interfaces exposed by other services, and the architect may specify these as used interfaces.

Specifying the interfaces that a service provides and uses defines which services are compatible with which other services. If Service A provides an interface, X and Service B can use Interface X then Service B can call upon at least some of the functionality of Service A.

**Implementation**

The S3 view can be represented using:

- S3 Service Interfaces diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='S3.png']
- S3 MS Word document report.

68833918

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Interface' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Method' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Flow Property' space='SYSMLP2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Parameter' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Port' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Port' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b6701496-6954-4ff3-9b11-9fa7c6f4c296"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">68833919</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4eaf0c68-530d-4b6f-9c77-b11c245821ca"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">68833921</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f114ce80-6094-46a7-b9fd-aca38f19c399"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">68833920</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The purpose of the S3  Service Interfaces view is to define the interfaces presented by a service. A service presents one or more interfaces to consumers (a “consumer” being any agent capable of using the service – a person, an organization, a system or another service). In this case, the architect specifies provided interfaces. A service may also be capable of using interfaces exposed by other services, and the architect may specify these as used interfaces.</p><p>Specifying the interfaces that a service provides and uses defines which services are compatible with which other services. If Service A provides an interface, X and Service B can use Interface X then Service B can call upon at least some of the functionality of Service A.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The S3 view can be represented using:</p><ul><li>S3 Service Interfaces diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.<br /><ac:image ac:title="S3 Service Interfaces diagram" ac:alt="S3 Service Interfaces diagram"><ri:attachment ri:filename="S3.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="S3 Service Interfaces" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="114de996-cdfe-4074-886b-5de4d3ff8db8">S3 MS Word document report.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ea61458a-ddf2-42c6-9d88-b9e40ab0a244"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">68833918</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service Interface" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service Method" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR3" ri:content-title="Flow Property" /></ac:link></span></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service Parameter" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Port" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Port" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227156102 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01003: S4 Service Functions

- page_id: `227156102`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227156102/S4+Service+Functions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:23:58.256+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Service viewpoint
- labels: ['s4-service-functions', 's4']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

824179280

824179282

824179281

**Description**

The S4 Service Functions view defines the behavior of a service in terms of the functions it is expected to perform. S4 is the key behavioral specification for services. Equivalent in nature to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L4 Logical Activities' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] and R4 - Resource Functions, it specifies a set of functions that a service implementation is expected to perform.

**Implementation**

The S4 view can be represented using:

- S4 Service Functions Taxonomy diagram based on the UML Clas diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='S4.png']

- S4 Service Functions diagram for the Operational Activity flows. This diagram is based on the UML Activity diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='S4_flow.png']
- UML Activity diagram.
- SysML Activity diagram.
- SysML Block Definition diagram.
- UML Class diagram.

824179278

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Function' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Function Action' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Function Control Flow' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Function Object Flow' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Method' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Parameter' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Interface' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Process Flow Diagrams From Compositions or Aggregations Defined in Process Diagrams' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227156108 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01004: S5 Service States

- page_id: `227156108`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227156108/S5+Service+States
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-02-09T14:27:11.669+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Service viewpoint
- labels: ['s5-service-states', 's5']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

824386959

824386961

824386960

**Description**

The purpose of the S5 Service States view is to specify the possible states a service may have, and the possible transitions between those states. It is generally considered good practice to make services stateless – i.e. consumers of a service are not aware of what state the service is in. However, in specifying a service, it is often necessary to specify the allowable states so as to constrain how implementations of the service will behave. S5 is a specification of those states, and the possible transitions between them.

**Implementation**

The S5 view can be represented using a S5 Service States diagram. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

824386958

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service State Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Interface' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Allocate Activities to State Internal Behaviors' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

It is generally considered good practice to make services stateless – i.e. consumers of a service are not aware of what state the service is in. However, in specifying a service, it is often necessary to specify the allowable states so as to constrain how implementations of the service will behave. S5 is a specification of those states, and the possible transitions between them.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The S5 view can be represented using a <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="ee4b8256-c894-4fc6-aa98-222af27fda31">S5 Service States diagram</ac:inline-comment-marker>.<br /><ac:image ac:title="S5 Service States diagram" ac:alt="S5 Service States diagram"><ri:attachment ri:filename="S5.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="S5 Service States" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c07d1543-7678-4bab-bd8f-d00b4602d54a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">824386958</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service State Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service Interface" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Allocate Activities to State Internal Behaviors" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227156110 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01005: S6 Service Interactions

- page_id: `227156110`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227156110/S6+Service+Interactions
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-02T10:30:37.015+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Service viewpoint
- labels: ['s6-service-interactions', 's6']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

824439177

824439179

824439178

**Description**

The purpose of S6 Service Interactions view is to specify how a service interacts with external agents, and the sequence and dependencies of those interactions. An S6 product does not specify the sequencing of an orchestrated set of services (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L6 Logical Sequence' space='UAF12P2024xR3']). Its purpose is to specify the general sequence of interactions that are possible for a given service.

**Implementation**

The S6 view can be represented using a S6 Service Interactions diagram which is based on the UML Sequence diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

824439176

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Message' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Interface' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Flow Property' space='SYSMLP2024xR3']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

An S6 product does not specify the sequencing of an orchestrated set of services (see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="L6 Logical Sequence" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[L6 - Logical Sequence]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>). Its purpose is to specify the general sequence of interactions that are possible for a given service.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The S6 view can be represented using a S6 Service Interactions diagram which is based on the UML Sequence diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="S6 Service Interactions diagram" ac:alt="S6 Service Interactions diagram"><ri:attachment ri:filename="S6.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="S6 Service Interactions" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b75e8ee9-be52-4cfb-8183-de3b778ba7ff"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">824439176</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" ri:content-title="Interaction" /></ac:link></span></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service Message" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service Interface" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR3" ri:content-title="Flow Property" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227156117 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01007: S8 Service Parametric

- page_id: `227156117`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227156117/S8+Service+Parametric
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-02T10:30:37.322+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Service viewpoint > S8 Service Policy
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

498484258

498484260

498484259

**Description**

A Parametric diagram includes the usage of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Block' space='SYSMLP2024xR3'] to constrain the properties of another Service Specifiction. It contains [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Property' space='SYSMLP2024xR3'] and constraint parameters as well as other properties from within that internal block context. All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a constraint parameter or contain a property that is bound to a constraint parameter (through any number of containment levels). A constraint block generally contain many constraints, each of them containing many constraint parameters.

498484257

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Parametric diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5a3480db-fc7b-4a03-a43a-b23d31d83517"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">498484258</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8b862fb5-b94e-4498-8552-9b333d962dca"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">498484260</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9889853a-21ca-4dc2-846e-ce9969bc4bac"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">498484259</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>A Parametric diagram includes the usage of a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR3" ri:content-title="Constraint Block" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[constraint block]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to constrain the properties of another Service Specifiction. It contains <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR3" ri:content-title="Constraint Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[constraint properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and constraint parameters as well as other properties from within that internal block context. All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a constraint parameter or contain a property that is bound to a constraint parameter (through any number of containment levels). A constraint block generally contain many constraints, each of them containing many constraint parameters.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="67eb6d9b-397a-4e8d-9f70-f4bd54e72298"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">498484257</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Parametric diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227156114 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01008: S8 Service Policy

- page_id: `227156114`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227156114/S8+Service+Policy
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:51:59.102+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Service viewpoint
- labels: ['s8-service-policy', 's8']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

824966407

824966409

824966408

**Description**

The purpose of the S8 Service Policy view is to specify constraints that apply to implementations of services. To better enable consistency and re-use of service specifications, it is important to set constraints on how a service should behave. An S8 product specifies constraints against services to which implementations of must conform.

**Implementation**

The S8 view can be represented using:

- S8 Service Policy table. [ATTACHMENT filename='S8.png']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='S8 Service Parametric' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] diagram.
- S8 spreadsheet report .

**Constraint owner**

You can choose where all the constraints will be stored through the**Project Options**.

To select a constraint owner

1. In the main menu, click Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. Go to General > UAF , and in the Default Constraint Owners property group, choose a needed owner.

824966406

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Policy' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Interface' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

**Related procedures**

- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

918471926

918471928

918471927

The Security (Sc) domain addresses the security constraints and information assurance attributes that exist on exchanges between resources and Operational Performers. It illustrates the security assets, security constraints, security controls, families, and measures required to address specific security concerns.

918471925

**Views**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227156766 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01012: Security Connectivity

- page_id: `227156766`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227156766/Security+Connectivity
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:14:07.503+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Security
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

918511719

918511721

918511720

**Description**

The Security Connectivity (Sc-Cn) domain addresses the security constraints and information assurance attributes that exist on exchanges across resources and across performers. It lists security exchanges across security assets; the applicable security controls; and the security enclaves that house the producers and consumers of the exchanges. Measurements can optionally be included.

**Implementation**

The Security Connectivity (Sc-Cn) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Internal Connectivity (Resource) diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Connectivity (Resource) Table' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Role-based Connectivity (Resource) Table' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] .
- Reports (.xlsx): - Security Connectivity Table Excel Spreadsheet. - Security Role-based Connectivity Table Excel Spreadsheet.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Security Connectivity diagram

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Security Internal Connectivity diagram

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Security Connectivity table

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Security Role-based Connectivity table

- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Security
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

918678581

918678584

918678582

**Definition**

The Security Constraints (Sc-Ct) domain shows the security-related policy, guidance, laws and regulations as applicable to resources. It Specifies textual rules/non-functional requirements that are security constraints on resources, information and data (e.g. security-related in the form of rules (e.g. access control policy). A common way of representing access control policy is through the use of XACML (eXtensible Access Control Markup Language), it is expected that implementations of UAF allow users to link security constraints to external files represented in XACML.

**Implementation**

The Security Constraints (Sc-Ct) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Constraints Definition diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Constraints table' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] .

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Security Constraints diagram

**Constraint owner**

You can choose where all the constraints will be stored through the **Project Options**.

To select a constraint owner

1. In the main menu, click Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. Go to General > UAF , and in the Default Constraint Owners property group, choose a needed owner.

918678580

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227157993 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01015: Security Control

- page_id: `227157993`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227157993/Security+Control
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T13:21:32.625+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The management, operational, and technical control (i.e., safeguard or countermeasure) prescribed for an information system to protect the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of the system and its information.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Class

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The management, operational, and technical control (i.e., safeguard or countermeasure) prescribed for an information system to protect the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of the system and its information.</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Architecture Framework</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</span></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Extension</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">UML Class</p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227157994 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01016: Security Control Action

- page_id: `227157994`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227157994/Security+Control+Action
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T13:21:24.673+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A call of a Security Control in the context of another Security Control. It is used to show how a set of Security Controls can be used to protect an asset in a particular context.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Call Behavior Action

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">A call of a Security Control in the context of another Security Control. It is used to show how a set of Security Controls can be used to protect an asset in a particular context. </p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Architecture Framework</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</span></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Extension</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">UML Call Behavior Action</p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227157996 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01018: Security Enclave

- page_id: `227157996`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227157996/Security+Enclave
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T13:21:01.686+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

Collection of information systems connected by one or more internal networks under the control of a single authority and security policy. The systems may be structured by physical proximity or by function, independent of location.

**Architecture** **Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Class

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Description</strong></p><p>Collection of information systems connected by one or more internal networks under the control of a single authority and security policy. The systems may be structured by physical proximity or by function, independent of location.</p><p><strong>Architecture</strong> <strong>Framework</strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</span></p><p><strong>Extension</strong></p><p>UML Class</p><p><br /></p>
````

**Description**

The Security Parameters (Sc-Pm) domain depicts the measurements of security elements.

The intended usage of the Security Parameters (Sc-Pm) domain includes:

- Definition of performance characteristics and measurements.
- Identification of non-functional requirements.

**Implementation**

The Security Parameters (Sc-Pm) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Environment diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227157997 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01021: Security Process

- page_id: `227157997`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227157997/Security+Process
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T13:20:50.768+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The security-related procedure that satisfies the security control requirement.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Activity

## PAGE 01023: Security Processes

- page_id: `227156803`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227156803/Security+Processes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:10:38.751+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Security
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

918798584

918798586

918798585

**Description**

The **Security Processes (Sc-Pr)**domain shows the specification of the Security Control families, security controls, and measures required to address a specific security baseline. It provides a set of Security Controls and any possible enhancements as applicable to assets. The activity diagram describes operational or resource level processes that apply (operational level) or implement (resource level) security controls/enhancements to assets located in enclaves and across enclaves. This Security Process domain can be instantiated either as a variant of an activity/flow diagram or as a hierarchical work breakdown structure.

**Implementation**

The Security Processes (Sc-Pr) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Processes diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Process Flow (Resources) diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Process Flow (Operational) diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] .

918798583

## PAGE 01024: Security Risk

- page_id: `227157999`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227157999/Security+Risk
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T13:20:35.524+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The level of impact on enterprise operations, assets, or individuals resulting from the operation of an information system given the potential impact of a threat and the likelihood of that threat occurring [NIST SP 800-65].

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extensions**

Class

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The level of impact on enterprise operations, assets, or individuals resulting from the operation of an information system given the potential impact of a threat and the likelihood of that threat occurring [NIST SP 800-65].</p><p><strong>Architecture Framework</strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</span></p><p><strong>Extensions</strong></p><p>Class</p>
````

## PAGE 01025: Security Structure

- page_id: `227156813`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227156813/Security+Structure
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:56:29.193+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Security
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

918973399

918973401

918973400

**Description**

The Security Structure (Sc-Sr) domain shows the structure of security information and where it is used at the operational and resource level. It captures the allocation of assets (operational and resource, information, and data) across the security enclaves, shows applicable security controls necessary to protect organizations, systems, and information during processing, while in storage (bdd), and during transmission (flows on an ibd). This view also captures Asset Aggregation and allocates the usage of the aggregated information at a location.

**Implementation**

The Security Structure (Sc-Sr) domain is represented by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3'].

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Security Structure diagram

## PAGE 01026: Security Taxonomy

- page_id: `227156829`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

919067284

919067286

919067285

**Description**

The Security Taxonomy (Sc-Tx) domain shows the security assets and security enclaves. It defines the hierarchy of security assets and asset owners that are available to implement security, security constraints (policy, guidance, laws and regulations) and details where they are located (security enclaves).

**Implementation**

The Security Taxonomy (Sc-Tx) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Taxonomy diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Taxonomy table' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] . This table can be used to create and describe major domain elements faster.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Security Taxonomy diagram

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Security Taxonomy table

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

565418804

565418806

565418805

**Description**

The Security Traceability (Sc-Tr) domain depicts the mapping of a risk to each of the following: risk owner, risk mitigations, and affected asset roles.

**Implementation**

The Security Traceability (Sc-Tr) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Controls to Risks Mapping Matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] shows which operational or resource asset roles mitigate risks.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Risks to Assets Mapping Matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] shows which risks are applicable to Asset Roles.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Security Controls to Risk Mapping Matrix

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Risks to Assets Mapping Matrix

565418802

**Related procedures**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227157101 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01028: Select Framework Views dialog

- page_id: `227157101`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227157101/Select+Framework+Views+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-22T12:16:24.821+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Dialogs
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When you create a new project, you can easily define your own project structure by choosing only the views that you really need. You can achieve this by using the **Select Framework Views** dialog.

The **Select Framework Views**dialog is displayed when the **Select Framework Views** option in the **New Project** dialog is set to *True*. By default this option is set to *True*. If you want to create a project with the default structure containing all the views, set this option to *False*.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Select Framework Views option in the New Project dialog

In the **Select Framework Views**dialog, you can select which views you want to create in your model.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The dialog for selecting views for your new project

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><br /></p><p>When you create a new project, you can easily define your own project structure by choosing only the views that you really need. You can achieve this by using the <strong>Select Framework Views</strong> dialog.</p><p>The <strong>Select Framework Views </strong>dialog is displayed when the <strong>Select Framework Views</strong> option in the <strong>New Project</strong> dialog is set to <em>True</em>. By default this option is set to <em>True</em>. If you want to create a project with the default structure containing all the views, set this option to <em>False</em>.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_project_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Select Framework Views dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>The Select Framework Views option in the New Project dialog</h6><p>In the <strong>Select Framework Views </strong>dialog, you can select which views you want to create in your model. </p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_framework_views.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Select Framework Views dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>The dialog for selecting views for your new project</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227155308 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01029: Select Perspective dialog

- page_id: `227155308`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227155308/Select+Perspective+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-20T12:56:43.708+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Getting started > Customizing and Selecting Perspective
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When you launch a modeling tool for the first time, the **Select Perspective**dialog appears with the ability to switch between perspectives.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

| Element Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Business Analyst | This perspective provides features for Business Analysts. The role is responsible for defining business architecture. Code engineering, transformations, extensions, and other non-related features are hidden. |
| Business Architect | This perspective provides features for Business Architects. The role is responsible for defining business architecture. Code engineering, transformations, extensions, and other non-related features are hidden. |
| DoDAF 2.0 Architect | This perspective provides features dedicated to DoDAF 2.0 Architects. UML modeling features, code engineering, transformations, and other non-DoDAF 2.0-related features are hidden. |
| DODAF Architect | This perspective provides features dedicated to DoDAF Architects. UML modeling features, code engineering, transformations, and other non-DoDAF-related features are hidden. |
| MODAF Architect | This perspective provides features dedicated to MODAF Architects. UML modeling features, code engineering, transformations, and other non-MODAF-related features are hidden. |
| NAF 4.0 Architect | This perspective provides features dedicated to NAF 4.0 Architects. UML modeling features, code engineering, transformations, and other non-NAF 4.0-related features are hidden. |
| NAF Architect | This perspective provides features dedicated to NAF Architects. UML modeling features, code engineering, transformations, and other non-NAF-related features are hidden. |
| SOA Engineer | This perspective is a set of roles primarily involved in designing and implementing projects: Software Architect, Designer, Interface Designer, and Database Designer. This is the default Magic Systems of Systems Architect and Cameo Enterprise Architect configuration. All functionalities are available in expert mode. |
| Software Architect | This perspective is dedicated to Software Architect, Designer, Interface Designer, and Database Designer roles that design and implement the project. All functionalities are available in expert mode. |
| System Analyst | This perspective is a set of roles primarily dedicated to eliciting requirements and modeling the system. Analysis features are highlighted. Code engineering, transformations, and other non-related features are hidden. |
| System Engineer | This perspective provides features dedicated to System Engineers. SysML support is accessible. Requirements elicitation and modeling features are highlighted. UML modeling features, code engineering, transformations, and other non-system-modeling-related features are hidden. |
| TOGAF Architect | This perspective provides features for TOGAF Architects. The role is responsible for defining business architecture. Code engineering, transformations, extensions, and other non-related features are hidden. |
| UAF Architect | The perspective provides features dedicated to UAF Architects with all the UAF products available. UML modeling features, code engineering, transformations, and other non-UAF-related features are hidden. |
| EA-BPMN Architect* | This perspective provides features dedicated to Enterprise and IT Architects with the essential elements for this area: capabilities, requirements, operational behaviors (with BPMN), resources (hardware, software, facilities), data, and personnel. The user interface is simplified; advanced UAF elements and diagrams are hidden. |
| Zachman Framework | This Quick Start perspective provides basic features dedicated to modeling and not an overpopulated interface for quick learning. Code engineering, transformations, and other advanced features are hidden but easily accessible in the expert mode of this perspective. |
| Customize | Open the Customize Perspectives dialog to customize the selected perspective. You can make your tool interface less complicated by hiding irrelevant features or commands. For example, you can group functional features, and show, or hide selected commands. |

*Starting with the 2024x version, the **UAF Enterprise Architecture Project**was renamed to**EA with BPMN Project.**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227157104 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01030: Select Symbol dialog

- page_id: `227157104`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227157104/Select+Symbol+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-22T12:13:26.657+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Dialogs
- labels: ['select-symbol-dialog', 'affiliation', 'frame']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1015178682

1015178684

1015178683

Use **Select Symbol** dialog to apply military symbols for resources.

To open the **Select Symbol** dialog, do either

- Create resources from MIL-STD-2525C Symbology toolbar.
- Select resource and click [ATTACHMENT filename='military_symbols.png'] on the smart manipulator toolbar.
- Open the Specification window of any UAF element. Select Image property cell and click [ATTACHMENT filename='military_symbols.png'] . The **Select Symbol** dialog opens.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The **Select Symbol** dialog consists of these four components:

##### Whole hierarchy tree/ Recently used tree

Select the military symbol from the Whole hierarchy tree or Recently used tree.

##### Quick filter

You can search for symbols in the **Whole hierarchy** or **Recently used** tabs by using one of the following way:

- Simple Search: type what you want to find in the hierarchy.
- ID Search: use an MIL-STD-2525C symbology ID to find the exact symbol in the hierarchy. For example, type "1.X.3.2.2.1.3".
- Pattern Search: type * to find any symbols. For example, type "*Frying" if you want to find "Fixed Wing" somewhere in the hierarchy.

##### Affiliation area

Affiliation - the threat posed by the warfighting object being represented. The basic affiliation categories are unknown, friend, neutral, and hostile. Additionally the pending, suspect, assumed friend, joker and faker affiliations may be applied. Affiliation can be defined for all military symbols available. Its a bit different with a frame property. It can be changed for no more than 1/5 of a symbols.

##### Frame area

Frame - the geometric border of a symbol that provides an indication of the affiliation, battle dimension, and status of a warfighting object.

##### Preview area

You can preview the symbol before applying it on the resource.

1015178681

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying military symbols' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

if you want to find &quot;Fixed Wing&quot; somewhere in the hierarchy.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><h3><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Affiliation area</span><br /></span></h3><p>Affiliation - the threat posed by the warfighting object being represented. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227155300 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01031: Selecting Environment

- page_id: `227155300`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227155300/Selecting+Environment
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2019-07-31T07:00:06.744+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Getting started
- labels: ['environment-selection-dialog']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Environment Selection** dialog opens when starting the UAF plugin for the first time.

To select the enterprise architecture framework environment

1. Select the environment according to the architecture you are going to build: Read the brief descriptions of each enterprise architecture framework provided in the dialog. This information may help you decide what to choose.
  - UAF Enterprise Architecture
  - UAF
  - DoDAF 2.0
  - MODAF
  - NAF v. 3
  - NAF 4.0
  - BPMN
  - SysML
  - DoDAF 1.5
  - TOGAF
2. Click to select the Expert check box, if you want the user interface to be complex and have all details exposed. And if you are a new user, leave the Expert check box empty.
3. Click the Continue button. The sample project corresponding the selected enterprise architecture framework will be opened (only if using Cameo Enterprise Architecture tool).

For information about changing the environment, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Converting model between Enterprise Architecture Frameworks' space='UAF12P2024xR3'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>Environment Selection</strong> dialog opens when starting the UAF plugin for the first time.</p><p>To select the enterprise architecture framework environment</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the environment according to the architecture you are going to build:<br /><ul><li>UAF Enterprise Architecture</li><li>UAF</li><li>DoDAF 2.0</li><li>MODAF</li><li>NAF v. 3</li><li>NAF 4.0</li><li>BPMN</li><li>SysML</li><li>DoDAF 1.5</li><li>TOGAF</li></ul>Read the brief descriptions of each enterprise architecture framework provided in the dialog. This information may help you decide what to choose.</li><li>Click to select the <strong>Expert</strong> check box, if you want the user interface to be complex and have all details exposed. And if you are a new user, leave the <strong>Expert</strong> check box empty.</li><li>Click the <strong>Continue</strong> button. The sample project corresponding the selected enterprise architecture framework will be opened (only if using Cameo Enterprise Architecture tool).</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3c3a5619-bc24-48cf-9e54-25b1c53712c1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For information about changing the environment, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Converting model between Enterprise Architecture Frameworks" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

## PAGE 01032: Sequence

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A dependency relationship that asserts one Individual's temporal extent is completely before the temporal extent of another.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extensions**

Dependency
