## PAGE 01033: Service

- page_id: `227158001`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227158001/Service
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T13:20:12.587+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A mechanism to enable access to one or more capabilities, where the access is provided using a prescribed service interface and is exercised consistent with service constraints and policies.

**Architecture** **Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

Class

````html
<p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">A mechanism to enable access to one or more capabilities, where the access is provided using a prescribed service interface and is exercised consistent with service constraints and policies.</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Architecture</strong> <strong>Framework</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</span></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Extension</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">Class</p><p><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227157107 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01035: Service Assignment Wizard

- page_id: `227157107`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227157107/Service+Assignment+Wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-10-13T10:44:05.274+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Dialogs
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Service Assignment Wizard** is used to define Service for the Version Of Configuration in specified time frame.

To open the **Service Assignment Wizard**

1. Open a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Roadmap diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] diagram (UAF)
2. On the diagram pane, right-click the Version Of Configuration and select Service . The Service Assignment Wizard opens.

The wizard consists of three steps:

truenone

##### Service selection

In this step, you can select the existing or create new Service that will be assigned for Version Of Configuration.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create a Service for the Version Of Configuration

1. In the Select Service step, click the Creation Mode button.
2. Select package where you want to create a Service.
3. Click the Create button.
4. In the Specification window of the newly created Service, type its name.
5. Click Close .

##### Start date specification

In this step, you can set the start date (or select the date from the Timeline) when the selected Service is being used in Version Of Configuration.

For more information on setting the day and time, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Date and Time Settings dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR3']**Date and Time Settings** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### End date specification

In this step, you can set the end date (or select the date from the Timeline) when the selected Service stops being used in Version Of Configuration.

For more information on setting the day and time, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Date and Time Settings dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR3']**Date and Time Settings** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements

**Description**

A channel for exchange between two Service Specifications. Where one acts as the consumer of the other.

**Architecture** **Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Connector

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extensions**

Information Flow

## PAGE 01040: Service Exchange Item

- page_id: `227158007`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227158007/Service+Exchange+Item
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T11:07:08.504+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

An abstract grouping for elements that defines the types of elements that can be exchanged between Services and conveyed by a Service Exchange.

Service Exchange Item is abstract.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extensions**

Element

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227158008 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01041: Service Exchange Kind

- page_id: `227158008`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227158008/Service+Exchange+Kind
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T11:06:55.468+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

Enumeration of the possible kinds of Service Exchange.

The following are enumeration literals for Service Exchange Kind:

- Configuration Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where Capability Configurations flow from one Operational Performer to another.
- Energy Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where energy is flowed from one Operational Performer to another.
- Information Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where information is flowed from one Operational Performer to another.
- Materiel Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow of materiel (artifacts) between Functions.
- Organizational Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where human resources (Post Types, Role Types) flow between Operational Performers.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extensions**

Enumeration

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">Enumeration of the possible kinds of Service Exchange.</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">The following are enumeration literals for Service Exchange Kind:</p><ul><li>Configuration Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where Capability Configurations flow from one Operational Performer to another.</li><li>Energy Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where energy is flowed from one Operational Performer to another.</li><li>Information Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where information is flowed from one Operational Performer to another.</li><li>Materiel Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow of materiel (artifacts) between Functions.</li><li>Organizational Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where human resources (Post Types, Role Types) flow between Operational Performers.</li></ul><p><strong>Architecture Framework</strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</span></p><p><strong>Extensions</strong></p><p>Enumeration</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227157111 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01042: Service Exchange Manager dialog

- page_id: `227157111`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227157111/Service+Exchange+Manager+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-04-25T14:30:38.881+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Dialogs
- labels: ['service-exchange-manager-dialog']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Service Exchange Manager** dialog enables analysis and management of the Service Exchanges data. You can:

- Edit existing Service Exchanges by adding new Service Exchange Items
- Realize or ignore existing Service Exchanges on selected relationships
- Change the Service Exchange Item 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- Review producing and consuming Service Function pairs and remove the ones that are not needed
- Review the list of diagrams that display the Realizing Elements of the selected Exchange

To open a **Service Exchange Manager** dialog

1. Selecta Service Association, Service Connector, Service Control Flow, Service Object Flow, or Service Message in the diagram pane.
2. On the smart manipulator, click [ATTACHMENT filename='service_exchnge_icon.png'] . The Service Exchange Manager dialog opens.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- The Affected Diagrams button opens the list of diagrams that are affected by the selected Realized Service Exchange.
- The All Affected Diagrams button opens the list of diagrams that are affected by all the Realized Exchanges in the Service Exchange Manager dialog.

To learn more about previewing affected diagrams, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Preview Affected Diagrams dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR3'].

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Service Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Preview Affected Diagrams dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Producing and Consuming Service Functions dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Internal Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Connectivity table' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Role-based Connectivity table' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Connectivity matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Service Sequences diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Resource Exchange in SvcV-10c diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='S2 Service Internal Structure' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

## PAGE 01043: Service Function

An Activity that describes the abstract behavior of Service Specifications, regardless of the actual implementation.

**Architecture** **Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Activity

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227156056 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01056: Service viewpoint

- page_id: `227156056`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227156056/Service+viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:49:59.236+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0
- labels: ['service-viewpoint']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

824082325

824082327

824082326

The Service viewpoint is based on the concept of a Service-Oriented Architecture (SOA), which is fundamental to the NNEC paradigm. It is a description of services needed to directly support the operational domain as described in the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Logical viewpoint' space='UAF12P2024xR3']. A service, within the Service viewpoint, is understood in its broadest sense, as a unit of work through which a provider provides a useful result to a consumer. It focuses strictly on identifying and describing services. The viewpoint also supports the description of service taxonomies, service orchestrations, a mapping of services to operational activities, and a description of various forms of service behavior.

824082324

**Views**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

It focuses strictly on identifying and describing services. The viewpoint also supports the description of service taxonomies, service orchestrations, a mapping of services to operational activities, and a description of various forms of service behavior.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6f10d999-041b-41ff-a2f5-db4189aa34d4"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">824082324</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Views</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="7d837a84-49ab-4c64-8914-4d39441fcd73" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227156477 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01058: Services Connectivity

- page_id: `227156477`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227156477/Services+Connectivity
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:58:23.832+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Services
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

919223678

919223680

919223679

**Description**

The Services Connectivity (Sv-Cn) domain shows the interoperability among services. It specifies service interfaces, e.g. provided and required service operations, to ensure compatibility and reusability of services.

The purpose of the Services Connectivity (Sv-Cn) domain is to define the interfaces presented by a service. A Service presents one or more interfaces to consumers (a “consumer” being any agent capable of using the service – a person, an organization, a system or another service). In this case, the architect specifies the provided interfaces. A service may also be capable of using interfaces exposed by other services, and the architect may specify these as used interfaces.

**Implementation**

The Services Connectivity (Sv-Cn) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Internal Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] .

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Services Connectivity diagram

919223677

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="05336222-e704-4e40-8fdf-8ca1b9abeea8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">919223678</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2e9cbfa8-58ea-45ef-a884-7e8ef08e0084"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">919223680</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c26b58cb-d08b-415f-ae29-690aa25a3116"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">919223679</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The Services Connectivity (Sv-Cn) domain shows the interoperability among services. It specifies service interfaces, e.g. provided and required service operations, to ensure compatibility and reusability of services.</p><p>The purpose of the Services Connectivity (Sv-Cn) domain is to define the interfaces presented by a service. A Service presents one or more interfaces to consumers (a “consumer” being any agent capable of using the service – a person, an organization, a system or another service). In this case, the architect specifies the provided interfaces. A service may also be capable of using interfaces exposed by other services, and the architect may specify these as used interfaces.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The Services Connectivity (Sv-Cn) domain is represented by:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Services Connectivity diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Services Connectivity diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Services Internal Connectivity diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Services Internal Connectivity diagrams]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ul><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="services_connectivity.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Services Connectivity" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>An example of the Services Connectivity diagram</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="68a11de3-5032-4c0b-ba80-31bed7c53ce0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">919223677</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Measurement Set" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Service" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Measurable Element" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Provided Service Level" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Required Service Level" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service Connector" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service Interface" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227156507 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01059: Services Constraints

- page_id: `227156507`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227156507/Services+Constraints
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:51:38.018+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Services
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

919305469

919305472

919305470

**Description**

The Services Constraints (Sv-Cn) domain shows the service policies that apply to the implementation of services. It specifies traditional textual service policies that are constraints on how services are implemented within resources. The addition of SysML parametrics provides a computational means of defining service policies across the enterprise or within a specific service configuration.

The purpose of the Services Constraints (Sv-Cn) domain is to specify constraints that apply to providers and consumers of services. To better enable consistency and re-use of services, it is important to set constraints on how a service should behave. A Services Constraints (Sv-Cn) domain product specifies constraints against services to which service implementations must conform.

**Implementation**

The Services Constraints (Sv-Cn) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Constraints table' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Constraints Definition diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] .
- Services Constraints Excel Spreadsheet (.xlsx) report.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Services Constraints table

**Constraint owner**

You can choose where all the constraints will be stored through the **Project Options**.

To select a constraint owner

1. In the main menu, click Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. Go to General > UAF , and in the Default Constraint Owners property group, choose a needed owner.

919305467

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Rule' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

It specifies traditional textual service policies that are constraints on how services are implemented within resources. The addition of SysML parametrics provides a computational means of defining service policies across the enterprise or within a specific service configuration.</p><p>The purpose of the Services Constraints (Sv-Cn) domain is to specify constraints that apply to providers and consumers of services. To better enable consistency and re-use of services, it is important to set constraints on how a service should behave. A Services Constraints (Sv-Cn) domain product specifies constraints against services to which service implementations must conform.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The Services Constraints (Sv-Cn) domain is represented by:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Services Constraints table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Services Constraints table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Services Constraints Definition diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Services Constraints Definition diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Services Constraints Excel Spreadsheet (.xlsx) report.</li></ul><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="service_constraints.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Services Constraints" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>An example of the Services Constraints table</h6><p><strong>Constraint owner</strong></p><p>You can choose where all the constraints will be stored through the <strong>Project Options</strong>.</p><p>To select a constraint owner</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu, click <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227156576 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01060: Services Motivation

- page_id: `227156576`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227156576/Services+Motivation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-01-31T11:33:55.889+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Services
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The **Services Motivation** (Rq-Mv) view provides a central reference for a set of stakeholder needs expressed as requirements, their relationship (via traceability) to more detailed requirements, and the solution described by the architecture that will meet those requirements. It is used to represent requirements, their properties, and relationships (trace, verify, satisfy, refine) both to each other and to UAF architectural elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The <strong>Services Motivation</strong> (Rq-Mv) view provides a central reference for a set of stakeholder needs expressed as requirements, their relationship (via traceability) to more detailed requirements, and the solution described by the architecture that will meet those requirements. It is used to represent requirements, their properties, and relationships (trace, verify, satisfy, refine) both to each other and to UAF architectural elements.</p>
````

**Description**

The Services Parameters (Sv-Pm) domain depicts the measurements of Services.

The intended usage of the Services Parameters (Sv-Pm) domain includes:

- Definition of performance characteristics and measurements.
- Identification of non-functional requirements.

**Implementation**

The Services Parameters (Sv-Pm) domain is represented by:

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227156514 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01062: Services Processes

- page_id: `227156514`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227156514/Services+Processes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:56:14.909+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Services
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

920305902

920305904

920305903

**Description**

The Services Processes (Sv-Pr) domain shows the behavior of a service in terms of the operational activities and functions it is expected to support. It provides detailed information regarding the allocation of service functions to service specifications, and data flows between service functions. The Services Processes (Sv-Pr) domain is the key behavioral specification for services.

**Implementation**

The Services Processes (Sv-Pr) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Processes diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Processes Flow diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] .
- Services Processes Flow diagram (BPD). It is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD) and describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the business work. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed business activities. An Services Processes Flow (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Services Processes diagram

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Services Processes Flow diagram

920305901

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Information' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227156522 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01063: Services Roadmap

- page_id: `227156522`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227156522/Services+Roadmap
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:04:58.426+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Services
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

921650000

921650003

921650002

**Description**

The Services Roadmap (Sv-Rm) domain shows the service changes over time. It provides an overview of how service changes over time. It shows the combination of several services mapped against a timeline. The Services Roadmap (Sv-Rm) domain presents a whole lifecycle view of resources (services), describing how it changes over time. It shows the structure of several resources mapped against a timeline.

In addition, this model is useful in support of net-centric (service-oriented) implementation of services. This model can present a timeline of services that evolve or are replaced over time, including services that are internal and external to the scope of the architecture.

The intended usage of the Services Roadmap (Sv-Rm) domain includes:

- Development of incremental acquisition strategy.
- Planning technology insertion.

**Implementation**

The Services Roadmap (Sv-Rm) domain is represented by a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Roadmap diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] which is realized as a time based diagram.

921649999

## PAGE 01064: Services Sequences

- page_id: `227156530`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227156530/Services+Sequences
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-02T10:30:46.819+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Services
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

919410764

919410767

919410766

**Description**

The Services Sequences (Sv-Sq) domain shows the behavior of a service specification in terms of expected time-ordered examination of the interactions between service roles. It specifies how service roles interact with each other, service providers and consumers, and the sequence and dependencies of those interactions.

The purpose of the Services Sequences (Sv-Sq) domain is to specify how a service interacts with external agents and the sequence and dependencies of those interactions. A Services Sequences (Sv-Sq) domain product does not specify the sequencing of an orchestrated set of services. Its purpose is to specify the general sequence of interactions that are possible for a given service.

**Implementation**

The Services Sequences (Sv-Sq) domain is represented by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Service Sequences diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] that is based on [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MD2024xR3'].

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227156535 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01065: Services States

- page_id: `227156535`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227156535/Services+States
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-02T10:30:46.993+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Services
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

921781882

921781884

921781883

**Description**

The Services States (Sv-St) domain shows the behavior of a service specification in terms of states and events causing transitions between states. It specifies the possible states a service specification may have, and the possible transitions between those states.

The purpose of the Services States (Sv-St) domain is to specify the possible states a service may have, and the possible transitions between those states. It is generally considered good practice to make services stateless – i.e. consumers of a service are not aware of what state the service is in. However, in specifying a service, it is often necessary to specify the allowable states so as to constrain how implementations of the service will behave. As the states of a service may affect its ability to supply those services, it is important for consumers to understand those states. The Services States (Sv-St) domain is a specification of those states, and the possible transitions between them.

**Implementation**

The Services States (Sv-St) domain is represented by a Services States diagram that is based on [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='MD2024xR3'].

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Services States diagram

It specifies the possible states a service specification may have, and the possible transitions between those states.</p><p>The purpose of the Services States (Sv-St) domain is to specify the possible states a service may have, and the possible transitions between those states. It is generally considered good practice to make services stateless – i.e. consumers of a service are not aware of what state the service is in. However, in specifying a service, it is often necessary to specify the allowable states so as to constrain how implementations of the service will behave. As the states of a service may affect its ability to supply those services, it is important for consumers to understand those states. The Services States (Sv-St) domain is a specification of those states, and the possible transitions between them.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The Services States (Sv-St) domain is represented by a Services States diagram that is based on <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" ri:content-title="State Machine diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[UML State Machine diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="service_states.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Services States" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>An example of the Services States diagram </h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="05249d80-0bda-4cd6-806f-bb755fc7453c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">921781881</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service State Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Allocate Activities to State Internal Behaviors" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 01067: Services Taxonomy

- page_id: `227156554`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227156554/Services+Taxonomy
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:05:51.803+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Services
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

921953081

921953084

921953083

**Description**

The Services Taxonomy (Sv-Tx) domain shows the service types and required and provided service levels of these types.

The Services Taxonomy (Sv-Tx) domain specifies a hierarchy of services. The elements in the hierarchy are services (i.e., service interfaces), and the relationships between the elements are specializations – i.e., one Service is a special type of another.

**Implementation**

The Services Taxonomy (Sv-Tx) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Taxonomy diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] , which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Taxonomy table' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] . This table can be used to create and describe major domain elements faster.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of Services Taxonomy diagram

The elements in the hierarchy are services (i.e., service interfaces), and the relationships between the elements are specializations – i.e., one Service is a special type of another.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The Services Taxonomy (Sv-Tx) domain is represented by:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Services Taxonomy diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Services Taxonomy diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, which is based on the UML Class diagram.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Services Taxonomy table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Services Taxonomy table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. This table can be used to create and describe major domain elements faster.</li></ul><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image ac:title="Service Taxonomy" ac:alt="Service Taxonomy"><ri:attachment ri:filename="service_taxonomy.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Services Taxonomy" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>An example of Services Taxonomy diagram</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e1a14e33-f384-44c4-bb1f-cf8ced93ac13"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">921953080</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Measurement" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Measurement Set" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Property Set" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Service" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Measurement" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Property Set" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Provided Service Level" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Required Service Level" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service Policy" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="c2c12614-7e5d-4d54-b51b-c64195df79db" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 01068: Services Traceability

- page_id: `227156566`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227156566/Services+Traceability
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:57:32.174+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Services
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

922069205

922069207

922069206

**Description**

The Services Traceability (Sv-St) domain shows the traceability between operational activities and services that support them and depicts how services contribute to the achievement of a capability.

**Implementation**

The Services Traceability (Sv-St) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services to Capabilities Mapping matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] . The intended usage of the Service Exhibits Traceability includes:
  - Tracing Capability requirements to Services .
  - Capability audit.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services to Operational Activities Mapping matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR3']. 
The intended usage of the Services to Operational Activities Mapping Matrix includes:
  - Tracing service functional requirements to user requirements.
  - Tracing solution options to requirements.
  - Identification of overlaps or gaps.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services to Service Contracts Mapping matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] . The Services to Service Contracts Mapping matrix depicts mapping between the Services or their specializations and the Service Contracts they govern.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Services to Capabilities Mapping Matrix

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Services to Operational Activities Mapping Matrix

922069204

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Activity' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

**Related procedures**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227155455 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01069: Services viewpoint

- page_id: `227155455`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227155455/Services+viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:12:10.360+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0
- labels: ['services-viewpoint']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

394609712

394609714

394609713

“The DoDAF-described Models within the Services Viewpoint describes services and their interconnections providing or supporting, DoD functions. DoD functions include both warfighting and business functions. The Service Models associate service resources to the operational and capability requirements. These resources support the operational activities and facilitate the exchange of information. The relationship between architectural data elements across the Services Viewpoint to the Operational Viewpoint and Capability Viewpoint can be exemplified as services are procured and fielded to support the operations and capabilities of organizations. The structural and behavioral models in the OVs and SvcVs allow architects and stakeholders to quickly ascertain which functions are carried out by humans and which by Services for each alternative specification and so carry out trade analysis based on risk, cost, reliability, etc.” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]

394609711

**Views**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

These resources support the operational activities and facilitate the exchange of information. The relationship between architectural data elements across the Services Viewpoint to the Operational Viewpoint and Capability Viewpoint can be exemplified as services are procured and fielded to support the operations and capabilities of organizations. The structural and behavioral models in the OVs and SvcVs allow architects and stakeholders to quickly ascertain which functions are carried out by humans and which by Services for each alternative specification and so carry out trade analysis based on risk, cost, reliability, etc.” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d17dbc01-880e-42a0-a24d-4eb8b80deb66"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">394609711</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Views</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="c9c73c39-ed1b-4d6e-b5f3-011a350b97f9" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227157780 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01070: ServicesBToSSync 1

- page_id: `227157780`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227157780/ServicesBToSSync+1
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-04-26T07:47:11.549+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Validation rules > Correctness validation rules
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Abbreviation**

ServicesBToSSync[1]

**Description**

No compatible Service Interface. Thisvalidation rule checks whether Service Ports and Service Interfaces are compatible with flows between the Service Roles. The missing Ports and Interfaces can be handled automatically using solvers.

**Severity**

warning****

**Constrained Element**

Diagram****

**Solvers**

- Create Service Port - creates a new Service Port typed by a compatible Service Interface.
- Choose Compatible ServiceInterface - changes an existing Service Port type to a compatible Service Interface.
- Add Missing Flow Property - adds a new Flow Property to a Service Interface that types a Service Port.
- Reverse Direction of Service Port - changes the Service Port direction, thus changing the Flow Property direction of a Service Interface that types a Service Port.

**Example**

###### **[IMAGE alt='' src='']**The Service Port*iPayment*is created using Behavior to Structure Synchronization solver.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227157782 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01071: ServicesBToSSync 2

- page_id: `227157782`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227157782/ServicesBToSSync+2
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-04-26T07:59:20.924+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Validation rules > Correctness validation rules
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Abbreviation**

ServicesBToSSync[2]

**Description**

No compatible ServiceConnector. The second validation rule checks whether a Service Connector exists between the two compatible Service Ports owned by the Asset Roles. The missing Service Connectors can be handled automatically using solvers.

**Severity**

warning****

**Constrained Element**

Diagram****

**Solvers**

**Create Service Connector** - creates a missing Service Connector between the two compatible Service Ports.

**Example**

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

###### Creating a Service Connector that is missing in the Service Internal Connectivity Diagram.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The missing Service Connectors can be handled automatically using solvers.</span></p><p><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Severity</strong></p><p>warning<strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>Constrained Element</strong></p><p>Diagram<strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>Solvers</strong></p><p><strong>Create Service Connector</strong> - creates a missing Service Connector between the two compatible Service Ports. </p><p><strong>Example</strong></p><p><strong><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="UAF_behavior_to_structure_services_connectors_validation_rule_process.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="ServicesBToSSync 2" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Creating a Service Connector that is missing in the Service Internal Connectivity Diagram.</h6>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227157784 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01072: ServicesBToSSync 3

- page_id: `227157784`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227157784/ServicesBToSSync+3
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-08T14:06:24.922+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Validation rules > Correctness validation rules
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Abbreviation**

ServicesBToSSync[3]

**Description**

No compatible Service Exchange realization. The third validation rule checks realized Service Exchanges in Activities and help to ensure the correct Service Exchanges are realized on Service Connectors that connect Service Ports typed by compatible Service Interface.Themissing Exchanges can be handled automatically using solvers.

**Severity**

warning****

**Constrained Element**

Diagram****

**Solvers**

- Realize Missing Service Exchange - r ealizes existing compatible Service Exchange found in the behavior model on the Service Connector .
- Open Service Exchange Manager - opens the Service Exchange Manager dialog.
- Change Exchange Realization Compatibility Checking - opens the Project Options dialog to turn on/off the Exchange realization checking.

**Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Realizing a missing Operational Exchange on the Connector in the Operational Internal Connectivity Diagram.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Abbreviation</strong></p><p>ServicesBToSSync[3]</p><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">No compatible Service Exchange realization. The third validation rule checks realized Service Exchanges in Activities and help to ensure the correct Service Exchanges are realized on Service Connectors that connect Service Ports typed by compatible Service Interface.<span> </span></span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span> </span>missing Exchanges can be handled automatically using solvers.</span></p><p><strong>Severity</strong></p><p>warning<strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>Constrained Element</strong></p><p>Diagram<strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>Solvers</strong></p><ul><li><strong>Realize Missing Service Exchange</strong> - <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">r</span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">ealizes existing compatible Service Exchange found in the behavior model on the Service Connector</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">.</span></li><li><strong>Open Service Exchange Manager</strong> - opens the <strong>Service Exchange</strong><strong> Manager</strong> dialog.</li><li><strong>Change Exchange Realization Compatibility Checking</strong> - <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">opens the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Project Options</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span> </span>dialog to turn on/off the Exchange realization checking. </span> <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span></li></ul><p><strong>Example</strong></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="UAF_behavior_to_structure_services_exchanges_validation_rule_process.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="ServicesBToSSync 3" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="5d5b7f9e-ce01-4e9c-a49f-30f4811c5bec">Realizing a missing Operational Exchange on the Connector in the Operational Internal Connectivity Diagram.</ac:inline-comment-marker></h6>

- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227157334/Setting+up+Automatic+Population+of+Measurement+Sets
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-04-17T07:24:55.089+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures > Measurements > Managing Measurement Sets and Actual Measurements
- labels: ['set-up-automatic-population-of-measurement-sets']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can set up the automatic measurementSet population each time creating the Instance of the associated type. 
 
To set up the automatic Measurement Set population

1. Create a Measurement Set.
2. Add measurements for the created Measurement Set.
3. Apply « DoDAF Properties» stereotype to the created Measurement Set.
4. Open the Measurement Set Specification window and make sure the Expert properties mode is selected.
5. In the Tags property group, select the appliesFor and click the Create Value button.
6. Select the element stereotype (e.g. Operational Performer in the example below) as the appliesFor tag value. [ATTACHMENT filename='exchange_element_as_the appliedFor_tag_value.png']
7. Save and reload the project.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

After the automatic Measurements Set population is set up, you can apply Actual Measurements via the Specification window. To learn more, see the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying Actual Measurements' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] page.

If you choose to add a measurable element as an**appliesFor** tag value, the Measurement Set property is applied automatically.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can set up the automatic measurementSet population each time creating the Instance of the associated type. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" />To set up the automatic Measurement Set population</p><hr /><ol><li>Create a Measurement Set.</li><li>Add measurements for the created Measurement Set.<span style="color: rgb(124,127,147);"> </span></li><li>Apply «<em>DoDAF</em> <em>Properties»</em> stereotype to the created Measurement Set.</li><li>Open the Measurement Set Specification window and make sure the <strong>Expert </strong>properties mode is selected.</li><li>In the <strong>Tags</strong> property group, select the appliesFor and click the <strong>Create</strong> <strong>Value</strong> button.</li><li>Select the element stereotype (e.g. Operational Performer in the example below) as the <strong>appliesFor</strong> tag value.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="exchange_element_as_the appliedFor_tag_value.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Setting up Automatic Population of Measurement Sets" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><span style="color: rgb(124,127,147);"> </span>Save and reload the project.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="operational_performer_as_applyFor.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Setting up Automatic Population of Measurement Sets" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8fab1531-491e-4239-8f55-01cc7734c411"><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a3222beb-7b96-4f2b-8ef2-dc1cc2098762"><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">After the automatic Measurements Set population is set up, you can apply Actual Measurements via the Specification window. To learn more, see the </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Applying Actual Measurements" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link> page.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target">If you choose to add a measurable element as an<strong> appliesFor</strong> tag value, the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="840b7820-e817-4e96-84bf-e0d808cf798b">Measurement Set</ac:inline-comment-marker> property is applied automatically. </p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="measurable_element.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Setting up Automatic Population of Measurement Sets" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227157357 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01075: Show default documentation for UAF packages

- page_id: `227157357`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227157357/Show+default+documentation+for+UAF+packages
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-04-08T14:35:01.534+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To show the default documentation when creating a new project

1. In the New Project dialog, choose the required project template.
2. In the UAF Project Options area, set the Show default documentation for UAF Packages option to true. If you set the option to false, the new UAF Packages will be created without the default documentation. [ATTACHMENT filename='default_package_documentation_new_project.png']

The default documentation is initially displayed in the**Select Framework Views**dialog, which is shown when creatinga new project. You canchoose to show the defaultdocumentationin:

- The viewpoint package element's Specification window, Documentation/Comment property
- The selected viewpoint package element's Documentation tab, which is located in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To show the default documentation for the existing projects

1. In the modeling tool main menu, click Option > Project . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] dialog opens.
2. Go to General > UAF .
3. Find the Show default documentation for UAF Packages project option and set it to true (if it was set to false) .

If you set the option to true, a message appears where you can choose if the default Package documentation will be shown for the existing UAF Packages. In the message:

- Click Yes to show the default Documentation for existing UAF Packages if they have no custom documentation.
- Click No to keep the existing UAF Packages documentation unchanged.

- The “Show default documentation for UAF Packages” option will not affect read-only elements and elements locked by other users.
- Turning the “Show default documentation for UAF Packages” option off will NOT affect the existing UAF Packages' documentation to prevent data loss.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To show the default documentation when creating a new project</p><hr /><ol><li>In the New Project dialog, choose the required project template.</li><li>In the UAF Project Options area, set the <strong>Show default documentation for UAF Packages </strong>option to true.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9e04c890-ad17-4c94-834a-f93652f0c1ec"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you set the option to false, the new UAF Packages will be created without the default documentation.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="default_package_documentation_new_project.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Show default documentation for UAF packages" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="60c8c3c5-65f1-44c9-8faf-6bfb670831f2">The default documentation is initially displayed in the </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="60c8c3c5-65f1-44c9-8faf-6bfb670831f2">Select Framework Views</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="60c8c3c5-65f1-44c9-8faf-6bfb670831f2"> dialog, which is shown when creating </ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="35759eac-5818-45a9-8c26-bca136cb843e"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="60c8c3c5-65f1-44c9-8faf-6bfb670831f2">a new project. Y</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="60c8c3c5-65f1-44c9-8faf-6bfb670831f2">ou can </ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3c747802-afc5-4600-88cc-6e00906346a2"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="60c8c3c5-65f1-44c9-8faf-6bfb670831f2">choose to show the default</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="60c8c3c5-65f1-44c9-8faf-6bfb670831f2"> </ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c8eecc38-fbc3-4933-a424-289cde9e37e2"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="60c8c3c5-65f1-44c9-8faf-6bfb670831f2">documentation</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="60c8c3c5-65f1-44c9-8faf-6bfb670831f2"> in:</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><ul><li>The viewpoint package element's Specification window, Documentation/Comment property</li><li>The selected viewpoint package element's Documentation tab, which is located in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Model Browser" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link>.</li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="default_package_documentation.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Show default documentation for UAF packages" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a68132b6-e7e8-4e66-8cf9-d907c1ae46c7">T</ac:inline-comment-marker>o show the default documentation for the existing projects</p><hr /><ol><li>In the modeling tool main menu, click <strong>Option </strong>&gt; <strong>Project</strong>. The <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Setting project options" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Project Options]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Go to <strong>General </strong>&gt; <strong>UAF</strong>.</li><li>Find the <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Show default documentation for UAF Packages </strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">project option and set it to true (if it was set to false)</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.</span></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>If you set the option to true, a message appears where you can choose if the default Package documentation will be shown for the existing UAF Packages. In the message:</p><ul><li>Click <strong>Yes</strong> to show the default Documentation for existing UAF Packages if they have no custom documentation.</li><li>Click <strong>No</strong> to keep the existing UAF Packages documentation unchanged.</li></ul><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1a5b78b2-fee4-468d-88c3-7f3de0e29589"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The “Show default documentation for UAF Packages” option will not affect read-only elements and elements locked by other users.</li><li>Turning the “Show default documentation for UAF Packages” option off will NOT affect the existing UAF Packages' documentation to prevent data loss.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227157361 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01076: Show diagram abbreviation in Containment tree

- page_id: `227157361`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227157361/Show+diagram+abbreviation+in+Containment+tree
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-04-08T14:32:08.764+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can choose whether or not to display the diagram abbreviations in the Containment tree. A [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] controls this functionality.

To show the diagram abbreviations in the Containment tree

1. In the modeling tool main menu, click Options > Project .
2. In the Project Options dialog, go to General > Browser .
3. Set the Show Diagram Abbreviations option to true.
4. Click OK .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

This Project option is by default true for the new projects:

- Blank UAF Project
- UAF Project
- UAF Project Template with Process Guide
- EA Project Template with Process Guide
- EA with BPMN Project

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can choose whether or not to display the diagram abbreviations in the Containment tree. A <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Setting project options" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Project option]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> controls this functionality.</p><p><br /></p><p>To show the diagram abbreviations in the Containment tree</p><hr /><ol><li>In the modeling tool main menu, click <strong>Options </strong>&gt; <strong>Project</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog, go to <strong>General </strong>&gt; <strong>Browser</strong>.</li><li>Set the <strong>Show Diagram Abbreviations</strong> option to true.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="diagram_abbreviations_sample.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Show diagram abbreviation in Containment tree" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fd26eaec-a50c-4dc7-b64d-093141bbe4d5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This Project option is by default true for the new projects: </p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li>Blank UAF Project</li><li>UAF Project</li><li>UAF Project Template with Process Guide</li><li>EA Project Template with Process Guide</li><li>EA with BPMN Project</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227155682 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01078: SOV-3 Working with Services to Service Contracts Mapping matrix

- page_id: `227155682`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227155682/SOV-3+Working+with+Services+to+Service+Contracts+Mapping+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-10-21T08:58:19.770+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Service Oriented Viewpoint > MODAF. SOV-3 Capability to Service Mapping
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **SOV-3 Services to Service Contracts Mapping** matrix depicts mapping between the Services or their specializations and the Service Contracts they govern.

The Rows of this matrix are Services or their specializations,and the Columns are Service Contracts.

To create a SOV-3 Services to Service Contracts Mapping Matrix

1. Specify**Row Scope** (Services or their specializations);
2. Specify**Column Scope** (Service Contracts);
3. Click[IMAGE alt='' src=''].

Services or their specializations map to Service Contracts using a Governed By relationship.

To mapServices or their specializations to Service Contracts

1. Double-click an empty intersection.

To remove the relation

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>SOV-3 Services to Service Contracts Mapping</strong> matrix depicts mapping between the Services or their specializations and the Service Contracts they govern.</p><p><span>The Rows of this matrix are Services or their specializations,</span><span> and the Columns are Service Contracts</span><span>.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><span>To create a SOV-3 Services to Service Contracts Mapping Matrix</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Specify</span><strong> Row Scope</strong> (Services or their specializations);</span></li><li><span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Specify </span><strong>Column Scope</strong> (Service Contracts);</span></li><li><span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click </span><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper" style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="SOV-3 Working with Services to Service Contracts Mapping matrix" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></span>.</span></li></ol><p> Services <span>or their specializations</span> map to Service Contracts using a Governed By relationship.</p><p><br /></p><p>To map<span> Services or their specializations to Service Contracts </span></p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click an empty intersection.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To remove the relation</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

## PAGE 01079: SOV-4a Service Parametric

- page_id: `227155687`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227155687/SOV-4a+Service+Parametric
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-02T10:30:28.119+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Service Oriented Viewpoint > MODAF. SOV-4a Service Constraints
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

374424572

374424575

374424574

**Description**

A Parametric diagram includes the usage of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Block' space='SYSMLP2024xR3'] to constrain the properties of another Service Specification. It contains [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Property' space='SYSMLP2024xR3'] and constraint parameters as well as other properties from within that internal block context. All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a constraint parameter or contain a property that is bound to a constraint parameter (through any number of containment levels). A constraint block generally contain many constraints, each of them containing many constraint parameters.

374424571

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Parametric diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

It contains <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR3" ri:content-title="Constraint Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[constraint properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and constraint parameters as well as other properties from within that internal block context. All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a constraint parameter or contain a property that is bound to a constraint parameter (through any number of containment levels). A constraint block generally contain many constraints, each of them containing many constraint parameters.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5352cfdc-efd8-4a4e-9386-399ed9cb8bc0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">374424571</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Parametric diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227156118 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01080: Sr Service Roadmap

- page_id: `227156118`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227156118/Sr+Service+Roadmap
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:19:41.009+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Service viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1974596998

1974597000

1974596999

**Description**

The Sr Services Roadmap domain shows the service changes over time. It provides an overview of how a service changes over time. It shows the combination of several services mapped against a timeline. The Sr Services Roadmap domain presents a whole lifecycle view of resources (services), describing how it changes over time. It shows the structure of several resources mapped against a timeline.

In addition, this model is useful in support of net-centric (service-oriented) implementation of services. This model can present a timeline of services evolve or are replaced over time, including services that are internal and external to the scope of the architecture.

The intended usage of the Sr Services Roadmap domain includes:

- Development of incremental acquisition strategy.
- Planning technology insertion.

**Implementation**

The Sr Services Roadmap domain is represented by a Services Roadmap diagram which is realized as a time based diagram.

1974596997

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Project' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Project Milestone' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Milestone Dependency' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Versioned Element' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Version Of Configuration' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Version Succession' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Whole Life Configuration' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Whole Life Configuration Kind' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

**Related procedures**

It provides an overview of how a service changes over time. It shows the combination of several services mapped against a timeline. The Sr Services Roadmap domain presents a whole lifecycle view of resources (services), describing how it changes over time. It shows the structure of several resources mapped against a timeline.</p><p>In addition, this model is useful in support of net-centric (service-oriented) implementation of services. This model can present a timeline of services evolve or are replaced over time, including services that are internal and external to the scope of the architecture.</p><p>The intended usage of the Sr ### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A ratified and peer-reviewed specification that is used to guide or constrain the architecture. A Standard may be applied to any element in the architecture.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Class

**Description**

The Standards Parameters (Sd-Pm) domain depicts the measurements of resources.

The intended usage of the Standards Parameters (Sd-Pm) domain includes:

- Definition of performance characteristics and measurements.
- Identification of non-functional requirements.

**Implementation**

The Standards Parameters (Sd-Pm) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Typical Measurements table' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with hierarchical Typical Measurements table' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Actual Measurements table' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with hierarchical Actual Measurements table' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- Actual Measurements (.xlsx)

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227156911 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01086: Standards Roadmap

- page_id: `227156911`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227156911/Standards+Roadmap
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:12:47.411+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Standards
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

922198773

922198775

922198774

**Description**

The Standards Roadmap (Sd-Rm) domain shows the expected changes in technology-related standards and conventions, operational standards, or business standards and conventions. It defines the underlying current and expected standards. Expected standards are those that can be reasonably forecast given the current state of technology, and expected improvements/trends.

The Standards Roadmap (Sd-Rm) domain is a detailed description of emerging standards relevant to the systems, operational, and business activities covered by the Architectural Description. The forecast should be tailored to focus on areas that are related to the purpose for which a given Architectural Description is being built, and should identify issues that affect the architecture. The Standards Roadmap (Sd-Rm) domain complements and expands on the Standards Taxonomy and Standards Traceability views and should be used when more than one emerging standard time-period is applicable to the architecture.

One of the prime purposes of this model is to identify critical technology standards, their fragility, and the impact of these standards on the future development and maintainability of the architecture and its constituent elements.

The intended usage of the The Standards Roadmap (Sd-Rm) domain includes:

- Forecasting future changes in standards (informing project strategy).

**Implementation**

The Standards Roadmap (Sd-Rm) domain is implemented by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Standards Roadmap table' space='UAF12P2024xR3'].

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

It defines the underlying current and expected standards. Expected standards are those that can be reasonably forecast given the current state of technology, and expected improvements/trends.</p><p>The Standards Roadmap (Sd-Rm) domain is a detailed description of emerging standards relevant to the systems, operational, and business activities covered by the Architectural Description. The forecast should be tailored to focus on areas that are related to the purpose for which a given Architectural Description is being built, and should identify issues that affect the architecture. The Standards Roadmap (Sd-Rm) domain complements and expands on the Standards Taxonomy and Standards Traceability views and should be used when more than one emerging standard time-period is applicable to the architecture.</p><p>One of the prime purposes of this model is to identify critical technology standards, their fragility, and the impact of these standards on the 922668140

922668141

**Description**

The Standards Taxonomy (Pj-Tx) domain shows technical and non-technical standards, guidance and policy applicable to the architecture. It shows the taxonomy of types of technical, operational, and business standards, guidance and policy applicable to the architecture.

The intended usage of the Standards Taxonomy (Pj-Tx) domain includes:

- Application of standards (informing project strategy).
- Standards compliance.

**Implementation**

The Standards Taxonomy (Pj-Tx) domain is represented by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Standards Taxonomy diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] diagram.

includes:</p><ul><li>Application of standards (informing project strategy).</li><li>Standards compliance.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The Standards Taxonomy (Pj-Tx) domain is <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227156944 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01089: Standards Traceability

- page_id: `227156944`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227156944/Standards+Traceability
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:51:32.029+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Standards
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

922795287

922795290

922795289

**Description**

The Standards Traceability (Sd-Tr) domain shows the traceability between capabilities and projects that deliver them. It depicts the mapping of projects to capabilities and thus identifies the transformation of a capability(ies) into a purposeful implementation via projects.

The intended usage of the Standards Traceability (Sd-Tr) domain includes:

- Application of standards (informing project strategy).
- Standards compliance.

**Implementation**

The Standards Traceability (Sd-Tr) domain is represented by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Standards Traceability table' space='UAF12P2024xR3']Standards Traceability table.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Standards Traceability table

922795286

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Standard' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UAF Element' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227155585 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01090: Standards viewpoint

- page_id: `227155585`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227155585/Standards+viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:24:11.994+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0
- labels: ['standards-viewpoint']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

408621248

408621250

408621249

“The DoDAF-described Models within the Standards Viewpoint is the set of rules governing the arrangement, interaction, and interdependence of parts or elements of the Architectural Description. These sets of rules can be captured at the enterprise level and applied to each solution, while each solution’s architectural description depicts only those rules pertinent to architecture described. Its purpose is to ensure that a solution satisfies a specified set of operational or capability requirements. The Standards Models capture the doctrinal, operational, business, technical, or industry implementation guidelines upon which engineering specifications are based, common building blocks are established, and solutions are developed. It includes a collection of the doctrinal, operational, business, technical, or industry standards, implementation conventions, standards options, rules, and criteria that can be organized into profiles that govern solution elements for a given architecture. Current DoD guidance requires the Technical Standards portions of models be produced from DISR to determine the minimum set of standards and guidelines for the acquisition of all DoD systems that produce, use, or exchange information.” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]

408621246

**Views**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

These sets of rules can be captured at the enterprise level and applied to each solution, while each solution’s architectural description depicts only those rules pertinent to architecture described. Its purpose is to ensure that a solution satisfies a specified set of operational or capability requirements. The Standards Models capture the doctrinal, operational, business, technical, or industry implementation guidelines upon which engineering specifications are based, common building blocks are established, and solutions are developed. It includes a collection of the doctrinal, operational, business, technical, or industry standards, implementation conventions, standards options, rules, and criteria that can be organized into profiles that govern solution elements for a given architecture. Current DoD guidance requires the Technical Standards portions of models be produced from DISR to determine the minimum set of standards and guidelines for the acquisition of all DoD systems that produce, use, or exchange information.” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a655e724-7701-447a-8367-9ecf6b667a06"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">408621246</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Views</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="c46668b9-6e20-4c86-b041-dcbcb4118b67" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
