<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227155586 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01092: StdV-1 Standards Profile

- page_id: `227155586`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227155586/StdV-1+Standards+Profile
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-15T11:44:57.153+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Standards viewpoint
- labels: ['stdv-1-standards-profile', 'stdv-1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

408664615

408664617

408664616

**Description**

The StdV-1 defines the technical, operational, and business standards, guidance, and policy applicable to the architecture being described. As well as identifying applicable technical standards, the DoDAF V2.0 StdV-1 also documents the policies and standards that apply to the operational or business context. The DISR is an architecture resource for technical standards that can be used in the generation of the StdV-1 and StdV-2 Standards Forecast.

In most cases, building a Standards Profile consists of identifying and listing the applicable portions of existing and emerging documentation. A StdV-1 should identify both existing guidelines, as well as any areas lacking guidance. As with other models, each profile is assigned a specific timescale (e.g., “As-Is”, “To-Be”, or transitional). Linking the profile to a defined timescale enables the profile to consider both emerging technologies and any current technical standards that are expected to be updated or become obsolete. If more than one emerging standard time-period is applicable to an architecture, then a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='StdV-2 Standards Forecast' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] should be completed as well as a StdV-1.

The intended usage of the StdV-1 includes:

- Application of standards (informing project strategy).
- Standards compliance.

**Implementation**

StdV-1 can be represented by a StdV-1 table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The Standards cited are referenced as relationships to the systems, services, system functions, service functions, system data, service data, hardware/software items or communication protocols, where applicable, in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR3'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-1 Services Context Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-2 Services Resource Flow Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='DIV-2 Logical Data Model' space='UAF12P2024xR3'], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='DIV-3 Physical Data Model' space='UAF12P2024xR3'].

408664614

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Standard' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Protocol' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Protocol Stack' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating StdV-1 table' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227155590 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01093: StdV-2 Standards Forecast

- page_id: `227155590`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227155590/StdV-2+Standards+Forecast
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-15T11:46:16.180+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Standards viewpoint
- labels: ['stdv-2-standards-forecast', 'stdv-2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

408737149

408737151

408737150

**Description**

The StdV-2 contains expected changes in technology related standards, operational standards, or business standards and conventions, which are documented in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='StdV-1 Standards Profile' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] model. The forecast for evolutionary changes in the standards need to be correlated against the time periods mentioned in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-8 Systems Evolution Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3'].

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-8 Systems Evolution Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-9 Systems Technology and Skills Forecast' space='UAF12P2024xR3'], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-9 Services Technology and Skills Forecast' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] models.

A StdV-2 is a detailed description of emerging standards relevant to the systems, operational, and business activities covered by the Architectural Description. The forecast should be tailored to focus on areas that are related to the purpose for which a given Architectural Description is being built, and should identify issues that affect the architecture. A StdV-2 complements and expands on the StdV-1Standards Profile model and should be used when more than one emerging standard time-period is applicable to the architecture.

One of the prime purposes of this model is to identify critical technology standards, their fragility, and the impact of these standards on the future development and maintainability of the architecture and its constituent elements.

The intended usage of the StdV-2 includes:

- Forecasting future changes in standards (informing project strategy).

**Implementation**

The StdV-2 can be represented using a StdV-2 table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

StdV-2 delineates the standards that potentially impact the relevant system and service elements (from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR3'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-1 Services Context Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3'],[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-2 Services Resource Flow Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3'],[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR3'], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='DIV-2 Logical Data Model' space='UAF12P2024xR3']) and relates them to the time periods that are listed in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-8 Systems Evolution Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-8 Services Evolution Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-9 Systems Technology and Skills Forecast' space='UAF12P2024xR3'], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-9 Services Technology and Skills Forecast' space='UAF12P2024xR3'].

408737148

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Standard' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Protocol' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Forecast' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Protocol Stack' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating StdV-2 table' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5dab08df-45ed-463f-9891-33ad7dc25deb"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">408737149</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="508d5c6e-be40-4292-b0a8-f37df7f0298f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">408737151</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8a3450f7-473b-46f2-95f8-cea894e70d93"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">408737150</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The StdV-2 contains expected changes in technology related standards, operational standards, or business standards and conventions, which are documented in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="StdV-1 Standards Profile" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[StdV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> model. The forecast for evolutionary changes in the standards need to be correlated against the time periods mentioned in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-8 Systems Evolution Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link>.</p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-8 Systems Evolution Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SvcV-8 Services Evolution Description]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-9 Systems Technology and Skills Forecast" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-9 Systems Technology & Skills Forecast]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-9 Services Technology and Skills Forecast" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SvcV-9 Services Technology & Skills Forecast]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> models.</p><p>A StdV-2 is a detailed description of emerging standards relevant to the systems, operational, and business activities covered by the Architectural Description. The forecast should be tailored to focus on areas that are related to the purpose for which a given Architectural Description is being built, and should identify issues that affect the architecture. A StdV-2 complements and expands on the StdV-1Standards Profile model and should be used when more than one emerging standard time-period is applicable to the architecture.</p><p>One of the prime purposes of this model is to identify critical technology standards, their fragility, and the impact of these standards on the future development and maintainability of the architecture and its constituent elements.</p><p>The intended usage of the StdV-2 includes:</p><ul><li>Forecasting future changes in standards (informing project strategy).</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The StdV-2 can be represented using a StdV-2 table.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="StdV-2 Standards Forecast" ac:alt="StdV-2 Standards Forecast"><ri:attachment ri:filename="TV-2_StdV-2.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="StdV-2 Standards Forecast" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>StdV-2 delineates the standards that potentially impact the relevant system and service elements (from <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-4 Systems Functionality Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-1 Services Context Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link>,<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-2 Services Resource Flow Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ SvcV-2 Services Resource Flow Description]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>,<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="DIV-2 Logical Data Model" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link>) and relates them to the time periods that are listed in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-8 Systems Evolution Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-8 Services Evolution Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-9 Systems Technology and Skills Forecast" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-9 Systems Technology & Skills Forecast]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-9 Services Technology and Skills Forecast" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SvcV-9 Services Technology & Skills Forecast models]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="acf44592-71cd-48ac-a7fd-0d8fc1c99390"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">408737148</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Standard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Protocol" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Forecast" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Protocol Stack" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating StdV-2 table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227156261 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01094: Strategic Connectivity

- page_id: `227156261`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227156261/Strategic+Connectivity
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:25:07.486+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Strategy
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

922916381

922916383

922916382

**Description**

The Strategic Connectivity (St-Cn) diagram depicts relationships between capabilities in which one capability cannot succeed without some form of assistance from another capability. The groupings of capabilities are logical, and the purpose of the groupings is to guide enterprise management. In particular, the dependencies and groupings may suggest specific interactions between acquisition projects to achieve the overall capability.

The intended usage of the Strategic Connectivity (St-Cn) includes:

- Identification of capability dependencies.
- Capability management (impact analysis for options, disposal etc.).

**Implementation**

The Strategic Connectivity (St-Cn) view is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Connectivity matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] .

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Strategic Connectivity diagram

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Strategic Connectivity matrix

## PAGE 01096: Strategic Constraints

- page_id: `227156275`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`

- version_number: 1

- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Strategy
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1102244620

1102244623

1102244622

**Description**

The Strategic Constraints (St-Ct) details the measurements that set performance requirements constraining capabilities.

**Implementation**

The Strategic Constraints (St-Ct) view is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Constraints Definition diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] diagram based on a UML Class diagram.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Constraints table' space='UAF12P2024xR3'].

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of a Strategic Constraint Definition diagram.

**Constraint owner**

You can choose where all the constraints will be stored through the **Project Options**.

To select a constraint owner

1. In the main menu, click Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. Go to General > UAF , and in the Default Constraint Owners property group, choose a needed owner.

1102244619

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227157115 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01097: Strategic Drivers Table Creation Wizard

- page_id: `227157115`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227157115/Strategic+Drivers+Table+Creation+Wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-05-06T13:19:49.203+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Dialogs
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To open a Strategic Drivers Table Creation wizard, do one of the following

- Right-click the Strategic Motivation Package and select Create Diagram > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Drivers Table' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] .
- In the main toolbar, click Create Diagram and select to create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Drivers Table' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] .

The Strategic Drivers Table Creation Wizard consists of two steps:

truenone

##### Name and owning package specification

In this step, you can specify the diagram name and select or create the owning package. If you want to create a new owning package, click **Creation Mode**and then, click the **Create Owner** button. Also, you can clone an existing package by clicking the**Clone**button.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Drivers selection

In this step, you can choose the existing Drivers from the list, or create new Drivers.

To create new Drivers

1. Click the Creation Mode button.
2. Select an owner for new Drivers.
3. Click Create .
4. Enter specifications for the new Driver.
5. Click Close . A new Driver is added to the Selected elements list.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

| Button | Description |
| --- | --- |
|  | Selects a Driver.Alternatively, double-click the Driver. |
|  | Removes a Driver. |
|  | Removes all Drivers. |

If you do not wish to use this wizard next time, clear the **Show the wizard next time, when I create Strategic Drivers Table** check box.

To restore the usage of the**Strategic Drivers Table Creation Wizard**

1. Select Options > Environment .
2. In the Environment Options dialog select the UAF options group.
3. In the General properties, set the Show Strategic Drivers Creation Wizard property value to true .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To open a Strategic Drivers Table Creation wizard, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>Right-click the Strategic Motivation Package and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong><span> </span>&gt; <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Drivers Table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Strategic Drivers Table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>.</li><li>In the main toolbar, click <strong>Create Diagram</strong> and select to create a <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Drivers Table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Strategic Drivers Table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>The Strategic Drivers Table Creation Wizard consists of two steps:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b089103e-7443-47e5-98eb-da90b1abb62d"><ac:parameter ac:name="outline">true</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="style">none</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h3>Name and owning package specification</h3><p>In this step, you can specify the diagram name and select or create the owning package. If you want to create a new owning package, click <strong>Creation Mode</strong><span> and then, click the <strong>Create Owner</strong> button</span>. Also, you can clone an existing package by clicking the<span> </span><strong>Clone</strong><span> </span>button.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="strategic_drivers_table_creation_wizard_I.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Strategic Drivers Table Creation Wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3>Drivers selection</h3><p>In this step, you can choose the existing Drivers from the list, or create new Drivers.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create new Drivers</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the <strong>Creation Mode</strong> button.</li><li>Select an owner for new Drivers.</li><li>Click <strong>Create</strong>.</li><li>Enter specifications for the new Driver.</li><li>Click <strong>Close</strong>. A new Driver is added to the <strong>Selected elements</strong> list.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="strategic_drivers_table_creation_wizard_II.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Strategic Drivers Table Creation Wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 25.2331%;"><colgroup><col options group.</li><li>In the General properties, set the <strong style="text-align: left;">Show Strategic Drivers Creation Wizard</strong> property value to<em> true</em>.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227158028 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01098: Strategic Exchange

- page_id: `227158028`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227158028/Strategic+Exchange
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T13:27:17.184+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

Asserts that a flow can exist between Actual Strategic Phases (i.e. flows of information, people, materiel, or energy).

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extensions**

Information Flow

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">Asserts that a flow can exist between Actual Strategic Phases (i.e. flows of information, people, materiel, or energy).</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Architecture Framework</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</span></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Extensions</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">Information Flow</p>

**Description**

The **Strategic Information** (St-If) view addresses information that can be considered to be an enterprise strategic asset that can influence the achievement of enterprise goals. **Implementation**

The **Strategic Information** (St-If) is represented by the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Information diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3'].

## PAGE 01101: Strategic Motivation

- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`

- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Strategy
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The **Strategic Motivation** (St-Mv) view identifies and defines the drivers, challenges, and opportunities that are applicable to the architecture. It defines the desired outcomes, goals, and objectives that are motivated by the drivers, and the opportunities that enable the goals and objectives.

**Implementation**

The **Strategic Motivation** (St-Mv) is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Motivation diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Motivation Table' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Drivers Table' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Traceability relation maps' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] .

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing traceability and impact analysis' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

**Description**

The Strategic Parameters (St-Pm) view depicts the measures (metrics) of strategic elements.

The intended usage of the Strategic Parameters (St-Pm) view includes:

- Definition of performance characteristics and measures (metrics).
- Identification of non-functional requirements.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227156286 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01105: Strategic Roadmap

- page_id: `227156286`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227156286/Strategic+Roadmap
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:21:50.391+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Strategy
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

922995112

922995115

922995114

**Description**

The Strategic Roadmap (St-Rm) view represents the fulfillment of capability requirements and planned achievement of capability at different points in time or during specific periods of time.

The **Strategic Deployment** table shows the planned capability deployment for an actual resource and the responsible actual organization.

**Implementation**

The Strategic Roadmap (St-Rm) view is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Actual Deployment diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] . This diagram addresses the deployment of capabilities to actual organizations over time. This model shows the planned capability deployment and interconnection for a particular Capability Phase. The Strategic Deployment diagram can also be used to support the capability management process and, in particular, assist the planning of fielding. The intended usage of the Strategic Deployment diagram includes:
  - Fielding planning.
  - Capability integration planning.
  - Capability options analysis.
  - Capability redundancy/overlap/gap analysis.
  - Identification of deployment level shortfalls.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Phasing diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] . That diagram is used to plan achievement of capabilities at different points in time or during specific periods of time. The Strategic Phasing diagram supports the capability audit processes and similar processes used across the different COIs by providing a method to identify gaps or duplication in capability provision. This diagram indicates capability increments, which should be associated with delivery milestones within acquisition projects (when the increments are associated with capability deliveries). The intended usage of the Strategic Phasing diagram includes:
  - Capability planning (capability phasing).
  - Capability integration planning.
  - Capability gap analysis.
- Strategic Deployment Excel Spreadsheet (.xlsx).

**Samples**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Strategic Deployment diagram

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Strategic Phasing diagram

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of Actual Strategic Phases Gantt Chart

922995111

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1e7ca571-d38a-41e7-83fb-b8e40a784837"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">922995112</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="93da2f4d-c125-4217-8e3f-d3fb00842b4d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">922995115</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d56d04b2-2430-43db-b940-a9a7f3d74556"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">922995114</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The Strategic Roadmap (St-Rm) view represents the fulfillment of capability requirements and planned achievement of capability at different points in time or during specific periods of time.</p><p>The <strong>Strategic Deployment</strong> table shows the planned capability deployment for an actual resource and the responsible actual organization.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The Strategic Roadmap (St-Rm) view is represented by:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Actual Deployment diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Strategic Deployment diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. This diagram addresses the deployment of capabilities to actual organizations over time. This model shows the planned capability deployment and interconnection for a particular Capability Phase. The Strategic Deployment diagram can also be used to support the capability management process and, in particular, assist the planning of fielding.<br /><p>The intended usage of the Strategic Deployment diagram includes:</p><ul><li>Fielding planning.</li><li>Capability integration planning.</li><li>Capability options analysis.</li><li>Capability redundancy/overlap/gap analysis.</li><li>Identification of deployment level shortfalls.</li></ul><br /><br /></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Phasing diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Strategic Phasing diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. That diagram is used to plan achievement of capabilities at different points in time or during specific periods of time. The Strategic Phasing diagram supports the capability audit processes and similar processes used across the different COIs by providing a method to identify gaps or duplication in capability provision. This diagram indicates capability increments, which should be associated with delivery milestones within acquisition projects (when the increments are associated with capability deliveries).<br /><p>The intended usage of the Strategic Phasing diagram includes:</p><ul><li>Capability planning (capability phasing).</li><li>Capability integration planning.</li><li>Capability gap analysis.</li></ul></li><li>Strategic Deployment Excel Spreadsheet (.xlsx).</li></ul><p><strong>Samples</strong></p><p><ac:image ac:title="Strategic Deployment diagram " ac:alt="Strategic Deployment diagram "><ri:attachment ri:filename="strategic_roadmap.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Strategic Roadmap" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>An example of the Strategic Deployment diagram</h6><p><ac:image ac:title="Strategic Phasing diagram" ac:alt="Strategic Phasing diagram"><ri:attachment ri:filename="strategic_roadmap_phasing.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Strategic Roadmap" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>An example of the Strategic Phasing diagram</h6><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="actual_strategic_phases.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Strategic Roadmap" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>An example of Actual Strategic Phases Gantt Chart</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d2e45b3e-a11d-4dd0-b8e5-c5ca8b8a9b1c"><ac:parameter ## PAGE 01106: Strategic States

- page_id: `227156304`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1094426394

1094426396

1094426395

**Description**

The Strategic States (St-St) view shows the relationships between capability(ies) and desired effect(s) that implementation(s) of capability(ies) should achieve. It defines the different measurements required to define what the architecture is meant to achieve in quantitative measures.

**Implementation**

The Strategic States (St-St) view is represented by the Strategic States diagram based on a UML Class diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of a Strategic States diagram.

1094426392

## PAGE 01107: Strategic Structure

- page_id: `227156307`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227156307/Strategic+Structure
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:59:23.184+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Strategy
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

923196921

923196923

923196922

**Description**

The Strategic Structure (St-Sr) shows the composition of capabilities and how capabilities are decomposed from higher level ones as needed. This view addresses the enterprise concerns associated with the overall vision for transformational endeavors and thus defines the strategic context for a group of capabilities. The purpose of a Strategic Structure (St-Sr) view is to provide a strategic context for the capabilities described in the Architectural Description.

**Implementation**

The Strategic Structure (St-Sr) view is represented by a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3'].

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Strategic Structure diagram

923196920

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Enduring Task' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227156322 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01108: Strategic Taxonomy

- page_id: `227156322`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227156322/Strategic+Taxonomy
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:48:30.688+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Strategy
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

923322042

923322045

923322044

**Description**

The Strategic Taxonomy (St-Tx) describes the relationships between individual capabilities including composition, association, and generalization.

The Strategic Taxonomy (St-Tx) view specifies all the capabilities that are referenced throughout one or more architectures. In addition, it can be used as a source document for the development of high-level use cases and user requirements.

The intended usage of the Strategic Taxonomy (St-Tx) view includes:

- Identification of capability requirements.
- Capability planning (capability taxonomy).
- Codifying required capability elements.
- Capability audit.
- Capability gap analysis.
- Source for the derivation of cohesive sets of user requirements.
- Providing reference capabilities for architectures.

**Implementation**

The Strategic Taxonomy (St-Tx) view is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Taxonomy diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Taxonomy table' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] . This table can be used to create and describe major domain elements faster.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Strategic Taxonomy diagram

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Strategic Taxonomy table

923322041

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

**Related procedures**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0ee7673c-00ed-42a9-a528-c7700c9ea60b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">923322042</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="495d6385-8300-4fa0-bdfb-42d29a2b6457"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">923322045</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e87fec37-3671-45e8-8817-b6cfef619b9a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">923322044</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The Strategic Taxonomy (St-Tx) describes the relationships between individual capabilities including composition, association, and generalization.</p><p>The Strategic Taxonomy (St-Tx) view specifies all the capabilities that are referenced throughout one or more architectures. In addition, it can be used as a source document for the development of high-level use cases and user requirements.</p><p>The intended usage of the Strategic Taxonomy (St-Tx) view includes:</p><ul><li>Identification of capability requirements.</li><li>Capability planning (capability taxonomy).</li><li>Codifying required capability elements.</li><li>Capability audit.</li><li>Capability gap analysis.</li><li>Source for the derivation of cohesive sets of user requirements.</li><li>Providing reference capabilities for architectures.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The Strategic Taxonomy (St-Tx) view is represented by:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Taxonomy diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Strategic Taxonomy diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Taxonomy table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Strategic Taxonomy table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. This table can be used to create and describe major domain elements faster.</li></ul><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image ac:title="Strategic Taxonomy " ac:alt="Strategic Taxonomy "><ri:attachment ri:filename="strategic_taxonomy.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Strategic Taxonomy" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>An example of the Strategic Taxonomy diagram</h6><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="strategic_taxonomy_table.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Strategic Taxonomy" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>An example of the Strategic Taxonomy table</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b5f5c7ce-c4af-4364-ae42-8f55e64231bc"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">923322041</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Capability" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="aeb7c399-61f3-4689-8a99-e28123168ef4" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227156335 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01109: Strategic Traceability

- page_id: `227156335`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227156335/Strategic+Traceability
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-04-11T13:08:05.873+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Strategy
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Strategic Traceability (St-Tr) describes the mapping between the capabilities required by an Enterprise and the phasing constructs and association with relevant challenges and drivers. With a help of the Strategic Traceability (St-Tr) view specification, you can identify howthe required capabilities and the actual strategic phases that those actual strategic phases support are mapped.

**Implementation**

The Strategic Traceability (St-Tr) view specification is represented by an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Actual Strategic Phases to Capabilities Mapping matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] which is an editable Dependency Matrix. The Rows of this matrix are Actual Strategic Phases and the Columns are Capabilities.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Actual Strategic Phases to Capabilities Mapping matrix

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Strategic Phase' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Phases' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

**Related procedures**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227158031 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01111: Structural Part

- page_id: `227158031`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227158031/Structural+Part
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T13:26:50.519+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

Usage of an Enterprise Phase in the context of another Enterprise Phase. It asserts that one Enterprise Phase is a spatial part of another. Creates a whole-part relationship that represents the structure of the Enterprise Phase.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Property

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">Usage of an Enterprise Phase in the context of another Enterprise Phase. It asserts that one Enterprise Phase is a spatial part of another. Creates a whole-part relationship that represents the structure of the Enterprise Phase. </p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong><span class="fontstyle0">Architecture Framework</span></strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</span></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Extension</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">UML Property</p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227156252 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01116: Summary and Overview

- page_id: `227156252`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227156252/Summary+and+Overview
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:06:31.469+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

923507957

923507959

923507958

**Description**

The Summary & Overview viewpoint is for modeling the executive-level summary information in a consistent form. It provides executive-level summary information in a consistent form that allows quick reference and comparison between architectural descriptions.

The Summary and Overview Measurements (Pm-Me) domain depicts the measurements. The intended usage of The Summary and Overview Measurements (Pm-Me) domain includes:

- Definition of performance characteristics and measurements.
- Identification of non-functional requirements.

**Implementation**

The Summary & Overview domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Summary and Overview diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] . It shows a quick overview of an architecture description and a summary of the analysis. In the initial phases of architecture development, it serves as a planning guide. Upon completion of architecture, it provides a summary of findings and any conducted analysis.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Date and Time Summary Table' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] depicts all dates and times specified in the ISO8601 date-time format.
- Reports (.docx): - Summary and Overview. - All View.

The Summary and Overview Measurements (Pm-Me) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Typical Measurements table' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with hierarchical Typical Measurements table' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Actual Measurements table' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with hierarchical Actual Measurements table' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- Actual Measurements (.xlsx)

****

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Summary and Overview diagram

923507956

## PAGE 01117: Supports

- page_id: `227158036`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227158036/Supports
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T13:25:43.644+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A abstraction relationship that asserts that a service in someway contributes or assists in the execution of an Operational Activity.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

Abstraction

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">A abstraction relationship that asserts that a service in someway contributes or assists in the execution of an Operational Activity.</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Architecture Framework</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</span></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Extension</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">Abstraction</p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227158118 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01118: Supports relationship between Operational Activities and Services in UAF 1.2

- page_id: `227158118`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227158118/Supports+relationship+between+Operational+Activities+and+Services+in+UAF+1.2
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-05-19T11:30:32.982+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual > UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.2 > UPDM 2.1 to UAF 1.2. Mapping issues and solutions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

After the migration to UAF, the Supports relationships between Operational Activities and Services are created. The relationships are displayed in the following matrices:

UAF: Services to Operational Activities Mapping Matrix

DoDAF: SvcV-5 Services to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix

NAF: NSOV-3 Services to Operational Activities Mapping

NAF4: S4 Services to Operational Activities Mapping

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>After the migration to UAF, the Supports relationships between Operational Activities and Services are created. The relationships are displayed in the following matrices:</p><p>UAF: Services to Operational Activities Mapping Matrix</p><p>DoDAF: SvcV-5 Services to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix</p><p>NAF: NSOV-3 Services to Operational Activities Mapping</p><p>NAF4: S4 Services to Operational Activities Mapping</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227155519 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01119: SV-1 Systems Interface Description

- page_id: `227155519`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227155519/SV-1+Systems+Interface+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-02-21T11:25:05.910+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-1-systems-interface-description', 'sv-1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

404622161

404622164

404622162

**Description**

The SV-1 addresses the composition and interaction of systems. For DoDAF V2.0, the SV-1 incorporates the human elements as types of performers- Organizations and Personnel Types.

The SV-1 links together the operational and systems architecture models by depicting how resources are structured and interact to realize the logical architecture specified in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3']. A SV-1 may represent the realization of a requirement specified in an OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description (i.e., in a “To-Be” architecture), and so there may be many alternative SV models that could realize the operational requirement. Alternatively, in an “As-Is” architecture, the OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description may simply be a simplified, logical representation of the SV-1 to allow communication of key resource flows to non-technical stakeholders.

A system resource flow is a simplified representation of a pathway or network pattern, usually depicted graphically as a connector (i.e., a line with possible amplifying information). The SV-1 depicts all system resource flows between systems that are of interest. Note that resource flows between systems may be further specified in detail in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR3'].

Sub-system assemblies may be identified in SV-1 to any level (i.e., depth) of decomposition the architect sees fit. SV-1 may also identify the Physical Assets (e.g., Platforms) at which resources are deployed, and optionally overlay Operational Activities and Locations that utilize those resources. In many cases, an Operational Activity and Locations depicted in an OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description model may well be the logical representation of the resource that is shown in SV-1.

The intended usage of the SV-1 includes:

- Definition of system concepts.
- Definition of system options.
- System resource flow requirements capture.
- Capability integration planning.
- System integration management.
- Operational planning (capability and performer definition).

The SV-1 is used in two complementary ways:

- Describe the resource flows exchanged between resources in the architecture.
- Describe a solution, or solution option, in terms of the components of capability and their physical integration on platforms and other facilities.

**Implementation**

SV-1 can be represented using:

- SV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- SV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.
- UML Class diagram.
- UML Composite Structure diagram.
- SysML Block Definition diagram.
- SysML Internal Block diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

An SV-1 can optionally be adorned with performersoriginally specified in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3']. In this way, traceability can be established from the logical OV structure to the physical SV structure.

An interaction, as depicted in the SV-1, is an indicator that information passes from one performerto another. In the case of systems, this can be expanded into further detail in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3']. Resource Flows are summarized in a Systems-Systems Matrix ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-3 Systems-Systems Matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR3']).

The system Functions performed by the performersare specified in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3'], but may optionally be overlaid in the SV-1.

An Operational View (OV) suite may specify a set of requirements – either as a specific operational plan, or a scenario for procurement. As [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] specify the logical structure and behavior, SV-1 and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3']specify the physical structure and behavior (to the level of detail required by the architectural stakeholders).

404622159

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability Configuration' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Natural Resource' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Architecture' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Artifact' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Software' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Technology' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Organization' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Post' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Port' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Interface' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Role' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Connector' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Exhibits' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Information' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Geo Political Extent Type' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

For DoDAF V2.0, the SV-1 incorporates the human elements as types of <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">performers </span>- Organizations and Personnel Types.</p><p>The SV-1 links together the operational and systems architecture models by depicting how resources are structured and interact to realize the logical architecture specified in an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link>. A SV-1 may represent the realization of a requirement specified in an OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description (i.e., in a “To-Be” architecture), and so there may be many alternative SV models that could realize the operational requirement. Alternatively, in an “As-Is” architecture, the OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description may simply be a simplified, logical representation of the SV-1 to allow communication of key resource flows to non-technical stakeholders.</p><p>A system resource flow is a simplified representation of a pathway or network pattern, usually depicted graphically as a connector (i.e., a line with possible amplifying information). The SV-1 depicts all system resource flows between systems that are of interest. Note that resource flows between systems may be further specified in detail in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link>.</p><p>Sub-system assemblies may be identified in SV-1 to any level (i.e., depth) of decomposition the architect sees fit. SV-1 may also identify the Physical Assets (e.g., Platforms) at which resources are deployed, and optionally overlay Operational Activities and Locations that utilize those resources. In many cases, an Operational Activity and Locations depicted in an OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description model may well be the logical representation of the resource that is shown in SV-1.</p><p>The intended usage of the SV-1 includes:</p><ul><li>Definition of system concepts.</li><li>Definition of system options.</li><li>System resource flow requirements capture.</li><li>Capability integration planning.</li><li>System integration management.</li><li>Operational planning (capability and performer definition).</li></ul><p>The SV-1 is used in two complementary ways:</p><ul><li>Describe the resource flows exchanged between resources in the architecture.</li><li>Describe a solution, or solution option, in terms of the components of capability and their physical integration on platforms and other facilities.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SV-1 can be represented using:</p><ul><li>SV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</li><li>SV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.</li><li>UML Class diagram.</li><li>UML Composite Structure diagram.</li><li>SysML Block Definition diagram.</li><li>SysML Internal Block diagram.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" ac:alt="SV-1 Systems Interface Description"><ri:attachment ri:filename="sv_1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>An SV-1 can optionally be adorned with <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">performers </span>originally specified in an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. In this way, traceability can be established from the logical OV structure to the physical SV structure.</p><p>An interaction, as depicted in the SV-1, is an indicator that information passes from one <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">performer </span>to another. In the case of systems, this can be expanded into further detail in an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227155524 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01120: SV-1 Systems Interface Internal Description

- page_id: `227155524`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227155524/SV-1+Systems+Interface+Internal+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-02-21T11:28:16.946+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-1-systems-interface-internal-description', 'sv-1-internal']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

404838096

404838098

404838097

**Description**

The SV-1 addresses the internal composition and interaction of systems.

**Implementation**

SV-1 can be represented using:

- SV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- SV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.
- UML Class diagram.
- UML Composite Structure diagram.
- SysML Block Definition diagram.
- SysML Internal Block diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

An SV-1 can optionally be adorned with performers originally specified in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3']. In this way, traceability can be established from the logical OV structure to the physical SV structure.

An interaction, as depicted in the SV-1, is an indicator that information passes from one performer to another. In the case of systems, this can be expanded into further detail in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3']. Resource flows are summarized in a Systems-Systems Matrix ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-3 Systems-Systems Matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR3']).

Thesystem Functions performed by the Nodesare specified in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3'], but may optionally be overlaid in the SV-1.

An Operational View (OV) suite may specify a set of requirements – either as a specific operational plan, or a scenario for procurement. As [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] specify the logical structure and behavior, SV-1 and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] specify the physical structure and behavior (to the level of detail required by the architectural stakeholders).

404838095

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Role' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Port' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Connector' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SV-1 Internal diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Resource Exchange in SV-1 Internal diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6493c857-8dc1-42f9-b700-1dd7c7438ef5"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">404838096</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="60a6fa26-90db-4acb-8777-9613d321d6e2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">404838098</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2ae947e7-73c7-4d64-8162-a379e00972d4"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">404838097</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The SV-1 addresses the internal composition and interaction of systems.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SV-1 can be represented using:</p><ul><li>SV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</li><li>SV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.</li><li>UML Class diagram.</li><li>UML Composite Structure diagram.</li><li>SysML Block Definition diagram.</li><li>SysML Internal Block diagram.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:title="SV-1 Systems Interface Internal Description" ac:alt="SV-1 Systems Interface Internal Description"><ri:attachment ri:filename="sv_1_internal.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Internal Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>An SV-1 can optionally be adorned with performers originally specified in an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. In this way, traceability can be established from the logical OV structure to the physical SV structure.</p><p>An interaction, as depicted in the SV-1, is an indicator that information passes from one performer to another. In the case of systems, this can be expanded into further detail in an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. Resource flows are summarized in a Systems-Systems Matrix (<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-3 Systems-Systems Matrix" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-3]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>).</p><p>The<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> system Functions</span> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">performed</span> by the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Nodes </span>are specified in an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-4 Systems Functionality Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-4 System Functionality Description]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, but may optionally be overlaid in the SV-1.</p><p>An Operational View (OV) suite may specify a set of requirements – either as a specific operational plan, or a scenario for procurement. As <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-5 Operational Activity Model" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-5]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> specify the logical structure and behavior, SV-1 and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-4 Systems Functionality Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> specify the physical structure and behavior (to the level of detail required by the architectural stakeholders).</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3959acf7-850d-4cf6-b541-1391f37bafd1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">404838095</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Role" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Port" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Connector" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Exchange" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating SV-1 Internal diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating Resource Exchange in SV-1 Internal diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227155701 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01121: SV-10a Resource Parametric

- page_id: `227155701`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227155701/SV-10a+Resource+Parametric
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-02T10:30:29.100+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint > MODAF. SV-10a Resource Constraints Specification
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

497880528

497880530

497880529

**Description**

A Parametric diagram includes the usage of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Block' space='SYSMLP2024xR3'] to constrain the properties of another Capability Configuration. It contains [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Property' space='SYSMLP2024xR3'] and constraint parameters as well as other properties from within that internal block context. All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a constraint parameter or contain a property that is bound to a constraint parameter (through any number of containment levels). A constraint block generally contain many constraints, each of them containing many constraint parameters.

497880527

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Parametric diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

A constraint block generally contain many constraints, each of them containing many constraint parameters.</p><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9e0817d3-1533-46ca-b368-1e58ee00302d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">497880527</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Parametric diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227155574 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01123: SV-10a Systems Rules Model

- page_id: `227155574`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227155574/SV-10a+Systems+Rules+Model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-05-19T08:05:17.964+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-10a-systems-rules-model', 'sv-10a']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

408324973

408324976

408324974

**Description**

The SV-10a specifies functional and nonfunctional constraints on the implementation aspects of the architecture (i.e., the structural and behavioral elements of the Systems Viewpoint).

The SV-10a DoDAF-described Model describes constraints on the resources, functions, data, and ports that make up the SV. The constraints are specified in text and may be functional or structural (i.e., non-functional).

The intended usage of the SV-10a includes:

- Definition of implementation logic.
- Identification of resource constraints.

Implementation

SV-10a can be represented using:

- SV-10a table. [ATTACHMENT filename='sv_10a.png']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-10a Systems Parametric' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] .
- SV-10a spreadsheet report.

In contrast to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-6a Operational Rules Model' space='UAF12P2024xR3'], SV-10a focuses on physical and data constraints rather than business rules.

**Constraint owner**

You can choose where all the constraints will be stored through the**Project Options**.

To select a constraint owner

1. In the main menu, click Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. Go to General > UAF , and in the Default Constraint Owners property group, choose a needed owner.

408324971

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Constraint' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Artifact' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Software' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability Configuration' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Organization' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Post' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Function' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Information' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SV-10a table' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-10a Systems Parametric' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

The constraints are specified in text and may be functional or structural (i.e., non-functional).</p><p>The intended usage of the SV-10a includes:</p><ul><li>Definition of implementation logic.</li><li>Identification of resource constraints.</li></ul><p>Implementation</p><p>SV-10a can be represented using:</p><ul><li>SV-10a table.<br /><ac:image ac:title="SV-10a Systems Rules Model" ac:alt="SV-10a Systems Rules Model"><ri:attachment ri:filename="sv_10a.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-10a Systems Rules Model" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-10a Systems Parametric" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-10a Systems Parametric diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>SV-10a spreadsheet report.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>In contrast to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-6a Operational Rules Model" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link>, SV-10a focuses on physical and data constraints rather than business rules.</p><p style=""><strong>Constraint owner</strong></p><p style="">You can choose where all the constraints will be stored through the<span> </span><strong>Project Options</strong>.</p><p style="">To select a constraint owner</p><hr style="" /><ol style=""><li>In the main menu, click<span> </span><strong>Options</strong><span> </span>&gt;<span> </span><strong>Project</strong>. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227155579 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01124: SV-10b Systems State Transition Description

- page_id: `227155579`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227155579/SV-10b+Systems+State+Transition+Description
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-02T10:30:25.975+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-10b-systems-state-transition-description', 'sv-10b']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

408450020

408450022

408450021

**Description**

The SV-10b is a graphical method of describing a resourceor system function response to various events by changing its state. The diagram basically represents the sets of events to which the resources in the Activities respond (by taking an action to move to a new state) as a function of its current state. Each transition specifies an event and an action.

The explicit time sequencing of service functions in response to external and internal events is not fully expressed in SV-4 Systems Functionality Description. The SV-10b can be used to describe the explicit sequencing of the functions. Alternatively, SV-10b can be used to reflect explicit sequencing of the actions internal to a single function, or the sequencing of system functions with respect to a specific resource.

The intended usage of the SV-10b includes:

- Definition of states, events and state transitions (behavioral modeling).
- Identification of constraints.

**Implementation**

SV-10b can be represented using a UML State Machine diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The SV-10b can be used to describe the detailed sequencing of functions described in SV-4 Systems Functionality Description. However, the relationship between the actions included in SV-10b and the functions in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] depends on the purposes of the architecture and the level of abstraction used in the models. The explicit sequencing of functions in response to external and internal events is not fully expressed in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3']. SV-10b can be used to reflect explicit sequencing of the functions, the sequencing of actions internal to a single function, or the sequencing of functions with respect to a specific resource.

Depending upon the architecture project’s needs, the SV-10b may be used separately or in conjunction with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3'].

408450019

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Artifact' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Software' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability Configuration' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Organization' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Post' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Allocate Activities to State Internal Behaviors' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8900a3eb-51b4-4f94-b0f3-9b675ed973f7"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">408450020</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="572a16c2-827b-40b3-836b-4b2284ccb659"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">408450022</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="22b4d0d0-c773-49b9-89b8-4a86c485845a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">408450021</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The SV-10b is a graphical method of describing a resource<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> or system function</span> response to various events by changing its state. The diagram basically represents the sets of events to which the resources in the Activities respond (by taking an action to move to a new state) as a function of its current state. Each transition specifies an event and an action.</p><p>The explicit time sequencing of service functions in response to external and internal events is not fully expressed in SV-4 Systems Functionality Description. The SV-10b can be used to describe the explicit sequencing of the functions. Alternatively, SV-10b can be used to reflect explicit sequencing of the actions internal to a single function, or the sequencing of system functions with respect to a specific resource.</p><p>The intended usage of the SV-10b includes:</p><ul><li>Definition of states, events and state transitions (behavioral modeling).</li><li>Identification of constraints.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SV-10b can be represented using a UML State Machine diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="SV-10b Systems State Transition Description" ac:alt="SV-10b Systems State Transition Description"><ri:attachment ri:filename="SV-10b_sys_state_transit_desc.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-10b Systems State Transition Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The SV-10b can be used to describe the detailed sequencing of functions described in SV-4 Systems Functionality Description. However, the relationship between the actions included in SV-10b and the functions in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-4 Systems Functionality Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link> depends on the purposes of the architecture and the level of abstraction used in the models. The explicit sequencing of functions in response to external and internal events is not fully expressed in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-4 Systems Functionality Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link>. SV-10b can be used to reflect explicit sequencing of the functions, the sequencing of actions internal to a single function, or the sequencing of functions with respect to a specific resource.</p><p>Depending upon the architecture project’s needs, the SV-10b may be used separately or in conjunction with the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link>.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dc102278-27b9-4eeb-a893-6027b841a78d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">408450019</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" ri:content-title="State" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Artifact" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Software" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Capability Configuration" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Organization" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Post" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Allocate Activities to State Internal Behaviors" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227155581 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01125: SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description

- page_id: `227155581`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227155581/SV-10c+Systems+Event-Trace+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:47:55.273+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-10c-systems-event-trace-description', 'sv-10c']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

408519862

408519864

408519863

**Description**

The SV-10c provides a time-ordered examination of the interactions between functional resources. Each event-trace diagram should have an accompanying description that defines the particular scenario or situation.

The SV-10c is valuable for moving to the next level of detail from the initial solution design, to help define a sequence of functions and system data interfaces, and to ensure that each participating resource or system port role has the necessary information it needs, at the right time, to perform its assigned functionality.

The intended usage of the SV-10c includes:

- Analysis of resource events impacting operation.
- Behavioral analysis.
- Identification of non-functional system requirements.

**Implementation**

SV-10b can be represented using a UML State Machine diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The SV-10c is typically used in conjunction with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-10b Systems State Transition Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] to describe the dynamic behavior of resources. The data content of messages that connect resource flows in a SV-10c may be related with resource flows (the interactions in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-3 Systems-Systems Matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR3']), resource flows (the data in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR3']) and entities (in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='DIV-3 Physical Data Model' space='UAF12P2024xR3']) modeled in other models.

408519861

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Message' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

**Related procedures**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
