Each event-trace diagram should have an accompanying description that defines the particular scenario or situation.</p><p>The SV-10c is valuable for moving to the next level of detail from the initial solution design, to help define a sequence of functions and system data interfaces, and to ensure that each participating resource or system port role has the necessary information it needs, at the right time, to perform its assigned functionality.</p><p>The intended usage of the SV-10c includes:</p><ul><li>Analysis of resource events impacting operation.</li><li>Behavioral analysis.</li><li>Identification of non-functional system requirements.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SV-10b can be represented using a UML State Machine diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description" ac:alt="SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description"><ri:attachment ri:filename="SV-10c_sys_event_trace_desc.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The SV-10c is typically used in conjunction with the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-10b Systems State Transition Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link> to describe the dynamic behavior of resources. The data content of messages that connect resource flows in a SV-10c may be related with resource flows (the interactions in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-3 Systems-Systems Matrix" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-3 Systems- Systems Matrix]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>), resource flows (the data in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-4 Systems Functionality Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link>) and entities (in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="DIV-3 Physical Data Model" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link>) modeled in other models.</p><p><br /></p><p> </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bd311ea2-b24d-4037-8781-e1b9e6789282"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">408519861</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Message" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Exchange" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="2e3c6f87-8b7d-41b6-915c-e381a25cebce" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227155584 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01126: SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description BPD

- page_id: `227155584`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227155584/SV-10c+Systems+Event-Trace+Description+BPD
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-15T11:43:22.792+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description (BPD) diagram is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD). It describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the businesswork. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed businessactivities.

SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description (BPD) diagram is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD). It describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">business </span>work. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">business </span>activities.</p><p>SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).</p>
````

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

405340330

405340333

405340331

**Description**

A SV-2 specifies the internal system resource flows between systems and may also list the protocol stacks used in connections.

**Implementation**

SV-2 can be represented using:

- SV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- SV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.
- UML Class diagram.
- UML Composite Structure diagram.
- SysML Block Definition diagram.
- SysML Internal Block diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Any protocol referred to in a SV-2 diagram needs to be defined in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='StdV-1 Standards Profile' space='UAF12P2024xR3'].

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227155529 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01128: SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description

- page_id: `227155529`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227155529/SV-2+Systems+Resource+Flow+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-02-21T11:29:33.492+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-2-systems-resource-flow-description', 'sv-2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

404994356

404994358

404994357

**Description**

A SV-2 specifies the system resource flows between systems and may also list the protocol stacks used in connections.

A SV-2 described Model is used to give a precise specification of a connection between systems. This may be an existing connection, or a specification for a connection that is to be made.

The intended usage of the SV-2 includes:

- Resource flow specification.

**Implementation**

SV-2 can be represented using:

- SV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- SV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.
- UML Class diagram.
- UML Composite Structure diagram.
- SysML Block Definition diagram.
- SysML Internal Block diagram.

Any protocol referred to in a SV-2 diagram needs to be defined in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='StdV-1 Standards Profile' space='UAF12P2024xR3'].

404994355

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227155537 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01129: SV-3 Systems-Systems Matrix

- page_id: `227155537`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227155537/SV-3+Systems-Systems+Matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-02T10:30:24.933+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-3-systems-systems-matrix', 'sv-3']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

405463656

405463658

405463657

**Description**

A SV-3 enables a quick overview of all the system resource interactions specified in one or more [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] models. The SV-3 provides a tabular summary of the system interactions specified in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] model for the Architectural Description. The matrix format supports a rapid assessment of potential commonalities and redundancies (or, if fault-tolerance is desired, the lack of redundancies).

The SV-3 can be organized in a number of ways to emphasize the association of groups of system pairs in context with the architecture’s purpose.

The intended usage of the SV-3 includes:

- Summarizing system resource interactions.
- Interface management.
- Comparing interoperability characteristics of solution options.

**Implementation**

SV-3 can be represented using a SV-3 diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] concentrates on system resources and their interactions, and these are summarized in a SV-3.

405463655

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Artifact' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="85ea93d4-7ef1-4360-9bb0-6ee767f7c7a1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">405463656</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5625214c-482c-4439-9887-068a7ed52122"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">405463658</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b2442d89-f777-43dc-b834-dd1d1b4fd4b4"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">405463657</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>A SV-3 enables a quick overview of all the system resource interactions specified in one or more <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link> models. The SV-3 provides a tabular summary of the system interactions specified in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link> model for the Architectural Description. The matrix format supports a rapid assessment of potential commonalities and redundancies (or, if fault-tolerance is desired, the lack of redundancies).</p><p>The SV-3 can be organized in a number of ways to emphasize the association of groups of system pairs in context with the architecture’s purpose.</p><p>The intended usage of the SV-3 includes:</p><ul><li>Summarizing system resource interactions.</li><li>Interface management.</li><li>Comparing interoperability characteristics of solution options.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SV-3 can be represented using a SV-3 diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="SV-3 Systems-Systems Matrix" ac:alt="SV-3 Systems-Systems Matrix"><ri:attachment ri:filename="sv_3.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-3 Systems-Systems Matrix" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> concentrates on system resources and their interactions, and these are summarized in a SV-3.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8910d4b1-1972-465c-ad22-5ebb9d357a47"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">405463655</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Artifact" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Software" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Capability Configuration" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Organization" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Post" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Exchange" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227155548 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01130: SV-4 Functionality Description BPD

- page_id: `227155548`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227155548/SV-4+Functionality+Description+BPD
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-15T11:02:36.431+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

SV-4 Functionality Description BPD (BPD) diagram is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD). It describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the businesswork. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed businessactivities.

SV-4 Functionality Description BPD (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>SV-4 Functionality Description BPD (BPD) diagram is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD). It describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">business </span>work. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">business </span>activities.</p><p>SV-4 Functionality Description BPD (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).</p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227155542 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01131: SV-4 Systems Functionality Description

- page_id: `227155542`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227155542/SV-4+Systems+Functionality+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2019-07-24T14:12:30.110+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-4-systems-functionality-description', 'sv-4']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

405595921

405595923

405595922

**Description**

The SV-4 addresses human and system functionality.

The primary purposes of SV-4 are to:

- Develop a clear description of the necessary data flows that are input (consumed) by and output (produced) by each resource.
- Ensure that the functional connectivity is complete (i.e., that a resource’s required inputs are all satisfied).
- Ensure that the functional decomposition reaches an appropriate level of detail.

The Systems Functionality Description provides detailed information regarding the:

- Allocation of functions to resources.
- Flow of resources between functions.

The SV-4 is the Systems Viewpoint model counterpart to the OV-5b Activity Model of the Operational Viewpoint.

The intended usage of the SV-4 includes:

- Description of task workflow.
- Identification of functional system requirements.
- Functional decomposition of systems.
- Relate human and system functions.

**Implementation**

SV-4 can be represented using:

- SV-4 diagram for System Function hierarchies. This diagram is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='sv_4.png']
- SV-4 diagram for System Function flows. This diagram is based on the UML Activity diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- UML Class diagram.
- UML Activity diagram.
- SysML Block diagram.
- SysML Activity diagram.

The SV-4 is the behavioral counterpart to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] (in the same way that [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space='UAF12P2024xR3']OV-5b Operational Activity Model is the behavioral counterpart to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3']OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description).

405595920

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227155549 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01132: SV-5a Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix

- page_id: `227155549`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227155549/SV-5a+Operational+Activity+to+Systems+Function+Traceability+Matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-02T10:30:25.241+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-5a', 'sv-5a-operational-activity-to-systems-function-traceability-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

406176080

406176083

406176082

**Description**

The SV-5a addresses the linkage between systemfunctions described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] and Operational Activities specified in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space='UAF12P2024xR3']. The SV-5a depicts the mapping of systemfunctions and, optionally, the capabilities and performers that provide them to operational activities. The SV-5a identifies the transformation of an operational need into a purposefulaction performed by a systemor solution.

During requirements definition, the SV-5a plays a particularly important role in tracing the architectural elements associated with system function requirements to those associated with user requirements.

The intended usage of the SV-5a includes:

- Tracing functional system requirements to user requirements.
- Tracing solution options to requirements.
- Identification of overlaps or gaps.

**Implementation**

SV-5a can be represented using a SV-5a diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. Systemfunctions are used as row elements and Operational Activities are used as column elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

DoDAF uses the term Operational Activity in the OVs and the term system function in the SVs to refer to essentially the same kind of thing; both activities and functions are tasks that are performed, accept inputs, and develop outputs. The distinction between an Operational Activity and a Function is a question of what and how.

The Operational Activity is a specification of what is to be done, regardless of the mechanism used. A system function is specifies how a resource carries it out. For this reason, SV-5a is a significant model, as it ties together the logical specification in the OV-5a with the physical specification of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3']. System functions can be carried out by Functional Resources(systems, performers executing activities, and performers).

406176079

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Function' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Activity' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Implements' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources to Capabilities Mapping matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Implied Relations' space='MD2024xR3']

The SV-5a depicts the mapping of <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">system </span>functions and, optionally, the capabilities and performers that provide them to operational activities. The SV-5a identifies the transformation of an operational need into a purposeful<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> action performed by a system </span>or solution.</p><p>During requirements definition, the SV-5a plays a particularly important role in tracing the architectural elements associated with system function requirements to those associated with user requirements.</p><p>The intended usage of the SV-5a includes:</p><ul><li>Tracing functional system requirements to user requirements.</li><li>Tracing solution options to requirements.</li><li>Identification of overlaps or gaps.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SV-5a can be represented using a SV-5a diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">System </span>functions are used as row elements and Operational Activities are used as column elements.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="SV-5a Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix" ac:alt="SV-5a Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix"><ri:attachment ri:filename="SV-5a.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-5a Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>DoDAF uses the term Operational Activity in the OVs and the term <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">system</span> function in the SVs to refer to essentially the same kind of thing; both activities and functions are tasks that are <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">performed, accept inputs, and develop outputs. The distinction between an Operational Activity and a Function is a question of what and how.</span></p><p>The Operational Activity is a specification of what is to be done, regardless of the mechanism used. A <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">system</span> function is specifies how a resource carries it out. For this reason, SV-5a is a significant model, as it ties together the logical specification in the OV-5a with the physical specification of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-4 Systems Functionality Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link>. <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">System</span> functions can be carried out by Functional Resources<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> (systems, performers executing activities, and performers).</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cca833aa-6a8b-429c-b0f9-15449df37733"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">406176079</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Function" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Activity" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Implements" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="f8c1b6a1-6e44-4d11-ae6b-2dccc6fde77c" /></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Resources to Capabilities Mapping matrix" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Resources to Capabilities Mapping matrix]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" ri:content-title="Using Implied Relations" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227155552 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01133: SV-5b Operational Activity to Systems Traceability Matrix

- page_id: `227155552`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227155552/SV-5b+Operational+Activity+to+Systems+Traceability+Matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-02T10:30:25.445+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-5b-operational-activity-to-systems-traceability-matrix', 'sv-5b']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

407600326

407600328

407600327

**Description**

The SV-5b addresses the linkage between described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] and Operational Activities specified in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space='UAF12P2024xR3']. The SV-5b depicts the mapping of systems and, optionally, the capabilities and nodes that provide them to operational activities.The SV-5b identifies the transformation of an operational need into a purposeful action performed by a system or solution.

During requirements definition, the SV-5b plays a particularly important role in tracing the architectural elements associated with system requirements to those associated with user requirements.

The intended usage of the SV-5b includes:

- Tracing system requirements to user requirements.
- Tracing solution options to requirements.
- Identification of overlaps or gaps.

**Implementation**

SV-5b can be represented using a SV-5b diagram which is a non-editable Dependency Matrix. Resourcesare used as row elements and Operational Activities are used as column elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The SV-5b is generally presented as a matrix of the relationship between systemresourcesand activities and can be a summary of the Operational Activity to System Function Traceability Matrix ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-5a Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR3']). The SV-5b can show requirements traceability with Operational Activities on one axis of a matrix, the System Functions on the other axis, and with an X, date, or phase in the intersecting cells, where appropriate.

407600325

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Artifact' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Function' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Activity' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Is Capable To Perform' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Implements' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources to Capabilities Mapping matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Implied Relations' space='MD2024xR3']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The SV-5b depicts the mapping <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">of systems and, optionally, the capabilities and nodes that provide them to operational activities. </span>The SV-5b identifies the transformation of an operational need into a purposeful action <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">performed by a system or solution</span>.</p><p>During requirements definition, the SV-5b plays a particularly important role in tracing the architectural elements associated with system requirements to those associated with user requirements.</p><p>The intended usage of the SV-5b includes:</p><ul><li>Tracing system requirements to user requirements.</li><li>Tracing solution options to requirements.</li><li>Identification of overlaps or gaps.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SV-5b can be represented using a SV-5b diagram which is a non-editable Dependency Matrix. <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Resources </span>are used as row elements and Operational Activities are used as column elements.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="SV-5b Operational Activity to Systems Traceability Matrix" ac:alt="SV-5b Operational Activity to Systems Traceability Matrix"><ri:attachment ri:filename="sv_5b.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-5b Operational Activity to Systems Traceability Matrix" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The SV-5b is generally presented as a matrix of the relationship between <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">system </span><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">resources</span> </span>and activities and can be a summary of the Operational Activity to System Function Traceability Matrix (<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-5a Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-5a]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>). The SV-5b can show requirements traceability with Operational Activities on one axis of a matrix, the System Functions on the other axis, and with an X, date, or phase in the intersecting cells, where appropriate.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d3cb982c-f8e0-44e7-af22-f7c23654069e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">407600325</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Artifact" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Function" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Activity" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Is Capable To Perform" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Implements" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="8d6e176d-0784-4323-97c7-32722b44ca32" /></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Resources to Capabilities Mapping matrix" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Resources to Capabilities Mapping matrix]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" ri:content-title="Using Implied Relations" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227155556 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01134: SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix

- page_id: `227155556`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227155556/SV-6+Systems+Resource+Flow+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-02-21T11:31:41.675+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-6-systems-resource-flow-matrix', 'sv-6']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

407725747

407725749

407725748

**Description**

The SV-6 specifies the characteristics of the system resource flows exchanged between system resourceswith emphasis on resources crossing the system boundary.

The SV-6 focuses on the specific aspects of the system resource flow and the system resource flow content in a tabular format.

The intended usage of the SV-6 includes:

- Detailed definition of resource flows.

**Implementation**

SV-6 can be represented using:

- SV-6 matrix. [ATTACHMENT filename='sv_6.png']
- SV-6 role-based matrix. A role-based table represents exchanges between Resource Roles (resource usages). [IMAGE alt='' src='']
- SV-6 spreadsheet report.

The SV-6 is the physical equivalent of the logical [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] table and provides detailed information on the system connections which implement the resource flow exchanges specified in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR3'].

407725746

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Artifact' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Organization' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Post' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Information' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Natural Resource' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Geo Political Extent Type' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Function' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying Custom Measurements' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227155561 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01135: SV-7 Systems Measures Matrix

- page_id: `227155561`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227155561/SV-7+Systems+Measures+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-03-03T12:43:39.243+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-7-systems-measures-matrix', 'sv-7', 'sv-7-actual-measures-table', 'sv-7-typical-measures-table']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

407909009

407909011

407909010

**Description**

The SV-7 depicts the measures (metrics) of resources. The Systems Measures Matrix expands on the information presented in an SV-1 by depicting the characteristics of the resources in the SV-1.

The intended usage of the SV-7 includes:

- Definition of performance characteristics and measures (metrics).
- Identification of non-functional requirements.

**Implementation**

SV-7 can be represented using:

- SV-7 Typical measures table [ATTACHMENT filename='sv_7_typical.png']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with hierarchical Typical Measurements table' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with hierarchical Actual Measurements table' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- SV-7 Actual measures table [ATTACHMENT filename='sv_7_actual.png']
- SV-7 actual measures spreadsheet report

The Systems Measures Matrix expands on the information presented in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] by depicting the characteristics of the Systems and other Resources shown in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3']. The Systems Measures Matrix expands on the information presented in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] by depicting the characteristics of the Systems shown in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3'].

407909008

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Artifact' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Measurement' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

**Related procedures**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227155564 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01136: SV-8 Systems Evolution Description

- page_id: `227155564`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227155564/SV-8+Systems+Evolution+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-15T11:35:56.262+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-8-systems-evolution-description', 'sv-8']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

408089834

408089836

408089835

**Description**

The SV-8 presents a whole lifecycle view of resources (systems), describing how they change over time. It shows the structure of several resources mapped against a timeline.

The intended usage of the SV-8 includes:

- Development of incremental acquisition strategy.
- Planning technology insertion.

**Implementation**

The SV-8 view can be represented using a SV-8 Systems Evolution Description diagram which is realized as a time based diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The SV-8 model shows the structure of resources modeled in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3']. The changes depicted in the SV-8 are derived from the project milestones that are shown in a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='PV-2 Project Timelines' space='UAF12P2024xR3']. When the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='PV-2 Project Timelines' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] is used for capability acquisition projects, there is likely to be a close relationship between these two models.

408089833

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Whole Life Configuration' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Version Of Configuration' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Software' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227155569 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01137: SV-9 Systems Technology and Skills Forecast

- page_id: `227155569`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227155569/SV-9+Systems+Technology+and+Skills+Forecast
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-15T11:38:06.972+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-9-systems-technology-and-skills-forecast', 'sv-9']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

408231854

408231856

408231855

**Description**

The SV-9 defines the underlying current and expected supporting technologies and skills. Expected supporting technologies and skills are those that can be reasonably forecast given the current state of technology and skills as well as the expected improvements or trends. New technologies and skills are tied to specific time periods, which can correlate against the time periods used in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-8 Systems Evolution Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] milestones and linked to capability phases.

The SV-9 provides a summary of emerging technologies and skills that impact the architecture.

The SV-9 provides descriptions of relevant:

- Emerging capabilities.
- Industry trends.
- Predictions (with associated confidence factors) of the availability and readiness of specific hardware and software systems.
- Current and possible future skills.

In addition to providing an inventory of trends, capabilities and systems, the DoDAF-described Model SV-9 also includes an assessment of the potential impact of these items on the architecture. Given the future-oriented nature of this model, forecasts are typically made in short, mid and long-term timeframes, such as 6, 12 and 18-month intervals.

The intended usage of the SV-9 includes:

- Forecasting technology readiness against time.
- HR Trends Analysis.
- Recruitment Planning.
- Planning technology insertion.
- Input to options analysis.

**Implementation**

SV-9 can be represented using a SV-9 table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The SV-9 DoDAF-described Model forecasts relates to the Standards Profile ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='StdV-1 Standards Profile' space='UAF12P2024xR3']) in that a timed forecast may contribute to the decision to retire or phase out the use of a certain standard in connection with a resource. Similarly, SV-9 forecasts relate to the Standards Forecasts ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='StdV-2 Standards Forecast' space='UAF12P2024xR3']) in that a certain standard may be adopted depending on a certain technology or skill becoming available (e.g., the availability of Java Script may influence the decision to adopt a new HTML standard).

408231853

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Forecast' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Artifact' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Software' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability Configuration' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Organization' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Post' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Protocol' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Standard' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SV-9 table' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227158145 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01138: SvcV diagrams migration

- page_id: `227158145`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227158145/SvcV+diagrams+migration
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-09-16T11:53:04.704+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual > UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.1 > Mapping issues and solutions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Issue**

All Service Access elements are migrated to Capability Configuration elements. That affects all diagrams in the Services viewpoint (SvcV). For resources modelingin SV diagrams, use Capability Configuration elements, but for services modeling in SvcV diagrams, the Capability Configurations, Resource Roles, all relationships, and Ports must be converted manually.

**Solution**

For smooth Capability Configurations, Resource Roles, all relationships, and Ports manually conversion, do the following:

truenone

##### Convert Capability Configurations to Services

To convert Capability Configuration to Service

1. Right-click the single or multiple Capability Configurations.
2. Select **Refactor** > **Convert To** > **More Elements**.
3. From the opened dialog, choose the Service element.
4. Confirm this selection by clicking **Yes** in the warning message.

##### Convert Resource Roles to Service Roles

If Capability Configuration has a Resource Role, it must be converted to Service Role manually.

To convert Resource Role to Service Role

1. Right-click the single or multiple Resource Roles.
2. Select **Refactor** > **Convert To** > **More Elements**.
3. From the opened dialog, choose the Service Role element.
4. Confirm this selection by clicking **Yes** in the warning message.

##### Convert relationships between Service Roles to Service Connectors

All existing relationships between already converted Service Roles must be converted to Service Connector manually.

To convert relationships between Service Role to Service Connector

1. Right-click the single or multiple relationships between ServiceRoles.
2. Select **Refactor** > **Convert To** > **More Elements**.
3. From the opened dialog, choosethe Service Connectorelement.
4. Confirm this selection by clicking **Yes** in the warning message.

##### Convert Ports to Service Ports

If Service has Ports, they must be converted to Service Ports manually.

To convert Port to Service Port

1. Right-click the single or multiple Ports.
2. Select **Refactor** > **Convert To** > **More Elements**.
3. From the opened dialog, choosethe Service Port element.
4. Confirm this selection by clicking **Yes** in the warning message.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><strong>Issue</strong></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">All Service Access elements are migrated to Capability Configuration elements. That affects all diagrams in the Services viewpoint (<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">SvcV</span>). For resources modeling<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> in SV diagrams,</span> </span> use Capability Configuration elements, but for services modeling in SvcV diagrams, the Capability Configurations, Resource Roles, all relationships, and Ports must be converted manually.<br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><strong>Solution</strong><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">For smooth Capability Configurations, Resource Roles, all relationships, and Ports manually conversion, do the following:</span></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="51f1f6e4-4f96-42ae-9acb-55cef083c46e"><ac:parameter ac:name="outline">true</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="style">none</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h3>Convert Capability Configurations to Services</h3><p><br /><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">To convert Capability Configuration to Service</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Right-click the single or multiple Capability Configurations.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Select <strong>Refactor</strong> &gt; <strong>Convert To</strong> &gt; <strong>More Elements</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">From the opened dialog, choose the Service element.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Confirm this selection by clicking <strong>Yes</strong> in the warning message.</span></li></ol><h3><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Convert Resource Roles to Service Roles </span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">If Capability Configuration has a Resource Role, it must be converted to Service Role manually.</span><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">To convert <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Resource Role</span></span> to Service <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Role</span></span><br /></span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Right-click the single or multiple <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Resource Role</span>s.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Select <strong>Refactor</strong> &gt; <strong>Convert To</strong> &gt; <strong>More Elements</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">From the opened dialog, choose the Service <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Role</span> element.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Confirm this selection by clicking <strong>Yes</strong> in the warning message.</span></li></ol><h3><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Convert relationships <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">between Service <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Role</span></span></span>s to Service Connectors<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">All existing relationships between already converted Service Roles must be converted to Service Connector manually.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">To convert relationships between Service <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Role</span></span> <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">to Service Connector</span><br /></span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Right-click the single or multiple <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">relationships <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">between Service<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Role</span></span></span>s</span>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Select <strong>Refactor</strong> &gt; <strong>Convert To</strong> &gt; <strong>More Elements</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">From the opened dialog, choose<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> the Service Connector </span>element.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Confirm this selection by clicking <strong>Yes</strong> in the warning message.</span></li></ol><h3><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Convert Ports to Service Ports</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">If Service has Ports, they must be converted to Service Ports manually.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">To convert Port <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">to Service Port</span></span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Right-click the single or multiple <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Ports</span>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Select <strong>Refactor</strong> &gt; <strong>Convert To</strong> &gt; <strong>More Elements</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">From the opened dialog, choose<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> the <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Service Port</span></span> </span> element.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Confirm this selection by clicking <strong>Yes</strong> in the warning message.</span></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227158125 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01139: SvcV diagrams migration in UAF 1.2

- page_id: `227158125`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227158125/SvcV+diagrams+migration+in+UAF+1.2
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-05-19T11:28:37.006+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual > UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.2 > UPDM 2.1 to UAF 1.2. Mapping issues and solutions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Issue**

All Service Access elements are migrated to Resource Service elements. That affects diagrams in Services viewpoint (SvcV). For resources modelingin SV diagrams, use Resource Service elements, but for services modeling in SvcV diagrams, the Resource Service, Resource Roles, all relationships, and Ports must be converted manually.

**Solution**

For a smooth Capability Configurations, Resource Roles, all relationships, and Ports manually conversion, do the following:

truenone

##### Convert Resource Service to Service

To convert Resource Service to Service

1. Right-click the single or multiple Resource Service.
2. Select **Refactor** > **Convert To** > **More Elements**.
3. From the opened dialog, choose Service element.
4. Confirm this selection by clicking **Yes** in the warning message.

##### Convert Resource Roles to Service Roles

If Resource Service has a Resource Role, it must be converted to Service Role manually.

To convert Resource Role to Service Role

1. Right-click the single or multiple Resource Roles.
2. Select **Refactor** > **Convert To** > **More Elements**.
3. From the opened dialog, choose Service Role element.
4. Confirm this selection by clicking **Yes** in the warning message.

##### Convert relationships between Service Roles to Service Connectors

All existing relationships between already converted Service Roles, must be converted to Service Connector manually.

To convert relationships between Service Role to Service Connector

1. Right-click the single or multiple relationships between Service Roles.
2. Select **Refactor** > **Convert To** > **More Elements**.
3. From the opened dialog, chooseService Connector element.
4. Confirm this selection by clicking **Yes** in the warning message.

##### Convert ports to Service Ports

If Service has ports, their must be converted to Service Ports manually.

To convert port to Service Port

1. Right-click the single or multiple ports.
2. Select **Refactor** > **Convert To** > **More Elements**.
3. From the opened dialog, chooseService Port element.
4. Confirm this selection by clicking **Yes** in the warning message.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<strong>Convert To</strong> &gt; <strong>More Elements</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">From the opened dialog, choose<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> Service Connector </span> element.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Confirm this selection by clicking <strong>Yes</strong> in the warning message.</span></li></ol><h3><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Convert ports to Service Ports</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">If Service has ports, their must be converted to Service Ports manually.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">To convert port <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">to Service Port</span></span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Right-click the single or multiple p<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">orts</span>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Select <strong>Refactor</strong> &gt; <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227155456 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01140: SvcV-1 Services Context Description

- page_id: `227155456`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227155456/SvcV-1+Services+Context+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-15T07:55:43.712+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint
- labels: ['svcv-1-services-context-description', 'svcv-1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

394841328

394841330

394841329

**Description**

The SvcV-1 addresses the composition and interaction of services. For DoDAF V2.0, SvcV-1 incorporates human elements as types ofperformers - Organizations and Personnel Types.

The SvcV-1 links together the operational and services architecture models by depicting how resources are structured and interact to realize the logical architecture specified in an OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description. A SvcV-1 may represent the realization of a requirement specified in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] (i.e., in a “To-Be” Architectural Description), and so there may be many alternative SvcV models that could realize the operational requirement. Alternatively, in an “As-Is” Architectural Description, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] may simply be a simplified, logical representation of the SvcV-1 to allow communication of key Resource Flows to non-technical stakeholders.

The intended usage of the SvcV-1 includes:

- Definition of service concepts.
- Definition of service options.
- Service Resource Flow requirements capture.
- Capability integration planning.
- Service integration management.
- Operational planning (capability and performer definition).

The SvcV-1 is used in two complementary ways:

- Describe the Resource Flows exchanged between resources in the architecture.
- Describe a solution, or solution option, in terms of the components of capability and their physical integration on platforms and other facilities.

**Implementation**

SvcV-1 can be represented using a SvcV-1 diagram which is based on the UML class diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The SvcV-1 links together the operational and services architecture models by depicting how resources are structured and interact to realize the logical architecture specified in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3']. A SvcV-1 may represent the realization of a requirement specified in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] (i.e., in a “To-Be” Architectural Description), and so there may be many alternative SvcV models that could realize the operational requirement. Alternatively, in annus-Is” Architectural Description, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] may simply be a simplified, logical representation of the SvcV-1 to allow communication of key Resource Flows to non-technical stakeholders.

Some Resources can carry out service functions (activities) as described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] models and these functions can optionally be overlaid on a SvcV-1.

The SvcV-1 depicts all Resource Flows between resources that are of interest. Note that Resource Flows between resources may be further specified in detail in the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-2 Services Resource Flow Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] model.

394841327

## PAGE 01141: SvcV-10a Services Parametric

- page_id: `227155510`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227155510/SvcV-10a+Services+Parametric
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-02T10:30:24.226+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint > SvcV-10a Services Rules Model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

497450681

497450683

497450682

**Description**

A Parametric diagram includes the usage of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Block' space='SYSMLP2024xR3'] to constrain the properties of another Service Specification. It contains [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Property' space='SYSMLP2024xR3'] and constraint parameters as well as other properties from within that internal block context. All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a constraint parameter or contain a property that is bound to a constraint parameter (through any number of containment levels). A constraint block generally contain many constraints, each of them containing many constraint parameters.

497450680

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Parametric diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

It contains <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR3" ri:content-title="Constraint Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[constraint properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and constraint parameters as well as other properties from within that internal block context. All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a constraint parameter or contain a property that is bound to a constraint parameter (through any number of containment levels). A constraint block generally contain many constraints, each of them containing many constraint parameters.</p><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a693f2ce-e38f-4839-af40-e1a65d49e6b7"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">497450680</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Parametric diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227155506 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01142: SvcV-10a Services Rules Model

- page_id: `227155506`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227155506/SvcV-10a+Services+Rules+Model
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-02T10:30:24.069+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint
- labels: ['svcv-10a-services-rules-model', 'svcv-10a']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

397362153

397362155

397362154

**Description**

The SvcV-10a is to specify functional and nonfunctional constraints on the implementation aspects of the architecture (that is, the structural and behavioral elements of the Services Model).

The SvcV-10a describes constraints on the resources, functions, data and ports that make up the Service Model.

The constraints are specified in text and may be functional or structural (that is, non-functional).

The intended usage of the SvcV-10a includes:

- Definition of implementation logic.
- Identification of resource constraints.

**Implementation**

SvcV-10a can be represented using:

- SvcV-10a table. [ATTACHMENT filename='svcv_10a.png']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-10a Services Parametric' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] .
- SvcV-10a spreadsheet report.

In contrast to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-6a Operational Rules Model' space='UAF12P2024xR3'], the SvcV-10a focuses physical and data constraints rather than business rules.

**Constraint owner**

You can choose where all the constraints will be stored through the**Project Options**.

To select a constraint owner

1. In the main menu, click Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. Go to General > UAF , and in the Default Constraint Owners property group, choose a needed owner.

397362152

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SvcV-10a table' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-10a Services Parametric' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4221bc57-0d15-4029-b15f-7fa7fd576e38"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">397362153</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="facbb14a-0821-4de4-8523-6e1e287c484f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">397362155</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d6fe72fb-c1ab-4e17-a80d-fe6b500fb83f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">397362154</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The SvcV-10a is to specify functional and nonfunctional constraints on the implementation aspects of the architecture (that is, the structural and behavioral elements of the Services Model).</p><p>The SvcV-10a describes constraints on the resources, functions, data and ports that make up the Service Model.</p><p>The constraints are specified in text and may be functional or structural (that is, non-functional).</p><p>The intended usage of the SvcV-10a includes:</p><ul><li>Definition of implementation logic.</li><li>Identification of resource constraints.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SvcV-10a can be represented using:</p><ul><li>SvcV-10a table.<br /><ac:image ac:title="SvcV-10a Services Rules Model" ac:alt="SvcV-10a Services Rules Model"><ri:attachment ri:filename="svcv_10a.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-10a Services Rules Model" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-10a Services Parametric" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SvcV-10a Services Parametric diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>SvcV-10a spreadsheet report.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>In contrast to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-6a Operational Rules Model" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link>, the SvcV-10a focuses physical and data constraints rather than business rules.</p><p style=""><strong>Constraint owner</strong></p><p style="">You can choose where all the constraints will be stored through the<span> </span><strong>Project Options</strong>.</p><p style="">To select a constraint owner</p><hr style="" /><ol style=""><li>In the main menu, click<span> </span><strong>Options</strong><span> </span>&gt;<span> </span><strong>Project</strong>. The<span> </span><strong>Project Options</strong><span> </span>dialog opens.</li><li>Go to<span> </span><strong>General</strong><span> </span>&gt;<span> </span><strong>UAF</strong>, and in the<span> </span><strong>Default Constraint Owners</strong><span> </span>property group, choose a needed owner.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="74274b3b-6dd1-4bbb-9e1d-10860fd94370"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">397362152</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" ri:content-title="Constraint" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating SvcV-10a table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-10a Services Parametric" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227155511 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01143: SvcV-10b Services State Transition Description

- page_id: `227155511`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227155511/SvcV-10b+Services+State+Transition+Description
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-02T10:30:24.367+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint
- labels: ['svcv-10b-services-state-transition-description', 'svcv-10b']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

397511127

397511129

397511128

**Description**

The SvcV-10b is a graphical method of describing a resource (or function) response to various events by changing its state. The diagram basically represents the sets of events to which the resources in the Activities respond (by taking an action to move to a new state) as a function of its current state. Each transition specifies an event and an action.

The explicit time sequencing of service functions in response to external and internal events is not fully expressed in SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description. SvcV-10b can be used to describe the explicit sequencing of the service functions. Alternatively, SvcV-10b can be used to reflect explicit sequencing of the actions internal to a single service function, or the sequencing of service functions with respect to a specific resource.

The intended usage of the SvcV-10b includes:

- Definition of states, events, and state transitions (behavioral modeling).
- Identification of constraints.

**Implementation**

SvcV-10b can be represented using a UML State Machine diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The SvcV-10b can be used to describe the detailed sequencing of service functions described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3']. However, the relationship between the actions included in SvcV-10b and the functions in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] depends on the purposes of the Architectural Description and the level of abstraction used in the models. The explicit sequencing of functions in response to external and internal events is not fully expressed in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3']. SvcV-10b can be used to reflect explicit sequencing of the functions, the sequencing of actions internal to a single function, or the sequencing of functions with respect to a specific resource.

397511126

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State' space='MD2024xR3']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Allocate Activities to State Internal Behaviors' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The diagram basically represents the sets of events to which the resources in the Activities respond (by taking an action to move to a new state) as a function of its current state. Each transition specifies an event and an action.</p><p>The explicit time sequencing of service functions in response to external and internal events is not fully expressed in SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description. SvcV-10b can be used to describe the explicit sequencing of the service functions. Alternatively, SvcV-10b can be used to reflect explicit sequencing of the actions internal to a single service function, or the sequencing of service functions with respect to a specific resource.</p><p>The intended usage of the SvcV-10b includes:</p><ul><li>Definition of states, events, and state transitions (behavioral modeling).</li><li>Identification of constraints.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SvcV-10b can be represented using a UML State Machine diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="SvcV-10b Services State Transition Description" ac:alt="SvcV-10b Services State Transition Description"><ri:attachment ri:filename="svcv_10b.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-10b Services State Transition Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The SvcV-10b can be used to describe the detailed sequencing of service functions described in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link>. However, the relationship between the actions included in <span class="confluence-link">SvcV-10b</span> and the functions in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SvcV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> depends on the purposes of the Architectural Description and the level of abstraction used in the models. The explicit sequencing of functions in response to external and internal events is not fully expressed in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link>. SvcV-10b can be used to reflect explicit sequencing of the functions, the sequencing of actions internal to a single function, or the sequencing of functions with respect to a specific resource.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="afe2bd9a-4e31-48a7-9bac-52694c82cc01"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">397511126</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" ri:content-title="State" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Allocate Activities to State Internal Behaviors" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227155513 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01144: SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description

- page_id: `227155513`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227155513/SvcV-10c+Services+Event-Trace+Description
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-02T10:30:24.520+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint
- labels: ['svcv-10c-services-event-trace-description', 'svcv-10c']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

397642427

397642429

397642428

**Description**

The SvcV-10c provides a time ordered examination of the interactions between services functional resources. Each event-trace diagram should have an accompanying description that defines the particular scenario or situation.

The SvcV-10c is valuable for moving to the next level of detail from the initial solution design, to help define a sequence of service functions and service data interfaces, and to ensure that each participating resource or Service Port role has the necessary information it needs, at the right time, to perform its assigned functionality.

The intended usage of the SvcV-10c includes:

- Analysis of resource events impacting operation.
- Behavioral analysis.
- Identification of non-functional system requirements.

**Implementation**

SvcV-10c can be represented using a UML Sequence diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The SvcV-10c is typically used in conjunction with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-10b Services State Transition Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] to describe the dynamic behavior of resources. The data content of messages that connect Resource Flows in a SvcV-10c model may be related, in modeling terms, with Resource Flows (interactions, in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-1 Services Context Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-3a Systems-Services Matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR3'], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-3b Services-Services Matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR3']), Resource Flows (data, in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3']) and information elements (in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='DIV-3 Physical Data Model' space='UAF12P2024xR3']) modeled in other models.

397642426

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction' space='MD2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Interface' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Message' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Method' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Resource Exchange in SvcV-10c diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

The data content of messages that connect Resource Flows in a SvcV-10c model may be related, in modeling terms, with Resource Flows (interactions, in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-1 Services Context Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-3a Systems-Services Matrix" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link>, and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-3b Services-Services Matrix" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link>), Resource Flows (data, in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link><span class="confluence-link"> </span>) and information elements (in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="DIV-3 Physical Data Model" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link>) modeled in other models.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b7d22c24-6109-4d78-8000-db93634c03a1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">397642426</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" ri:content-title="Interaction" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service Interface" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service Message" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service Method" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating Resource Exchange in SvcV-10c diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227155517 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01145: SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description BPD

- page_id: `227155517`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227155517/SvcV-10c+Services+Event-Trace+Description+BPD
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-15T08:42:37.782+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description (BPD) diagram is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD). It describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the businesswork. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed businessactivities.

SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description (BPD) diagram is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD). It describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">business </span>work. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">business </span>activities.</p><p>SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227155460 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01146: SvcV-2 Services Resource Flow Description

- page_id: `227155460`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227155460/SvcV-2+Services+Resource+Flow+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-02-21T11:22:09.971+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint
- labels: ['svcv-2-services-resource-flow-description', 'svcv-2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

395159322

395159324

395159323

**Description**

A SvcV-2 specifies the Resource Flows between Service Specificationsand may also list the protocol stacks used in connections.

A SvcV-2 DoDAF-described Model is used to give a precise specification of a connection between Service Specifications. This may be an existing connection or a specification of a connection that is to be made for a future connection.

The intended usage of the SvcV-2 includes:

- Resource Flow specification.

**Implementation**

SvcV-2 can be represented using:

- SvcV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- SvcV-2 Internal Block diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Any protocol referred to in a SvcV-2 diagram needs be defined in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='StdV-1 Standards Profile' space='UAF12P2024xR3']StdV-1 Standards Profile.

395159321

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Port' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Interface' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Exhibits' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Function' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Is Capable To Perform' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Standard' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Connector' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Artifact' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Post' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Person' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SvcV-2 Internal diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227155465 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01147: SvcV-3a Systems-Services Matrix

- page_id: `227155465`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227155465/SvcV-3a+Systems-Services+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-09-23T12:14:13.465+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint
- labels: ['svcv-3a-systems-services-matrix', 'svcv-3a']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

395570067

395570069

395570068

**Description**

A SvcV-3a enables a quick overview of all the system-to-service resource interactions specified in one or more [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-1 Services Context Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] models. The SvcV-3a provides a tabular summary of the system and services interactions specified in the SvcV-1 Services Context Description for the Architectural Description. This model can be useful in support existing systems that are transitioning to provide services. The matrix format supports a rapid assessment of potential commonalities and redundancies (or, if fault-tolerance is desired, the lack of redundancies).

The SvcV-3a can be organized in a number of ways to emphasize the association of system-to-service interactions in context with the architecture’s purpose.

The intended usage of the SvcV-3a includes:

- Summarizing system and service resource interactions.
- Interface management.
- Comparing interoperability characteristics of solution options.

**Implementation**

SvcV-3a can be represented using a SvcV-3a diagram which is non editable Dependency Matrix.

The SvcV-3a provides a tabular summary of the systemand servicesinteractions specified in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-1 Services Context Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3']. Each Resource Exchangelisted in the SvcV-3a table should be traceable to Operational Exchange listed in the corresponding [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR3']. Each Resource Exchangemay relate to a known Function (from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3']) that produces or consumes it. DataElements as well as the other type of Resource ExchangeItems are exchanged by Service Providers and Service Requesters represented in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR3']SV-6 table.

395570066

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Information' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SvcV-3a matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
