<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227155467 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01148: SvcV-3b Services-Services Matrix

- page_id: `227155467`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227155467/SvcV-3b+Services-Services+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-10-25T13:34:42.512+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint
- labels: ['svcv-3b-services-services-matrix', 'svcv-3b']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

396077917

396077919

396077918

**Description**

A SvcV-3b enables a quick overview of all the services resource interactions specified in one or more SvcV-1 Services Context Description models. The SvcV-3b provides a tabular summary of the services interactions specified in the SvcV-1 Services Context Description for the Architectural Description. The matrix format supports a rapid assessment of potential commonalities and redundancies (or, if fault-tolerance is desired, the lack of redundancies). In addition, this model is useful in support of net-centric (service-oriented) implementation of services as an input to the SvcV-10a Services Rules Model, SvcV-10b Services State Transition Description, and SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description, implemented as orchestrations of services.

The SvcV-3b can be organized in a number of ways to emphasize the association of service pairs in context with the architecture’s purpose. One type of organization is a Service Hierarchy or Taxonomy of Services.

The intended usage of the SvcV-3b includes:

- Summarizing service resource interactions.
- Interface management.
- Comparing interoperability characteristics of solution options.

**Implementation**

SvcV-3b can be represented using a SvcV-3b diagram which is non editable Dependency Matrix.

This model is useful in support of net-centric (service-oriented) implementation of services as an input to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-10a Services Rules Model' space='UAF12P2024xR3'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-10b Services State Transition Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3'], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3'], implemented as orchestrations of services.

396077916

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Exchange' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SvcV-3b matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

The SvcV-3b provides a tabular summary of the services interactions specified in the SvcV-1 Services Context Description for the Architectural Description. The matrix format supports a rapid assessment of potential commonalities and redundancies (or, if fault-tolerance is desired, the lack of redundancies). In addition, this model is useful in support of net-centric (service-oriented) implementation of services as an input to the SvcV-10a Services Rules Model, SvcV-10b Services State Transition Description, and SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description, implemented as orchestrations of services.</p><p>The SvcV-3b can be organized in a number of ways to emphasize the association of service pairs in context with the architecture’s purpose. One type of organization is a Service Hierarchy or Taxonomy of Services.</p><p>The intended usage of the SvcV-3b includes:</p><ul><li>Summarizing service resource interactions.</li><li>Interface management.</li><li>Comparing interoperability characteristics of solution options.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SvcV-3b can be represented using a SvcV-3b diagram which is non editable Dependency Matrix.</p><p>This model is useful in support of net-centric (service-oriented) implementation of services as an input to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-10a Services Rules Model" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SvcV- 10a Services Rules Model]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-10b Services State Transition Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link>, and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link>, implemented as orchestrations of services.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2284c6e9-45c1-4164-ab73-42370aceb193"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">396077916</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service Exchange" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating SvcV-3b matrix" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227155469 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01149: SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description

- page_id: `227155469`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227155469/SvcV-4+Services+Functionality+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2019-07-24T14:12:46.950+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint
- labels: ['svcv-4-services-functionality-description', 'svcv-4']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

396204750

396204752

396204751

**Description**

The SvcV-4 DoDAF-described Model addresses human and service functionality.

The primary purpose of SvcV-4 is to:

- Develop a clear description of the necessary data flows that are input (consumed) by and output (produced) by each resource.
- Ensure that the service functional connectivity is complete (i.e., that a resource’s required inputs are all satisfied).
- Ensure that the functional decomposition reaches an appropriate level of detail.

The Services Functionality Description provides detailed information regarding the:

- Allocation of service functions to resources.
- Flow of resources between service functions.

The SvcV-4 is the Services Viewpoint counterpart to the OV-5b Operational Activity Model of the Operational Viewpoint.

The intended usage of the SvcV-4 includes:

- Description of task workflow.
- Identification of functional service requirements.
- Functional decomposition of Services.
- Relate human and service functions.

**Implementation**

SvcV-4 can be represented using:

- SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='svcv_4.png']
- SvcV-4 Services Functionality Flow Description diagram which is based on the UML Activity diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='svcv_4_flow.png']

The SvcV-4 is the behavioral counterpart to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-1 Services Context Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] (in the same way that [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] is the behavioral counterpart to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3']).

396204748

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Function' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Function Action' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SvcV-4 Services Functionality Flow Description diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Process Flow Diagrams From Compositions or Aggregations Defined in Process Diagrams' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227155476 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01150: SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description BPD

- page_id: `227155476`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227155476/SvcV-4+Services+Functionality+Description+BPD
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-15T08:18:59.284+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description (BPD) diagram is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD). It describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the businesswork. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed businessactivities.

SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description (BPD) diagram is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD). It describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">business </span>work. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">business </span>activities.</p><p>SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227155477 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01151: SvcV-5 Services to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix

- page_id: `227155477`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227155477/SvcV-5+Services+to+Operational+Activity+Traceability+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-04-09T15:35:57.175+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint
- labels: ['svcv-5-operational-activity-to-services-traceability-matrix', 'svcv-5']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The SvcV-5 addresses the linkage between service functions described in SvcV-4 and Operational Activities specified in OV-5a Operational Activity Decomposition Tree or OV-5b Operational Activity Model. The SvcV-5 depicts the mapping of service functions (and, optionally, the capabilities and performers that provide them) to operational activities and thus identifies the transformation of an operational need into a purposeful action performed by a service solution. During requirements definition, the SvcV-5 plays a particularly important role in tracing the architectural elements associated with system function requirements to those associated with user requirements.

The intended usage of the SvcV-5 includes:

- Tracing service functional requirements to user requirements.
- Tracing solution options to requirements.
- Identification of overlaps or gaps.

**Implementation**

SvcV-5 can be represented using:

- The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with SvcV-5 matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] describes the mapping between Service s and Operational Activities directly or using the implied relations.

DoDAF uses the term Operational Activity in the OVs and the term Service Function in the SVs to refer to essentially the same kind of thing—both activities and service functions are tasks that are performed, accept inputs, and develop outputs. The distinction between an Operational Activity and a Service Function is a question of what and how. The Operational Activity is a specification of what is to be done, regardless of the mechanism used. A Service Function specifies how a resource carries it out. For this reason, the SvcV-5 is a significant model, as it ties together the logical specification in the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating OV-5a Operational Activity Model diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] with the physical specification of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3']. Service Functions can be carried out by Resources.

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Is Capable To Perform' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Function' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Activity' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Supports' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

**Related procedures**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The SvcV-5 addresses the linkage between service functions described in SvcV-4 and Operational Activities specified in OV-5a Operational Activity Decomposition Tree or OV-5b Operational Activity Model. The SvcV-5 depicts the mapping of service functions (and, optionally, the capabilities and performers that provide them) to operational activities and thus identifies the transformation of an operational need into a purposeful action performed by a service solution. During requirements definition, the SvcV-5 plays a particularly important role in tracing the architectural elements associated with system function requirements to those associated with user requirements.</p><p>The intended usage of the SvcV-5 includes:</p><ul><li>Tracing service functional requirements to user requirements.</li><li>Tracing solution options to requirements.</li><li>Identification of overlaps or gaps.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SvcV-5 can be represented using:</p><ul><li>The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with SvcV-5 matrix" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SvcV-5 Services to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><span> </span></span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">describes the mapping between </span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Service</span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">s and Operational Activities directly or using the implied relations.</span></li></ul><p>DoDAF uses the term Operational Activity in the OVs and the term Service Function in the SVs to refer to essentially the same kind of thing—both activities and service functions are tasks that are performed, accept inputs, and develop outputs. The distinction between an Operational Activity and a Service Function is a question of what and how. The Operational Activity is a specification of what is to be done, regardless of the mechanism used. A Service Function specifies how a resource carries it out. For this reason, the SvcV-5 is a significant model, as it ties together the logical specification in the<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating OV-5a Operational Activity Model diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ OV-5a Operational Activity Decomposition Tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-5b Operational Activity Model]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> with the physical specification of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link>. Service Functions can be carried out by Resources.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Is Capable To Perform" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service Function" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Activity" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Supports" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="3e634151-caf8-4f10-a97d-222f92b26569" /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227155481 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01152: SvcV-5 Services to Service Contracts Mapping Matrix

- page_id: `227155481`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227155481/SvcV-5+Services+to+Service+Contracts+Mapping+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-04-09T15:49:06.961+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **SvcV-5 Services to Service Contracts Mapping matrix** depicts mapping between the Services or their specializations and the Service Contracts they govern.

The Rows of this matrix are Services or their specializations,and the Columns are Service Contracts.

To create an SvcV-5 Services to Service Contracts Mapping Matrix

1. Specify**Row Scope** (Services or their specializations);
2. Specify**Column Scope** (Service Contracts);
3. Click[IMAGE alt='' src=''].

Services or their specializations map to Service Contracts using a Governed By relationship.

To mapServices or their specializations to Service Contracts

1. Double-click an empty intersection.

To remove the relation

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>SvcV-5 Services to Service Contracts Mapping matrix</strong> <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">depicts mapping between the Services or their specializations and the Service Contracts they govern.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><span>The Rows of this matrix are Services or their specializations,</span><span> and the Columns are Service Contracts</span><span>.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><span>To create an SvcV-5 Services to Service Contracts Mapping Matrix</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Specify</span><strong> Row Scope</strong> (Services or their specializations);</span></li><li><span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Specify </span><strong>Column Scope</strong> (Service Contracts);</span></li><li><span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click </span><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper" style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-5 Services to Service Contracts Mapping Matrix" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></span>.</span></li></ol><p> Services <span>or their specializations</span> map to Service Contracts using a Governed By relationship.</p><p><br /></p><p>To map<span> Services or their specializations to Service Contracts </span></p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click an empty intersection.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To remove the relation</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227155485 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01153: SvcV-6 Role-based Services Resource Flow Matrix

- page_id: `227155485`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227155485/SvcV-6+Role-based+Services+Resource+Flow+Matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-02T10:30:23.929+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Role-based Services Resource Flow Matrix** (SvcV-6) specifies the characteristics of the data exchanged between service roles.

##### Adding existing Service Exchanges

To add an existing Service Exchange

1. In the Role-based Services Resource Flow Matrix toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Elements dialog opens.
2. Double-click Service Exchange or Service Connector from the list to add them to the table. For multiple selection, click the **Multiple Selection** button in the right bottom of the dialog, select Service Exchange / Service Connector and click **+** button.In case a Service Connector is selected, all the Service Exchanges flowing via it will be added to the table.
3. Click OK when you are done.

- Service Exchange Identifier, Service Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and a wide range of measurements cells are editable in the table.
- Other cells are read-only.
- To create and modify the Service Interactions, you can use Services Structure (Sv-Sr) diagram.

##### Generating Reports

The Role-based Services Resource Flow Matrix has a predefined report available. You can generate the report:

- Directly from the table toolbar, clicking the **Report**button,
- Using a quick report option from the Containment tree
- Through the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report Wizard' space='MD2024xR3']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Role-based Services Resource Flow Matrix report

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE


<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227155483 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01154: SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix

- page_id: `227155483`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227155483/SvcV-6+Services+Resource+Flow+Matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-02T10:30:23.832+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**The SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix** addresses service exchanges between services.

You can add any available kind (such as Information Exchange, Materiel Exchange, Organizational Exchange, Energy Exchange) of Service Exchanges in this table.

##### Adding new Service Exchanges

To add new Service Exchange

1. In the SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix toolbar, click the Add New button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Service Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR3']**Realized Services Exchange** wizard opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.
3. Click Finish when you are done. The rows with created Service Exchanges are added to the table.

##### Adding existing Service Exchanges

To add an existing Service Exchange

1. In the SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Service Exchange dialog opens.
2. Select Service Exchanges by clicking the + button.
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Service Exchanges are added to the table.

- Service Exchange Identifier, Service Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and a wide range of measurement cells are editable in the table.
- Other cells are read-only.
- To create and modify the Service Exchanges, you can use Services Taxonomy (Sv-Tx) diagram.
- You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR3']**Service Exchange Manager** dialogthat enables you to analyze and manage the Service Exchange data.

##### Adding Service Functions

To add Service Functions

1. In the SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix, click a Service Exchange row for which you want to add Service Functions.
2. In the table toolbar, click the Service Functions button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Producing and Consuming Functions dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] dialog opens.
3. In the open dialog, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Producing and Consuming Functions dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] .
4. Click OK .

##### Generating Reports

The Services Resource Flow Matrix has a predefined report available. You can generate the report:

- Directly from the table toolbar, clicking the **Report**button,
- Using a quick report option from the Containment tree
- Through the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report Wizard' space='MD2024xR3']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Services Resource Flow Matrix report

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>The SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix</strong> addresses service exchanges between services.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f572216b-74e7-460e-bb6a-59e6ea7a4ac5"><ac:rich-text-body>You can add any available kind (such as Information Exchange, Materiel Exchange, Organizational Exchange, Energy Exchange) of Service Exchanges in this table.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Adding <span class="confluence-link">new</span> Service Exchanges</h3><p>To add new Service Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>In the SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix toolbar, click the <strong>Add New </strong>button. You can generate the report:</span></p><ul><li><span>Directly from the table toolbar, clicking the <strong>Report </strong>button,</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Using a quick report option from the Containment tree</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Through the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" ri:content-title="Report Wizard" /></ac:link></span></li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="svcv-6_service_resource_flow_matrix_report.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">An example of the Services Resource Flow Matrix report</span></h6><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227155487 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01155: SvcV-7 Services Measures Matrix

- page_id: `227155487`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227155487/SvcV-7+Services+Measures+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-03-03T12:41:04.588+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint
- labels: ['svcv-7-services-measures-matrix', 'svcv-7']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

396906475

396906478

396906477

**Description**

The SvcV-7 depicts the measures (metrics) of resources. The Services Measures Matrix expands on the information presented in an SvcV-1 Services Context Description by depicting the characteristics of the resources in the SvcV-1 Services Context Description.

In addition, this model is useful in support of the net-centric (service-oriented) implementation of services. Service measures for Service Level Agreements for each service and may include a number of service consumers, service usage by consumers, and the minimum, average and maximum response times, allowed downtime, etc. Measures of interest for a Chief Information Office or Program manager may include measures that assess service reuse, process efficiency, and business agility.

The intended usage of the SvcV-7 includes:

- Definition of performance characteristics and measures (metrics).
- Identification of non-functional requirements.

**Implementation**

SvcV-7 can be represented using:

- SvcV-7 Typical measures table [ATTACHMENT filename='svcv_7_typical.png']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with hierarchical Typical Measurements table' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with hierarchical Actual Measurements table' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- SvcV-7 Actual measures table [ATTACHMENT filename='svcv_7_actual.png']
- SvcV-7 actual measures spreadsheet report

396906474

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Measurement' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

Service measures for Service Level Agreements for each service and may include a number of service consumers, service usage by consumers, and the minimum, average and maximum response times, allowed downtime, etc. Measures of interest for a Chief Information Office or Program manager may include measures that assess service reuse, process efficiency, and business agility.</p><p>The intended usage <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227155492 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01156: SvcV-8 Services Evolution Description

- page_id: `227155492`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227155492/SvcV-8+Services+Evolution+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-15T08:31:51.696+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint
- labels: ['svcv-8-services-evolution-description', 'svcv-8']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

397044898

397044900

397044899

**Description**

The SvcV-8 presents a whole lifecycle view of resources (services), describing how it changes over time. It shows the structure of several resources mapped against a timeline.

In addition, this model is useful in support of net-centric (service-oriented) implementation of services. This model can present a timeline of services evolve or are replaced over time, including services that are internal and external to the scope of the architecture.

The intended usage of the SvcV-8 includes:

- Development of incremental acquisition strategy.
- Planning technology insertion.

**Implementation**

The SvcV-8 view can be represented using a SvcV-8 Services Evolution Description diagram which is realized as a time based diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

397044897

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227155501 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01157: SvcV-9 Services Technology and Skills Forecast

- page_id: `227155501`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227155501/SvcV-9+Services+Technology+and+Skills+Forecast
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-15T08:34:52.800+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint
- labels: ['svcv-9-services-technology-and-skills-forecast', 'svcv-9']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

397238836

397238838

397238837

**Description**

The SvcV-9 defines the underlying current and expected supporting technologies and skills. Expected supporting technologies and skills are those that can be reasonably forecast given the current state of technology and skills, and expected improvements or trends. New technologies and skills are tied to specific time periods, which can correlate against the time periods used in SvcV-8 Services Evolution Description model milestones and linked to Capability Phases.

The SvcV-9 provides a summary of emerging technologies and skills that impact the architecture. The SvcV-9 provides descriptions of relevant:

- Emerging capabilities.
- Industry trends.
- Predictions (with associated confidence factors) of the availability and readiness of specific hardware and software services.
- Current and possible future skills.

In addition to providing an inventory of trends, capabilities and services, the SvcV-9 also includes an assessment of the potential impact of these items on the architecture. Given the future-oriented nature of this model, forecasts are typically made in short, mid and long-term timeframes, such as 6, 12 and 18-month intervals. In addition, this model is useful in support of net-centric (service-oriented) implementation of services. As technologies change, like incorporation of Representational State Transfer (REST) services in the Web Services Description Language, this model can present a timeline of technologies related services over time.

The intended usage of the SvcV-9 includes:

- Forecasting technology readiness against time.
- HR Trends Analysis.
- Recruitment Planning.
- Planning technology insertion.
- Input to options analysis.

**Implementation**

SvcV-9 can be represented using a SvcV-9 table.

The specific time periods selected (and the trends being tracked) can be coordinated with architecture transition plans (which the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-8 Services Evolution Description' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] can support). That is, insertion of new capabilities and upgrading or re-training of existing resources may depend on or be driven by the availability of new technology and associated skills.

If standards are an integral part of the technologies important to the evolution of a given architecture, then it may be convenient to combine SvcV-9 with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='StdV-2 Standards Forecast' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] into a composite Fit-for-Purpose View.

397238834

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Forecast' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SvcV-9 table' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

Expected supporting technologies and skills are those that can be reasonably forecast given the current state of technology and skills, and expected improvements or trends. New technologies and skills are tied to specific time periods, which can correlate against the time periods used in SvcV-8 Services Evolution Description model milestones and linked to Capability Phases.</p><p>The SvcV-9 provides a summary of emerging technologies and skills that impact the architecture. The SvcV-9 provides descriptions of relevant:</p><ul><li>Emerging capabilities.</li><li>Industry trends.</li><li>Predictions (with associated confidence factors) of the availability and readiness of specific hardware and software services.</li><li>Current and possible future skills.</li></ul><p>In addition to providing an inventory of trends, capabilities and services, the SvcV-9 also includes an assessment of the potential impact of these items on the architecture. Given the future-oriented nature of this model, forecasts are typically made in short, mid and long-term timeframes, such as 6, 12 and 18-month intervals. In addition, this model is useful in support of net-centric (service-oriented) implementation of services. As technologies change, like incorporation of Representational State Transfer (REST) services in the Web Services Description Language, this model can present a timeline of technologies related services over time.</p><p>The intended usage of the SvcV-9 includes:</p><ul><li>Forecasting technology readiness against time.</li><li>HR Trends Analysis.</li><li>Recruitment Planning.</li><li>Planning technology insertion.</li><li>Input to options analysis.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SvcV-9 can be represented using a SvcV-9 table.</p><p>The specific time periods selected (and the trends being tracked) can be coordinated with architecture transition plans (which the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-8 Services Evolution Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link> can support). <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227155320 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01158: Switching between projects with different AF related perspectives

- page_id: `227155320`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227155320/Switching+between+projects+with+different+AF+related+perspectives
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-05-11T13:15:59.842+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Getting started > Customizing and Selecting Perspective
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A particular behavior is applied when switching between projects with different Architecture Framework (AF) related perspectives.When you work with two projects using different perspectives than those projects were created with and switch between them, a message is displayed.You can choose whether to change the perspective according to the project's framework or the latest used perspective in that message.

In the following table, see the cases of the actions required when switching between projects A and B.

| Actions of Project A | Project A perspective according to AF | The last used perspective of Project A | Actions of Project B | Project B perspective according to AF | The last used perspective of Project B | Required actions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Create Project A | UAF | - | Create project B | MODAF | - | The project you are opening/creating requires the MODAF perspective. The perspective will be changed to MODAF automatically. (In the example below, Case #1) |
| Open Project A | UAF | - | Open project B | MODAF | - | The project you are opening/creating requires the MODAF perspective. The perspective will be changed to MODAF automatically. (In the example below, Case #1) |
| Migrate/Open/Create Project A | UAF | - | Migrate project B | MODAF | - | The project you are opening/creating requires the MODAF perspective. The perspective will be changed to MODAF automatically.(In the example below, Case #1) |
| Create Project A | UAF | - | Create project B | UAF | - | No actions |
| Open project A | UAF | - | Open project B | UAF | - | No actions |
| Migrate/Open/Create Project A | UAF | - | Migrate project B | UAF | - | No actions |
| Create Project A | UAF | MODAF | Create project B | MODAF | - | No actions |
| Open Project A | UAF | MODAF | Open project B | MODAF | - | No actions |
| Switch to opened Project A | UAF | MODAF | Switch to opened project B | UAF | - | The project you are opening/creating requires the UAF perspective. The perspective will be changed to UAF automatically.(In the example below, Case #1) |
| Switch to opened Project A | UAF | - | Switch to opened project B | NAF | - | The project you are opening/creating requires the NAF perspective. The perspective will be changed to NAF automatically.(In the example below, Case #1) |
| Switch to opened Project A | UAF | MODAF | Switch to opened project B | NAF | DoDAF 2 | The project you are opening requires the NAF perspective.To continue, choose one of the following:OK to switch the perspective to NAF (according to the framework).Cancel to switch the perspective to DoDAF 2(the latest used perspective).(In the example below, Case #2) |
| Switch to opened Project A | UAF | MODAF | Switch to opened project B | MODAF | DoDAF 2 | The project you are opening requires the MODAF perspective.To continue, choose one of the following:OK to switch the perspective to MODAF (according to the framework).Cancel to switch the perspective to DoDAF 2 (the latest used perspective).(In the example below, Case #2) |

##### Examples of messages

Here are examples of messages you receive when switching projects with different perspectives.

**Case #1.**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Case #2.**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227158146 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01159: SysML Compliance mode changes

- page_id: `227158146`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227158146/SysML+Compliance+mode+changes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-02-20T07:37:20.387+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual > UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.1 > Mapping issues and solutions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Using the UAF Profile, the SysML Compliance mode is no longer used, because the elements are inherited from the SysML. If the UPDM2 project was using SysML Compliance mode, after the migration, the Tags from the earlier used SysML elements are copied to the same location of the UAF elements.

The following elements will have two stereotypes (UAF stereotype and SysML PartProperty stereotype):

- Capability Property
- Concept Role
- Known Resource
- Operational Role
- Problem Domain
- Resource Role
- Structural Part
- Temporal Part

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Using the UAF Profile, the SysML Compliance mode is no longer used, because the elements are inherited from the SysML. If the UPDM2 project was using SysML Compliance mode, after the migration, the Tags from the earlier used SysML elements are copied to the same location of the UAF elements.</p><p>The following elements will have two stereotypes (UAF stereotype  and SysML PartProperty stereotype):</p><ul><li>Capability Property</li><li>Concept Role</li><li>Known Resource</li><li>Operational Role</li><li>Problem Domain</li><li>Resource Role</li><li>Structural Part</li><li>Temporal Part</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227158126 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01160: SysML Compliance mode changes in UAF 1.2

- page_id: `227158126`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227158126/SysML+Compliance+mode+changes+in+UAF+1.2
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-05-19T11:30:14.575+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual > UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.2 > UPDM 2.1 to UAF 1.2. Mapping issues and solutions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Using the UAF Profile, the SysML Compliance mode is no longer used, because the elements are inherited from the SysML. If the UPDM2 project was using SysML Compliance mode, after the migration, the Tags from the earlier used SysML elements are copied to the same location of the UAF elements.

The following elements will have two stereotypes (UAF stereotype and SysML PartProperty stereotype):

- Capability Property
- Concept Role
- Known Resource
- Operational Role
- Problem Domain
- Resource Role
- Structural Part
- Temporal Part

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Using the UAF Profile, the SysML Compliance mode is no longer used, because the elements are inherited from the SysML. If the UPDM2 project was using SysML Compliance mode, after the migration, the Tags from the earlier used SysML elements are copied to the same location of the UAF elements.</p><p>The following elements will have two stereotypes (UAF stereotype  and SysML PartProperty stereotype):</p><ul><li>Capability Property</li><li>Concept Role</li><li>Known Resource</li><li>Operational Role</li><li>Problem Domain</li><li>Resource Role</li><li>Structural Part</li><li>Temporal Part</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227158037 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01161: System

- page_id: `227158037`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227158037/System
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T13:25:32.772+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

An integrated set of elements, subsystems, or assemblies that accomplish a defined objective. These elements include products (hardware, software, firmware), processes, people, information, techniques, facilities, services, and other support elements.

**Architecture** **Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Class

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Description</strong></p><p>An integrated set of elements, subsystems, or assemblies that accomplish a defined objective. These elements include products (hardware, software, firmware), processes, people, information, techniques, facilities, services, and other support elements.</p><p><strong>Architecture</strong> <strong>Framework</strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</span></p><p><strong>Extension</strong></p><p>UML Class</p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227155518 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01162: Systems viewpoint

- page_id: `227155518`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227155518/Systems+viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:12:18.932+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0
- labels: ['systems-viewpoint']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

404545554

404545557

404545555

“The DoDAF-described Models within the Systems Viewpoint describes systems and interconnections providing for, or supporting, DoD functions. DoD functions include both warfighting and business functions. The Systems Models associate systems resources to the operational and capability requirements. These systems resources support the operational activities and facilitate the exchange of information. The Systems DoDAF-described Models are available for support of legacy systems. As architectures are updated, they should transition from Systems to Services and utilize the models within the Services Viewpoint.” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]

The basic modeling principles are demonstrated in the demo**[***UPDM: Systems viewpoint*.](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qvL33ckmJJY)

Some elements in the demo are different from UAF Plugin but the modeling principles are the same.

404545552

**Views**

## PAGE 01164: Temporal Part

- page_id: `227158039`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227158039/Temporal+Part
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T13:25:09.872+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

Usage of an Enterprise Phase in the context of another Enterprise Phase. It asserts that one Enterprise Phase is a spatial part of another. Creates a whole-part relationship that represents the temporal structure of the Enterprise Phase.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Property

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">Usage of an Enterprise Phase in the context of another Enterprise Phase. It asserts that one Enterprise Phase is a spatial part of another. Creates a whole-part relationship that represents the temporal structure of the Enterprise Phase.</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong><span class="fontstyle0">Architecture Framework</span></strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</span></p><p><strong>Extension</strong></p><p>UML Property</p><p><br /></p>
````

## PAGE 01165: Time Periods dialog

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Button Visibility

1014964890

##### Time Periods

In the UPDM plugin, time periods are used to specify resources for the architecture against a timeline indicating when they are expected to be in use. Time periods can be defined when the subjects, or resources, of the forecast are already added in the table.As shown below, the**Technology area**column must be filled in with resources**before**adding a specific time period.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Time Periods Dialog

The **Time Periods** dialog is essential when defining time periods for the **forecast of the resources**.

The **Time Periods** dialog consists of three areas:

- **Toolbar** - This area allows you to control how properties in the dialog are displayed: Categorized view, Alphabetical view, Expand all, Collapse all.
- **Properties** - Provides a list of properties that are available to specify.
- Properties value cells - The fields where you can set values for each property.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Time Periods dialog

Due to the abundance of button in the dialog, the instructions are broken down into two parts: General instructions and Specific instructions. General instructions will explain how to[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Time Periods dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR3']the Time Periods dialog and how to approach the main part of the Time Periods dialog: the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Time Periods dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR3']. Specific instructions will explain how tospecify [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Forecast Kinds in Time Periods dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] in the dialog.

openTo open the **Time Periods** dialog

- Open the **Time Periods** dialog, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the table toolbar. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### NOTE: Button Visibility

Button Visibility

The button "Time Period" is visible **only** in the **Forecast** diagram or any diagram containing a forecast.

##### Specifying Timelines

When specifying timelines, you can either [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Time Periods dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] a pre-existing timeline or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Time Periods dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] a new one.

timelineTo specify the **Timeline**

There are two things you can do here:

- select Select a pre-existing Timeline.
  1. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='alt+down.png'] , or Alt+Down for a drop-down menu to make your selection. [ATTACHMENT filename='specify_timeline.png']
- newtimeline Create a new Timeline.
  1. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='three_dot_edit_small.png'] for a **Select Timeline** dialog.
  2. Click Creation Mode. [ATTACHMENT filename='turned_on_creation_mode.png']
  3. Select the appropriate package from the list in which you want to store a new Timeline.
  4. Click Create. (Shortcut: Right-click and click Create. ) The new Timeline's specification window opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='Create_timeline_button.png']
  5. Type the name of the new Timeline. Click Close. [ATTACHMENT filename='Specifying_name_of_new_timeline.png']
  6. Click OK.

1014964888

INLINE

**Sample Model:**

The model used in the figures on this page is the **DoDAF Sample**. To open this sample, go to*<modeling tool installation directory>\samples\UPDM\DoDAF Sample.mdzip*

create a new Timeline element."><ri:attachment ri:filename="Create_timeline_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Time Periods dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><span>Type the name of the new Timeline. Click </span><strong>Close.</strong><br /><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="The Specification window of newly created Timeline with Name property specified as Software Time Line." ac:alt="The Specification window of newly created Timeline with Name property specified as Software Time Line."><ri:attachment ri:filename="Specifying_name_of_new_timeline.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Time Periods dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Click <strong>OK. </strong></li></ol></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e45e65c0-15f0-4a24-8dd2-215837471655"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1014964888</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating P1 Technology Forecast table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating A8 Standards Forecast table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating StdV-2 table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating SvcV-9 table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating SV-9 table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f4094778-c6d2-419d-a9b2-5a6066673dca"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">579277482</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Sample Model:</strong></p><p>The model used in the figures on this page is the <strong>DoDAF Sample</strong>. To open this sample, <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">go to </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\samples\UPDM\DoDAF Sample.mdzip</em></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227157301 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01167: Traceability relation maps

- page_id: `227157301`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227157301/Traceability+relation+maps
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-03-26T15:03:49.254+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures > Managing traceability and impact analysis
- labels: ['traceability-relation-maps', 'capability-structural-map', 'capability-behavioral-map', 'implementation-map', 'operational-activity-implementation-map', 'operational-activity-map', 'organization-structure-map', 'system-resource-map', 'variant-map']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Relation Map diagram enables you to review and analyze relations rapidly between the elements of the whole model.

The UAF Plugin presents you a number of predefined relation maps. You will be able to change the context and scope.

You can change the context by dragging the selected element onto the map.

The predefined relation maps are the following:

##### Capability Behavioral Map

The **Capability Behavioral Map** depicts the behavioral relationships of a Capability. This predefined map includes the Capability itself, related Operational Activities (Operational Activity, Standard Operational Activity), related Functions (Function, Project Activity, Security Process), and related Service Functions. The Capability Behavioral Map also shows the internal decomposition of each mapped behavioral element, as well as the relationships among these elements.

##### Capability Structural Map

The **Capability Structural Map** depicts the structural relationships of a Capability. This predefined map includes the Capability itself, related Operational Agents (Operational Performer, Operational Architecture), Resource Performers (Capability Configuration, Organization, Software, and other elements), Services, Actual Resource, and Actual Service. The Capability Structural Map also shows the internal compositions of each mapped element, as well as the relationships among these elements.

##### Implementation Map

The **Implementation Map** depicts the implementation relationships of an Operational Performer. This predefined map includes the Operational Element (Operational Performer, Operational Architecture, Operational Interface, Operational Role, Operational Information), and the Resource Elements (Resource Performer, Resource Role, Resource Interface, Resource Information) that have been indicated as implementing that operational element. The Implementation Map also shows the internal compositions of each mapped element, as well as the relationships among these elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Operational Activity Decomposition Map

The **Operational Activity Decomposition Map** depicts the behavioral decomposition of an Operational Activity.

##### Operational Activity Implementation Map

The **Operational Activity Implementation Map** depicts the behavioral implementation of an Operational Activity, relating the Operational Activity and Functions that implement the operational behavior. When the Operational Activity has composite behaviors and Functions have composite functionality, the Operational Activity Implementation Map also includes the behavioral elements for the composed elements.

##### Operational Performer Impact Analysis Map

The **Operational Performer Impact Analysis Map** depicts the model elements influenced by the changes of the Operational Performer. This predefined map includes:

- Performs Operational Activity
- Exhibits Capability
- Context (Operational Roles, where Operational Performer is used as Context)
- Inputs and Outputs (Operational Performers connected using Operational Exchanges)
- Operational Exchanges

##### Operational Role Impact Analysis Map

The **Operational Role Impact Analysis Map**depicts the model elements influenced by the changes of the Operational Role. This predefined map includes:

- Context (Operational Performer that is the context of the Operational Role)
- Inputs and Outputs (Operational Roles connected using Operational Connectors)
- Performs In Context (Operational Activities performed by the Operational Role)
- Performs (by Type) (Operational Activities performed by the Type of the Operational Role)
- Exhibits Capability (by Type) (Capabilities exhibited by the Type of the Operational Role)

##### Organization Structure Map

The **Organization Structure Map** depicts the structural composition of an Actual Organization. The predefined map includes:

- Actual Organization itself
- Actual Posts
- Actual Persons

The Organization Structure Map illustrates the relationships between these organizational elements.

##### Resource Performer Map

The **Resource Performer Map** depicts the structural decomposition of a Resource Performer including Capability Configuration, Organization, Software, and other elements.

##### Resource Role Impact Analysis Map

The **Resource Role Impact Analysis Map** depicts the model elements influenced by the changes of the Resource Role. This predefined map includes:

- Context (Resource which is used as the context of the Resource Role)
- Inputs and Outputs (Resource Roles connected using Resource Connectors)
- Performs In Context (Functions performed by the Resource Role)
- Performs (by Type) (Function Actions performed by the Type of the Resource Role)
- Implements (by Type) (Operational Performers implemented by the Type of the Resource Role)
- Exhibits Capability (by Type) (Capabilities exhibited by the Type of the Resource Role)

##### Strategic Driver Map

**The Strategic Driver Map (St-Mv)**depicts the relationships of a Driver.

This predefined map includes the Driver, Enterprise Goals, and/or Enterprise Objectives that are related to a Driver using the Motivated By relationship, and Actual Strategic Phases that are related to the Enterprise Goals or Enterprise Objectives using the Phases relationship.

The Context of the relation map can be either a Driver or a Package. The Relationship Criteria include Owned Element (if the Context Element is Package), Motivated By, Phases, Is Composed of Enterprise Goals, and Is Composed of Enterprise Objectives. The Element Type includes Driver, Enterprise Goal, Enterprise Objective, and Actual Strategic Phase.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Variant Map

The **Variant Map** depicts the specializations of a Resource Performer, including Capability Configuration, Software, and other elements. Specializations of a Resource Performer are modeled using the Generalization relationship.

**Related procedures**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>A Relation Map diagram enables you to review and analyze relations rapidly between the elements of the whole model.</p><p>The UAF Plugin presents you a number of predefined relation maps. You will be able to change the context and scope.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a815edba-6ddc-4108-9f70-02174da37c82"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can change the context by dragging the selected element onto the map.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="confluence-link">The predefined relation maps are the following:</p><p class="confluence-link"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b0983454-d2b0-4d94-bfa5-ca17ef10aa9c" /></p><h3>Capability Behavioral Map</h3><p>The <strong>Capability Behavioral Map</strong> depicts the behavioral relationships of a Capability. This predefined map includes the Capability itself, related Operational Activities (Operational Activity, Standard Operational Activity), related Functions (Function, Project Activity, Security Process), and related Service Functions. The Capability Behavioral Map also shows the internal decomposition of each mapped behavioral element, as well as the relationships among these elements. </p><h3>Capability Structural Map</h3><p>The <strong>Capability Structural Map</strong> depicts the structural relationships of a Capability. This predefined map includes the Capability itself, related Operational Agents (Operational Performer, Operational Architecture), Resource Performers (Capability Configuration, Organization, Software, and other elements), Services, Actual Resource, and Actual Service. The Capability Structural Map also shows the internal compositions of each mapped element, as well as the relationships among these elements. </p><h3>Implementation Map</h3><p>The <strong>Implementation Map</strong> depicts the implementation relationships of an Operational Performer. This predefined map includes the Operational Element (Operational Performer, Operational Architecture, Operational Interface, Operational Role, Operational Information), and the Resource Elements (Resource Performer, Resource Role, Resource Interface, Resource Information) that have been indicated as implementing that operational element. The Implementation Map also shows the internal compositions of each mapped element, as well as the relationships among these elements. </p><p><ac:image ac:title="Creating Implementation Map" ac:alt="Creating Implementation Map"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Implementation_map.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Traceability relation maps" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3>Operational Activity Decomposition Map</h3><p>The <strong>Operational Activity Decomposition Map</strong> depicts the behavioral decomposition of an Operational Activity. </p><h3>Operational Activity Implementation Map</h3><p>The <strong>Operational Activity Implementation Map</strong> depicts the behavioral implementation of an Operational Activity, relating the Operational Activity and Functions that implement the operational behavior. When the Operational Activity has composite behaviors and Functions have composite functionality, the Operational Activity Implementation Map also includes the behavioral elements for the composed elements. </p><h3>Operational Performer Impact Analysis Map</h3><p>The <strong>Operational Performer Impact Analysis Map</strong> depicts the model elements influenced by the changes of the Operational Performer. This predefined map includes:</p><ul><li>Performs Operational Activity</li><li>Exhibits Capability</li><li>Context (Operational Roles, where Operational Performer is used as Context)</li><li>Inputs and Outputs (Operational Performers connected using Operational Exchanges)</li><li>Operational Exchanges</li></ul><h3>Operational Role Impact Analysis Map        </h3><p>The <strong>Operational Role Impact Analysis Map </strong>depicts the model elements influenced by the changes of the Operational Role. This predefined map includes:</p><ul><li>Context (Operational Performer that is the context of the Operational Role)  </li><li>Inputs and Outputs (Operational Roles connected using Operational Connectors)</li><li>Performs In Context (Operational Activities performed by the Operational Role)</li><li>Performs (by Type) (Operational Activities performed by the Type of the Operational Role)</li><li>Exhibits Capability (by Type) (Capabilities exhibited by the Type of the Operational Role)</li></ul><h3>Organization Structure Map</h3><p>The <strong>Organization Structure Map</strong> depicts the structural composition of an Actual Organization. The predefined map includes:</p><ul><li>Actual Organization itself</li><li>Actual Posts</li><li>Actual Persons</li></ul><p>The Organization Structure Map illustrates the relationships between these organizational elements.</p><h3>Resource Performer Map</h3><p>The <strong>Resource Performer Map</strong> depicts the structural decomposition of a Resource Performer including Capability Configuration, Organization, Software, and other elements.</p><h3>Resource Role Impact Analysis Map</h3><p>The <strong>Resource Role Impact Analysis Map</strong> depicts the model elements influenced by the changes of the Resource Role. This predefined map includes:</p><ul><li>Context (Resource which is used as the context of the Resource Role)</li><li>Inputs and Outputs (Resource Roles connected using Resource Connectors)</li><li>Performs In Context (Functions performed by the Resource Role)</li><li>Performs (by Type) (Function Actions performed by the Type of the Resource Role)</li><li>Implements (by Type) (Operational Performers implemented by the Type of the Resource Role)</li><li>Exhibits Capability (by Type) (Capabilities exhibited by the Type of the Resource Role) </li></ul><h3>Strategic Driver Map</h3><p><strong>The Strategic Driver Map (St-Mv) </strong>depicts the relationships of a Driver.</p><p>This predefined map includes the Driver, Enterprise Goals, and/or Enterprise Objectives that are related to a Driver using the Motivated By relationship, and Actual Strategic Phases that are related to the Enterprise Goals or Enterprise Objectives using the Phases relationship.   </p><p>The Context of the relation map can be either a Driver or a Package. The Relationship Criteria include Owned Element (if the Context Element is Package), Motivated By, Phases, Is Composed of Enterprise Goals, and Is Composed of Enterprise Objectives. The Element Type includes Driver, Enterprise Goal, Enterprise Objective, and Actual Strategic Phase.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="strategic_driver_map.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Traceability relation maps" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3>Variant Map</h3><p>The <strong>Variant Map</strong> depicts the specializations of a Resource Performer, including Capability Configuration, Software, and other elements. Specializations of a Resource Performer are modeled using the Generalization relationship. </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="3e61ded7-6246-4140-b283-6f70bc0cef81" /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227157176 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01168: Typical Resources Deployment Wizard

- page_id: `227157176`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227157176/Typical+Resources+Deployment+Wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-04-25T14:31:03.973+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Dialogs
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The purpose of the **Typical Resources Deployment Wizard** is to deploy the Resources that are necessary for the Actual Responsible Resource to accomplish the selected Capability.

To open the **Typical Resources Deployment Wizard** do the following

1. Open a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with CV-5 typical table' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] (DoDAF), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with StV-5 typical table' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] (MODAF), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with NCV-5 typical table' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] (NAF), or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Typical Deployment diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3'] (UAF) table.
2. Double-click a cell wherein you want to add a Resource.
3. Click the + button that appears on the right side of the cell. The input data for the wizard is the Capability to be deployed and the Actual Organizational Resource responsible for that Capability deployment.

The wizard consists of three steps:

truenone

##### Select Resources

In this step, select the Resourcesrequired by the Actual Responsible Resources to accomplish the Capability. You can search for existing Resources or create new ones.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### In Service date specification

In this step, you can specify the In Service date and time for the selected Resource configuration to be released and used. For the date and time specification, the wizard provides you with the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Date and Time Settings dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR3']**Date and Time Setting** dialog. By default, the Today date is selected.

If the dates are defined in the corresponding Actual Enterprise Phase, those dates can be reused (select a check box next to the name of the Actual Enterprise Phase) and the date selection steps are not available.

The In Service date for the Resource usage can not be later than the Out Of Service date of the table context. You will be able to finish the **Typical Resources Deployment Wizard** wizard only if the start date is specified.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Out Of Service date specification

In this step, you can specify the Out Of Service date and time for the selected Resource configuration to be withdrawn. For the date and time specification, the wizard provides you with the Date and Time Setting dialog. If the dates are defined in the corresponding Actual Enterprise Phase, those dates are reused.

The Out Of Service date for the Resourceusage can not be earlier than the In Service date of the table context. You will be able to finish the **Typical Resources Deployment Wizard** wizard if the end date is not specified.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The Actual Project Milestones are created. If the milestones with the same chosen dates already exist, they are reused.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with CV-5 typical table' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with StV-5 typical table' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with NCV-5 typical table' space='UAF12P2024xR3']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Typical Deployment diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR3']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>The purpose of the <strong>Typical Resources Deployment Wizard</strong> is to deploy the Resources that are necessary for the Actual Responsible Resource to accomplish the selected Capability.</p><p><br /></p><p>To open the <strong>Typical Resources Deployment Wizard</strong> do the following</p><hr /><ol><li>Open a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with CV-5 typical table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[CV-5]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (DoDAF), <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with StV-5 typical table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[StV-5]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (MODAF), <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with NCV-5 typical table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[NCV-5]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (NAF), or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Typical Deployment diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Strategic Typical Deployment]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (UAF) table.</li><li>Double-click a cell wherein you want to add a Resource.</li><li>Click the <strong>+</strong> button that appears on the right side of the cell.<br />The input data for the wizard is the Capability to be deployed and the<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Actual Organizational Resource </span>responsible for that Capability deployment.</li></ol><p>The wizard consists of three steps:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9a5f6eb2-f967-41d3-ace3-1682ec042c89"><ac:parameter ac:name="outline">true</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="style">none</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h3>Select Resources</h3><p>In this step, select the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Resources </span>required by the Actual Responsible Resources to accomplish the Capability. You can search for existing <span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Resources</span></span> or create new ones.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="typical_resource_deployment_wizard_I.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Typical Resources Deployment Wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3>In Service date specification</h3><p>In this step, you can specify the In Service date and time for the selected Resource configuration to be released and used<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">. For the date and time specification, the wizard provides you with the </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Date and Time Settings dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /><ac:link-body><strong>Date and Time Setting</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>. By default, the Today date is selected.</p><p>If the dates are defined in the corresponding Actual Enterprise Phase, those dates <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">can be reused (select a check box next to the name of the Actual Enterprise Phase)</span> and the date selection steps are not available. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2aeaf087-6e32-4bfb-8357-ed8801f7246a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The In Service date for the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Resource</span> usage can not be later than the Out Of Service date of the table context. You will be able to finish the <strong>Typical Resources Deployment Wizard</strong> wizard only if the start date is specified.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="typical_resource_deployment_wizard_II.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Typical Resources Deployment Wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3>Out Of Service date specification</h3><p>In this step, you can specify the Out Of Service date and time for the selected Resource configuration to be withdrawn<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">. For the date and time specification, the wizard provides you with the Date and Time Setting dialog. If the dates are defined in the corresponding Actual Enterprise Phase, those dates are reused.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="22148d28-e480-4577-83a3-a50b5eb00146"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Out Of Service date for the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Resource </span>usage can not be earlier than the In Service date of the table context. You will be able to finish the <strong>Typical Resources Deployment Wizard</strong> wizard if the end date is not specified.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="typical_resource_deployment_wizard_III.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Typical Resources Deployment Wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The Actual Project Milestones are created. If the milestones with the same chosen dates already exist, they are reused.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with CV-5 typical table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with StV-5 typical table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with NCV-5 typical table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Typical Deployment diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227158086 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01169: UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.2. Changes of element type and metaclass

- page_id: `227158086`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227158086/UAF+1.0+to+UAF+1.2.+Changes+of+element+type+and+metaclass
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-05-27T07:27:35.192+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UAF projects migration manual > Migration from UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.2
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following table describes element metaclasses mapping between UAF 1.0 and UAF 1.2.

| Element | UAF 1.0 [Metaclass] | UAF 1.2 [Metaclass] |
| --- | --- | --- |
| AssetRole | Element | Property |
| Desirer | Class | Element |
| WholeLifeEnterprise | Class | InstanceSpecification |

###### Changes types of Elements

The following table describes element metaclasses mapping between UAF 1.0 and UAF 1.2.

| Element/Property | UAF 1.0 [Type] | UAF 1.2 [Type] |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Activity::activityPerformableUnderCondition | Condition | ActualCondition |
| EnterpriseGoal::benefit | String | ValueItem |
| Measurement::environmentalContext | Condition | ActualCondition |
| LocationHolder::requiredEnvironment | Environment | ActualEnvironment |
| CapabilityConfiguration::doctrine | Constraint | StandardOperationalActivity |

For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.2. Mapping solutions and issues' space='UAF12P2024xR3'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The following table describes element metaclasses mapping between UAF 1.0 and UAF 1.2.</span></p><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><thead><tr><th
````
