<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227158089 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01170: UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.2. Mapping solutions and issues

- page_id: `227158089`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227158089/UAF+1.0+to+UAF+1.2.+Mapping+solutions+and+issues
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-05-27T12:19:20.506+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UAF projects migration manual > Migration from UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.2
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Known Resource exception

946151309

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Known Resource exception

946151320

946151307

**On this page**

3

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Known Resource exception

946151310

##### Removed tag added to ToDo property

Condition::conditionKind tag was removed, so after the migration it will be added into ToDo property in the Specification window of the Condition element.

##### Changed Property Types

44

###### Changes for Condition and Environment

In earlier versions, the Activity Performable Under Condition property (owned by Activity) had the Condition as its value. After the migration, the newly created Actual Condition with the Condition set as a type, replaces this property.

The same is valid for :

- Environmental Context (owned by Measurement) value Condition.
- Required Environment (owned by Location Holder) value. If the value was Environment, the Actual Environment is created . I f the value was Condition, the Actual Condition is created.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Changes for Doctrine

In earlier versions, the Doctrine property (owned by Capability Configuration) had Constraint for its value. In UAF 1.1 Constraint is replaced by Standard Operational Activity. After the migration, Capability Configuration is set as constrainedElement to Constraint Except if Constraint is Operational Constraint or Service Policy.

##### The Security Property renamed

In UAF 1.2, the Security Property is renamed to Operational Information Role and the new Resource Information Role is implemented. The Operational Information Role is used by the Operational Assets and the Resource Information Role is used by the Resource Assets.

- Operational Asset - An abstract element used to group the elements of Operational Agent and Operational Information.
- Resource Asset - An abstract element used to group the elements of Resource Performer and Resource Information.

If Operational Agent and Operational Information (in UAF 1.2 grouped by Operational Asset) had a Security Property set, after the migration the Security Property will be changed to:

- Operational Information Role, if the type of the Security Property was any of the Operational Asset specializations.
- Simple UML property, if the type of the Security Property was Resource Asset.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If Resource Performer and Resource Information (in UAF 1.2 grouped by Resource Asset) had a Security Property set, after the migration the Security Property will be changed to:

- Resource Information Role, if the type of the Security Property was Resource Asset.
- Simple UML property, if the type of the Security Property was Operational Asset.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### NOTE: Known Resource exception

Known Resource exception

The Known Resource is both: Resource Asset and Operational Asset. After the migration, the Security Property (owned by the Know Resource) is changed depending on its type:

- If the type was Resource Information, the Security Property becomes Resource Information Role.
- If the type was Operational Information, the Security Property becomes Operational Information Role.

##### Enduring Task removed

Enduring Task element was removed, so after the migration, it become Strategic Phase.

##### Capability For Task removed

Capability For Task was removed, so after the migration Capability For Task will be changed to Phases relationship (reverse).

##### Changes for properties of ActualEnterprisePhase

- Operational Architecture Of Enterprise Phase property was removed, so after the migration Implements relationship will be created between Actual Enterprise Phase and Operational Architecture element.
- Resource Architecture Of Enterprise Phase property was removed, so after the migration Implements relationship will be created between Actual Enterprise Phase and Resource Architecture element.
- Vision property was removed, so after the migration Phases relationship was created between Actual Enterprise Phase and Enterprise Vision
- Goal property was removed, so after the migration Phases relationship was created between Actual Enterprise Phase and Enterprise Goal
- **Concern**property was removed, so after the migration Phases relationship was created between *Actual Enterprise Phase* and Concern

##### Changes for *Concern property*

- Enterprise Phase property was removed, so after the migration Phases relationship was created between Actual Enterprise Phase and Concern

##### Changes for property of *Enterprise Goal*

- Enterprise Phase property was removed, so after the migration Phases relationship was created between Actual Enterprise Phase and Enterprise Goal

##### Changes for *property of* *Enterprise Vision*

- Enterprise Phase property was removed, so after the migration Phases relationship was created between Actual Enterprise Phase and Enterprise Vision

##### Changes for properties of Desired Effect and Achieved Effect

- AchievedEffect:: desiredEffect is removed.
- DesiredEffect:: achievedEffect is removed.
- After the migration, the Compares To relationship is created between **desires** target and **achieves** target if AchievedEffect:: **desiredEffect** and DesiredEffect::**achievedEffect** were linked with each other.

##### Consumes renamed

In UAF 1.2 Consumes relationship is renamed to Supports and the direction is reversed.

##### Specializations of Capable Element updated

In UAF 1.2 Actual Enterprise Phase and Actual Enduring Task are removed as specializations of Capable Element. If these elements have a relationship (Exhibits) with Capability, Exhibits relationship was removed and Phases will be created.

##### Changed Property Types

In earlier versions, the Benefit property (owned by Enterprise Goal) was String. In UAF 1.2 benefit property has value Item for its value. After migration, the Value Item element will be created based on benefit value.

##### Changed for Whole Life Enterprise

In the earlier version, Whole Life Enterprise was Class. In UAF 1.2 Whole Life Enterprise will be Instance Specification. After migration, Whole Life Enterprise becomes Strategic Phase.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227158087 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01171: UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.2. New elements

- page_id: `227158087`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227158087/UAF+1.0+to+UAF+1.2.+New+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-05-27T11:24:29.260+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UAF projects migration manual > Migration from UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.2
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

| UAF 1.2 | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Actual Effect | A real world phenomenon that follows and is caused by some previous phenomenon. |
| Actual Outcome | Something that happens or is produced as the final consequence or product and is related to one of the goals for the business or enterprise. Outcome is a special kind of effect, one that is usually at the end of a chain of effects, i.e. an "end effect". |
| Capability Kind | Enumeration of the possible kinds of Capability. The following are enumeration literals for Capability Kind: Operational, Other, Personnel, Resource, Security, Service, and Strategic. |
| Challenge | An existing or potential difficulty, circumstance, or obstacle which will require effort and determination from an enterprise to overcome in achieving its goals. |
| Challenge Kind | Enumeration of the possible kinds of Challenge. The following are enumeration literals for ChallengeKind: Business, Enterprise, Mission, Other, and Strategic. |
| Compares To | An abstraction relationship relating the effect that is achieved with the originally expected Desired Effect. Providing a means of comparison, between the expectation of the desirer and the actual result. |
| Creates | A dependency relationship denoting that an Actual Strategic Phase brings into existence a Strategic Asset. |
| Driver | A factor which will have a significant impact on the activities, and goals of an enterprise. |
| Driver Kind | Enumeration of the possible kinds of Driver. The following are enumeration literals for DriverKind: Operational, Other, Personnel, Project, Resource, Security, Service, Standard, and Strategic. |
| Effect | A kind of phenomenon that follows and is caused by some previous phenomenon that could lead to downstream effects or to one or more desired outcomes. |
| Enables | A dependency relationship denoting that an Opportunity provides the means for achieving an Enterprise Goal or Objective. |
| Enterprise Mission | Mission captures at a high level what you will do to realize your vision. |
| Enterprise Objective | A statement of an attainable, time-targeted, and measurable target that the enterprise seeks to meet in order to achieve its Goals. |
| Evoked By | A dependency relationship denoting that a Risk is drawn out by an Opportunity. |
| Governed By | An abstraction relationship that exists between the ServiceContract and the Service that it governs. |
| Impacted By | A dependency relationship denoting that a Capability is affected by an Opportunity. |
| Maps To Goal | A dependency relationship denoting that some Strategic Information contributes to achieving an Enterprise Goal or Objective. |
| Motivated By | A dependency relationship denoting the reason or reasons one has for acting or behaving in a particular way. |
| Operational Asset | An abstract element used to group the elements of Operational Agent and Operational Information allowing them to own Operational Information Role. |
| Opportunity | An existing or potential favorable circumstance or combination of circumstances which can be advantageous for addressing enterprise Challenges. |
| Owns Value | An abstraction relationship denoting that an Actual Organizational Resource owns a Value Item. |
| Phases | An abstraction relationship that exists between a Phaseable Element and an Actual Strategic Phase that it is assigned to. |
| Presented By | A dependency relationship denoting that a Challenge must be overcome for addressing a Driver. |
| Resource Asset | An abstract element used to group the elements of Resource Performer and Resource Information allowing them to own Resource Information Roles |
| Resource Information Role* | A usage of Resource Information that exists in the context of an Resource Asset. It also allows the representation of the whole-part aggregation of Resource Information elements. |
| Resource Service | A services that a Resource Performer provides to support higher level Services or Operational Activities. Employee provisioning, backup and recovery, storage, self-service help desk are examples of Resource Services. |
| Resource Service Interface | A contract that defines the Resource Methods and Resource Signal receptions that the Resource Services realize. |
| Security Risk | The level of impact on enterprise operations, assets, or individuals resulting from the operation of an information system given the potential impact of a threat and the likelihood of that threat occurring [NIST SP 800-65]. |
| Sequence | A dependency relationship that asserts one Individual's temporal extent is completely before the temporal extent of another. |
| Service Architecture | An element used to denote a model of the Architecture, described from the Services perspective. |
| Service Contract | A constraint governing the use of one or more Services. |
| Service Control Flow | An Activity Edge that shows the flow of control between Service Function Actions. |
| Service Exchange | Asserts that a flow can exist between Services (i.e. flows of information, people, materiel, or energy). |
| Service Exchange Item | An abstract grouping for elements that defines the types of elements that can be exchanged between Services and conveyed by a Service Exchange. |
| Service Exchange Kind | Enumeration of the possible kinds of Service Exchange. The following are enumeration literals for Service Exchange Kind:Configuration Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where Capability Configurations flow from one Operational Performer to another.Energy Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where energy is flowed from one Operational Performer to another.Information Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where information is flowed from one Operational Performer to another.Materiel Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow of materiel (artifacts) between Functions.Organizational Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where human resources (Post Types, Role Types) flow between Operational Performers. |
| Service Object Flow | An Activity Edge that shows the flow of Resources (objects/information) between Service Function Actions. |
| Service Signal | A specification of a kind of communication between Services in which a reaction is asynchronously triggered in the receiver without a reply. |
| Service Signal Property | A property of a Service Signal typed by Service Exchange Item. It enables Service Exchange Item e.g. Operational Information to be passed as arguments of the Service Signal. |
| Strategic Constraint | A Rule governing a Capability. |
| Strategic Exchange | Asserts that a flow can exist between Actual Strategic Phases (i.e. flows of information, people, materiel, or energy). |
| Strategic Information | Knowledge communicated or received concerning a particular fact or circumstance that is strategic in nature that is important or essential in relation to a plan of action. |
| Value Item | An ideal, custom, or institution that an enterprise promotes or agrees with. It may be positive or negative, depending on point of view. |
| Value Item Kind | Enumeration of the possible kinds of Value Item. The following are enumeration literals for Value Item Kind: Benefit, Cost, KPI, Loss, Other, Quality, Revenue, and Time. |
| Value Stream | An end-to-end collection of activities that create a result for a customer, who may be the ultimate customer or an internal end-user of the value stream. Value stream nested within another value stream may represent Value Stream Stage - a distinct, identifiable phase or step within a value stream [The Business Architecture Metamodel Guide, 2020]. |

* For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.2. Mapping solutions and issues' space='UAF12P2024xR3'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><table class="relative-table" style="width: 100.0%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 12.0941%;" /><col style="width: 87.9059%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th colspan="1">UAF 1.2</th><th colspan="1">Description</th></tr><tr><td>Actual Effect</td><td>A real world phenomenon that follows and is caused by some previous phenomenon.</td></tr><tr><td>Actual Outcome</td><td>Something that happens or is produced as the final consequence or product and is related to one of the goals for the business or enterprise. Outcome is a special kind of effect, one that is usually at the end of a chain of effects, i.e. an &quot;end effect&quot;.</td></tr><tr><td>Capability Kind</td><td>Enumeration of the possible kinds of Capability. The following are enumeration literals for Capability Kind: Operational, Other, Personnel, Resource, Security, Service, and Strategic.</td></tr><tr><td>Challenge</td><td>An existing or potential difficulty, circumstance, or obstacle which will require effort and determination from an enterprise to overcome in achieving its goals.</td></tr><tr><td>Challenge Kind</td><td>Enumeration of the possible kinds of Challenge. The following are enumeration literals for ChallengeKind: Business, Enterprise, Mission, Other, and Strategic.</td></tr><tr><td>Compares To</td><td>An abstraction relationship relating the effect that is achieved with the originally expected Desired Effect. Providing a means of comparison, between the expectation of the desirer and the actual result.</td></tr><tr><td>Creates</td><td>A dependency relationship denoting that an Actual Strategic Phase brings into existence a Strategic Asset.</td></tr><tr><td>Driver</td><td>A factor which will have a significant impact on the activities, and goals of an enterprise.</td></tr><tr><td>Driver Kind</td><td>Enumeration of the possible kinds of Driver. The following are enumeration literals for DriverKind: Operational, Other, Personnel, Project, Resource, Security, Service, Standard, and Strategic.</td></tr><tr><td>Effect</td><td>A kind of phenomenon that follows and is caused by some previous phenomenon that could lead to downstream effects or to one or more desired outcomes.</td></tr><tr><td>Enables</td><td>A dependency relationship denoting that an Opportunity provides the means for achieving an Enterprise Goal or Objective.</td></tr><tr><td>Enterprise Mission</td><td>Mission captures at a high level what you will do to realize your vision.</td></tr><tr><td>Enterprise Objective</td><td>A statement of an attainable, time-targeted, and measurable target that the enterprise seeks to meet in order to achieve its Goals.</td></tr><tr><td>Evoked By</td><td>A dependency relationship denoting that a Risk is drawn out by an Opportunity.</td></tr><tr><td>Governed By</td><td>An abstraction relationship that exists between the ServiceContract and the Service that it governs.</td></tr><tr><td>Impacted By</td><td>A dependency relationship denoting that a Capability is affected by an Opportunity.</td></tr><tr><td>Maps To Goal</td><td>A dependency relationship denoting that some Strategic Information contributes to achieving an Enterprise Goal or Objective.</td></tr><tr><td>Motivated By</td><td>A dependency relationship denoting the reason or reasons one has for acting or behaving in a particular way.</td></tr><tr><td>Operational Asset</td><td>An abstract element used to group the elements of Operational Agent and Operational Information allowing them to own Operational Information Role.</td></tr><tr><td>Opportunity</td><td>An existing or potential favorable circumstance or combination of circumstances which can be advantageous for addressing enterprise Challenges.</td></tr><tr><td>Owns Value</td><td>An abstraction relationship denoting that an Actual Organizational Resource owns a Value Item.</td></tr><tr><td>Phases</td><td>An abstraction relationship that exists between a Phaseable Element and an Actual Strategic Phase that it is assigned to. </td></tr><tr><td>Presented By</td><td>A dependency relationship denoting that a Challenge must be overcome for addressing a Driver.</td></tr><tr><td>Resource Asset</td><td>An abstract element used to group the elements of Resource Performer and Resource Information allowing them to own Resource Information Roles</td></tr><tr><td>Resource Information Role*</td><td>A usage of Resource Information that exists in the context of an Resource Asset. It also allows the representation of the whole-part aggregation of Resource Information elements.</td></tr><tr><td>Resource Service</td><td>A services that a Resource Performer provides to support higher level Services or Operational Activities. Employee provisioning, backup and recovery, storage, self-service help desk are examples of Resource Services.</td></tr><tr><td>Resource Service Interface</td><td>A contract that defines the Resource Methods and Resource Signal receptions that the Resource Services realize.</td></tr><tr><td>Security Risk</td><td>The level of impact on enterprise operations, assets, or individuals resulting from the operation of an information system given the potential impact of a threat and the likelihood of that threat occurring [NIST SP 800-65].</td></tr><tr><td>Sequence</td><td>A dependency relationship that asserts one Individual's temporal extent is completely before the temporal extent of another.</td></tr><tr><td>Service Architecture</td><td>An element used to denote a model of the Architecture, described from the Services perspective.</td></tr><tr><td>Service Contract</td><td>A constraint governing the use of one or more Services.</td></tr><tr><td>Service Control Flow</td><td>An Activity Edge that shows the flow of control between Service Function Actions.</td></tr><tr><td>Service Exchange</td><td>Asserts that a flow can exist between Services (i.e. flows of information, people, materiel, or energy).</td></tr><tr><td>Service Exchange Item</td><td>An abstract grouping for elements that defines the types of elements that can be exchanged between Services and conveyed by a Service Exchange.</td></tr><tr><td>Service Exchange Kind</td><td><p>Enumeration of the possible kinds of Service Exchange. The following are enumeration literals for Service Exchange Kind:</p><ul><li>Configuration Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where Capability Configurations flow from one Operational Performer to another.</li><li>Energy Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where energy is flowed from one Operational Performer to another.</li><li>Information Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where information is flowed from one Operational Performer to another.</li><li>Materiel Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow of materiel (artifacts) between Functions.</li><li>Organizational Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where human resources (Post Types, Role Types) flow between Operational Performers.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td>Service Object Flow</td><td>An Activity Edge that shows the flow of Resources (objects/information) between Service Function Actions.</td></tr><tr><td>Service Signal</td><td>A specification of a kind of communication between Services in which a reaction is asynchronously triggered in the receiver without a reply.</td></tr><tr><td>Service Signal Property</td><td>A property of a Service Signal typed by Service Exchange Item. It enables Service Exchange Item e.g. Operational Information to be passed as arguments of the Service Signal.</td></tr><tr><td>Strategic Constraint</td><td>A Rule governing a Capability.</td></tr><tr><td>Strategic Exchange</td><td>Asserts that a flow can exist between Actual Strategic Phases (i.e. flows of information, people, materiel, or energy).</td></tr><tr><td>Strategic Information</td><td>Knowledge communicated or received concerning a particular fact or circumstance that is strategic in nature that is important or essential in relation to a plan of action.</td></tr><tr><td>Value Item</td><td>An ideal, custom, or institution that an enterprise promotes or agrees with. It may be positive or negative, depending on point of view.</td></tr><tr><td>Value Item Kind</td><td>Enumeration of the possible kinds of Value Item. The following are enumeration literals for Value Item Kind: Benefit, Cost, KPI, Loss, Other, Quality, Revenue, and Time.</td></tr><tr><td>Value Stream</td><td>An end-to-end collection of activities that create a result for a customer, who may be the ultimate customer or an internal end-user of the value stream. Value stream nested within another value stream may represent Value Stream Stage - a distinct, identifiable phase or step within a value stream [The Business Architecture Metamodel Guide, 2020].</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target">* For more information, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.2. Mapping solutions and issues" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link>.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227158088 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01172: UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.2. Renamed and removed elements

- page_id: `227158088`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227158088/UAF+1.0+to+UAF+1.2.+Renamed+and+removed+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-05-27T12:13:57.604+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UAF projects migration manual > Migration from UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.2
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following table describes element which are renamed in UAF 1.2 profile.

| Element UAF 1.0 [name] | Element UAF 1.2 [name] |
| --- | --- |
| Achieved Effect | Achieves |
| Capability Property | Capability Role |
| Consumes* | Supports |
| Data Element | Resource Information |
| Data Model | Information Model |
| Data Model Kind | Information Model Kind |
| Data Role | Resource Information Role |
| Desired Effect | Desires |
| Enterprise Phase | Strategic Phase |
| Information Element | Operational Information |
| Information Role | Operational Information Role |
| Security Property* | Operational Information Role |
| Service Specification | Service |
| Service Specification Role | Service Role |
| Organization In Enterprise | Organization In Phase |

* For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.2. Mapping solutions and issues' space='UAF12P2024xR3'].

The following table describes properties which are renamed in UAF 1.2 profile.

| Element | Property UAF 1.0 [name] | Property UAF 1.2 [name] |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Viewpoint | languagesmethods | languagemethod |
| OperationalExchange | trustlevel | trustLevel |
| ResponsibleFor | ResponsibleRoleKind | responsibleRoleKind |
| Architecture | desribedBy | describedBy |
| ArchitecturalDescription | viewPoint | viewpoint |

**Removed properties:**

- Concern::enterprisePhase
- Condition::conditionKind
- ActualEnterprisePhase:: resourceArchitectureOfEnterprisePhase
- ActualEnterprisePhase:: operationalArchitectureOfEnterprisePhase
- ActualEnterprisePhase:: vision
- ActualEnterprisePhase:: goal
- ActualEnterprisePhase:: concern
- AchievedEffect:: desiredEffect
- DesiredEffect::achievedEffect
- EnterpriseGoal::enterprisePhase
- EnterpriseVision::enterprisePhase
- Risk::riskOwner

**Removed elements:**

- Enduring Task*
- Capability For Task

For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.2. Mapping solutions and issues' space='UAF12P2024xR3'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227158084 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01173: UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2. Changes in diagrams, tables, and matrices

- page_id: `227158084`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227158084/UAF+1.1+to+UAF+1.2.+Changes+in+diagrams+tables+and+matrices
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-20T13:58:37.535+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UAF projects migration manual > Migration from UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

New diagrams, tables, and matrices:

- Architecture Management diagram
- Security Controls diagram
- Security Constraints table
- Services Role-based Connectivity Table
- Services Connectivity Table
- Services Connectivity Matrix
- Strategic Constraint table
- Strategic Information diagram
- Strategic Motivation diagram
- Strategic Processes
- Functions to Operational Activities Matrix
- Actual Responsible Resources to Actual Project Mapping Matrix

The following table shows how the tables, diagrams, and matrices were renamed consistent with the UAF 1.2 support.

| Name in UAF 1.1 | Name in UAF 1.2 |
| --- | --- |
| Capabilities to Actual Enduring Tasks Mapping Matrix | Actual Strategic Phases to Capabilities Mapping Matrix |
| Service Specifications to Capabilities Mapping matrix | Services to Capabilities Mapping matrix |
| Operational Activities to Service Specifications Mapping matrix | Services to Operational Activities Mapping matrix |
| Services to Operational Activities Mapping matrix | Services to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix |
| Operational Interaction Scenarios | Operational Sequences |
| Services Interaction Scenarios | Services Sequences |
| Personnel Interaction Scenarios | Personnel Sequences |
| Resources Interaction Scenarios | Resources Sequences |
| Operational Interaction Scenarios BPD | Operational Sequences BPD |
| Personnel Interaction Scenarios BPD | Personnel Sequences BPD |
| Resources Interaction Scenarios BPD | Resources Sequences BPD |
| Conceptual Data Model | Strategic Information |
| Logical Data Model | Operational Information |
| Physical Data Model | Resources Information |
| Actual Enterprise Phases Gantt Chart | Actual Strategic Phases Gantt Chart |

Updated diagrams, tables, and matrices:

| Name | Note |
| --- | --- |
| SvcV-3b Services-Services Matrix | In UAF 1.2, Associations were changed to Service Exchanges |
| Implementation matrix | The Implements relationship is available between the following client and supplier pairs:Resource Performer and Operational AgentFunction and Operational Activity or Service FunctionResource Interface and Service Interface or Operational InterfaceResource Connector and Operational Connector or Resource ConnectorResource Exchange and Operational ExchangeResource Role and Operational RoleResource Information and Operational informationArchitecture or Operational Activity and Actual Strategic PhaseResource Service and ServiceOperational Information and Strategic Information |
| SV-9 Systems Technology and Skills ForecastSvcV-9 Services Technology and Skills ForecastStdV-2 Standards ForecastNSV-9 Technology ForecastNTV-2 Technical Standards ForecastSV-9 Technology & Skills ForecastTV-2 Standards ForecastP1 Resource Types Technology ForecastA8 Standards ForecastStandards RoadmapResources ForecastPersonnel Forecast | It is possible to add the Actual Strategic Phase as a column in the table.In the 3rd step of the wizard, you can select Actual Strategic Phases. |
| Actual Responsible Resources to Actual Projects Mapping MatrixLr Responsibility MatrixAcV-1 Responsibility MatrixPV-1 Responsibility MatrixNPV-1 Responsibility Matrix | The row scope was changed from Actual Organizational Resources to Actual Responsible Resources. |
| Actual Resources Taxonomy Matrix | The row scope was changed from Actual Organizational Resources to Actual Responsible Resources. |
| Resources to Operational Activities Mapping Matrix | The dependency criterion was changed. |
| Operational Activity to Services Traceability | The dependency criterion was changed. |
| SV-5b Operational Activity to Systems Traceability Matrix | The dependency criterion was changed. |
| SvcV-3b Services-Services Matrix | The dependency criterion was changed. |
| UPDM Capability Behavioral Map | The dependency criterion name was changed. |
| UPDM Capability Structural Map | The dependency criterion name was changed. |
| UPDM Implementation Map | The dependency criterion name was changed. |
| UPDM Operational Activity Decomposition Map | The dependency criterion name was changed. |
| UPDM Operational Activity Implementation Map | The dependency criterion name was changed. |
| UPDM Operational Performer Impact Analysis Map | The dependency criterion name was changed. |
| UPDM Organization Structure Map | The dependency criterion name was changed. |
| UPDM Resource Performer Map | The dependency criterion name was changed. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Matrix</span></td><td><ul><li>The row scope was changed from Actual Organizational Resources to Actual Responsible Resources.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Resources to Operational Activities Mapping Matrix</span></td><td><ul><li>The dependency criterion was changed.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Operational Activity to Services Traceability</span></td><td><ul><li>The dependency criterion was changed.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">SV-5b Operational Activity to Systems Traceability Matrix</span></td><td><ul><li>The dependency criterion was changed.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">SvcV-3b Services-Services Matrix</span></td><td><ul><li>The dependency criterion was changed.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">UPDM Capability Behavioral Map</span></td><td><ul><li>The dependency criterion name was changed.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">UPDM Capability Structural Map</span></td><td><ul><li>The dependency criterion name was changed.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">UPDM Implementation Map</span></td><td><ul><li>The dependency criterion name was changed.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">UPDM Operational Activity Decomposition Map</span></td><td><ul><li>The dependency criterion name was changed.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">UPDM Operational Activity Implementation Map</span></td><td><ul><li>The dependency criterion name was changed.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">UPDM Operational Performer Impact Analysis Map</span></td><td><ul><li>The dependency criterion name was changed.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">UPDM Organization Structure Map</span></td><td><ul><li>The dependency criterion name was changed.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">UPDM Resource Performer Map</span></td><td><ul><li>The dependency criterion name was changed.</li></ul></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227158071 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01174: UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2. Changes of element metaclass and type

- page_id: `227158071`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227158071/UAF+1.1+to+UAF+1.2.+Changes+of+element+metaclass+and+type
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-05-27T07:24:07.403+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UAF projects migration manual > Migration from UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following table describes element metaclasses mapping between UAF 1.1 and UAF 1.2.

| Element | UAF 1.1 [Metaclass] | UAF 1.2 [Metaclass] |
| --- | --- | --- |
| WholeLifeEnterprise | Class | InstanceSpecification |

###### Changes types of Elements

The following table describes element metaclasses mapping between UAF 1.1 and UAF 1.2.

| Element/Property | UAF 1.1 [Type] | UAF 1.2 [Type] |
| --- | --- | --- |
| EnterpriseGoal::benefit | String | ValueItem |

For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2. Mapping solutions and issues' space='UAF12P2024xR3'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The following table describes element metaclasses mapping between UAF 1.1 and UAF 1.2.</span></p><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><thead><tr><th colspan="1"><p>Element</p></th><th><p>UAF 1.1 [Metaclass]</p></th><th><p><span>UAF 1</span>.2 [Metaclass]</p></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td colspan="1"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">WholeLifeEnterprise</span></td><td colspan="1">Class</td><td colspan="1">InstanceSpecification</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><h4><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="55b6d176-05eb-4bf4-aca8-b9bc3d6ce51a">Changes types of Elements</ac:inline-comment-marker></h4><p>The following table describes element metaclasses mapping between UAF 1.1 and UAF 1.2.</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><thead><tr><th colspan="1"><p>Element/Property</p></th><th><p>UAF 1.1 [Type]</p></th><th><p>UAF 1.2 [Type]</p></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td colspan="1"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">EnterpriseGoal::benefit</span></td><td colspan="1">String</td><td colspan="1">ValueItem</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target">For more information, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2. Mapping solutions and issues" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ac:link>.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227158074 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01175: UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2. Mapping solutions and issues

- page_id: `227158074`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227158074/UAF+1.1+to+UAF+1.2.+Mapping+solutions+and+issues
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-05-15T13:11:49.082+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UAF projects migration manual > Migration from UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

950009550

950009571

950009548

**On this page**

950009560

##### Enduring Task removed

The Enduring Task element was removed, so it became Strategic Phase after the migration.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Capability For Task removed

Capability For Task was removed, so after the migration, Capability For Task will be changed to Phases relationship (reverse).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Changes for properties of ActualEnterprisePhase

- Operational Architecture Of Enterprise Phase property was removed, so after the migration Implements relationship will be created between Actual Enterprise Phase and Operational Architecture element.
- Resource Architecture Of Enterprise Phase property was removed, so after the migration Implements relationship will be created between Actual Enterprise Phase and Resource Architecture element.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- Vision property was removed, so after the migration Phases relationship was created between Actual Enterprise Phase and Enterprise Vision
- Goal property was removed, so after the migration Phases relationship was created between Actual Enterprise Phase and Enterprise Goal
- **Concern**property was removed, so after the migration Phases relationship was created between *Actual Enterprise Phase* and Concern

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Changes for properties of Desired Effect and Achieved Effect

- AchievedEffect:: desiredEffect is removed.
- DesiredEffect:: achievedEffect is removed.
- After the migration, the Compares To relationship is created between **desires** target and **achieves** target if AchievedEffect:: **desiredEffect** and DesiredEffect::**achievedEffect** were linked with each other.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Consumes renamed

In UAF 1.2 Consumes relationship is renamed to Supports, and the direction is reversed.

##### Risk Owner removed

The Risk Owner property is removed, so after the migration, it will be added to the ToDo property in the Specification window of the Risk element.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Specializations of Capable Element updated

In UAF 1.2 Actual Enterprise Phase and Actual Enduring Task are removed as specializations of Capable Element. If these elements have a relationship (Exhibits) with Capability, the Exhibits relationship was removed, and Phases will be created.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Changes Property Types

In earlier versions, the benefit property (owned by Enterprise Goal) was String. In UAF 1.2, the benefit property has a value Item for its value.After migration, a value item element will be created based on benefit value.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Changes for Whole Life Enterprise

In the earlier version, Whole Life Enterprise was Class. In UAF 1.2 Whole Life Enterprise will be Instance Specification. After migration, Whole Life Enterprise becomes Strategic Phase.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Mapping solutions and issues" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="06d1ad52-188a-4a7c-911c-e7e0a9542b49">Specializations of Capable Element updated</ac:inline-comment-marker></h3><p>In UAF 1.2 Actual Enterprise Phase and Actual Enduring Task are removed as specializations of Capable Element. If these elements have a relationship (Exhibits) with Capability, the Exhibits relationship was removed, and Phases will be created. </p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="exhibits_to_phases.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227158072 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01176: UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2. New elements

- page_id: `227158072`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227158072/UAF+1.1+to+UAF+1.2.+New+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-05-27T07:25:10.379+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UAF projects migration manual > Migration from UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

| UAF 1.2 | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Actual Effect | A real world phenomenon that follows and is caused by some previous phenomenon. |
| Actual Outcome | Something that happens or is produced as the final consequence or product and is related to one of the goals for the business or enterprise. Outcome is a special kind of effect, one that is usually at the end of a chain of effects, i.e. an "end effect". |
| Capability Kind | Enumeration of the possible kinds of Capability. The following are enumeration literals for Capability Kind: Operational, Other, Personnel, Resource, Security, Service, and Strategic. |
| Challenge | An existing or potential difficulty, circumstance, or obstacle which will require effort and determination from an enterprise to overcome in achieving its goals. |
| Challenge Kind | Enumeration of the possible kinds of Challenge. The following are enumeration literals for ChallengeKind: Business, Enterprise, Mission, Other, and Strategic. |
| Compares To | An abstraction relationship relating the effect that is achieved with the originally expected Desired Effect. Providing a means of comparison, between the expectation of the desirer and the actual result. |
| Creates | A dependency relationship denoting that an Actual Strategic Phase brings into existence a Strategic Asset. |
| Driver | A factor which will have a significant impact on the activities, and goals of an enterprise. |
| Driver Kind | Enumeration of the possible kinds of Driver. The following are enumeration literals for DriverKind: Operational, Other, Personnel, Project, Resource, Security, Service, Standard, and Strategic. |
| Effect | A kind of phenomenon that follows and is caused by some previous phenomenon that could lead to downstream effects or to one or more desired outcomes. |
| Enables | A dependency relationship denoting that an Opportunity provides the means for achieving an Enterprise Goal or Objective. |
| Enterprise Mission | Mission captures at a high level what you will do to realize your vision. |
| Enterprise Objective | A statement of an attainable, time-targeted, and measurable target that the enterprise seeks to meet in order to achieve its Goals. |
| Evoked By | A dependency relationship denoting that a Risk is drawn out by an Opportunity. |
| Governed By | An abstraction relationship that exists between the ServiceContract and the Service that it governs. |
| Impacted By | A dependency relationship denoting that a Capability is affected by an Opportunity. |
| Maps To Goal | A dependency relationship denoting that some Strategic Information contributes to achieving an Enterprise Goal or Objective. |
| Motivated By | A dependency relationship denoting the reason or reasons one has for acting or behaving in a particular way. |
| Opportunity | An existing or potential favorable circumstance or combination of circumstances which can be advantageous for addressing enterprise Challenges. |
| Owns Value | An abstraction relationship denoting that an Actual Organizational Resource owns a Value Item. |
| Phases | An abstraction relationship that exists between a Phaseable Element and an Actual Strategic Phase that it is assigned to. |
| Presented By | A dependency relationship denoting that a Challenge must be overcome for addressing a Driver. |
| Resource Service | A services that a Resource Performer provides to support higher level Services or Operational Activities. Employee provisioning, backup and recovery, storage, self-service help desk are examples of Resource Services. |
| Resource Service Interface | A contract that defines the Resource Methods and Resource Signal receptions that the Resource Services realize. |
| Security Risk | The level of impact on enterprise operations, assets, or individuals resulting from the operation of an information system given the potential impact of a threat and the likelihood of that threat occurring [NIST SP 800-65]. |
| Sequence | A dependency relationship that asserts one Individual's temporal extent is completely before the temporal extent of another. |
| Service Architecture | An element used to denote a model of the Architecture, described from the Services perspective. |
| Service Contract | A constraint governing the use of one or more Services. |
| Service Control Flow | An Activity Edge that shows the flow of control between Service Function Actions. |
| Service Exchange | Asserts that a flow can exist between Services (i.e. flows of information, people, materiel, or energy). |
| Service Exchange Item | An abstract grouping for elements that defines the types of elements that can be exchanged between Services and conveyed by a Service Exchange. |
| Service Exchange Kind | Enumeration of the possible kinds of Service Exchange.The following are enumeration literals for Service Exchange Kind:Configuration Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where Capability Configurations flow from one Operational Performer to another.Energy Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where energy is flowed from one Operational Performer to another.Information Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where information is flowed from one Operational Performer to another.Materiel Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow of materiel (artifacts) between Functions.Organizational Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where human resources (Post Types, Role Types) flow between Operational Performers. |
| Service Object Flow | An Activity Edge that shows the flow of Resources (objects/information) between Service Function Actions. |
| Service Signal | A specification of a kind of communication between Services in which a reaction is asynchronously triggered in the receiver without a reply. |
| Service Signal Property | A property of a Service Signal typed by Service Exchange Item. It enables Service Exchange Item e.g. Operational Information to be passed as arguments of the Service Signal. |
| Strategic Constraint | A Rule governing a Capability. |
| Strategic Exchange | Asserts that a flow can exist between Actual Strategic Phases (i.e. flows of information, people, materiel, or energy). |
| Strategic Information | Knowledge communicated or received concerning a particular fact or circumstance that is strategic in nature that is important or essential in relation to a plan of action. |
| Value Item | An ideal, custom, or institution that an enterprise promotes or agrees with. It may be positive or negative, depending on point of view. |
| Value Item Kind | Enumeration of the possible kinds of Value Item. The following are enumeration literals for Value Item Kind: Benefit, Cost, KPI, Loss, Other, Quality, Revenue, and Time. |
| Value Stream | An end-to-end collection of activities that create a result for a customer, who may be the ultimate customer or an internal end-user of the value stream. Value stream nested within another value stream may represent Value Stream Stage - a distinct, identifiable phase or step within a value stream [The Business Architecture Metamodel Guide, 2020]. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Opportunity.</span></td></tr><tr><td><p>Governed By</p></td><td><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">An abstraction relationship that exists between the ServiceContract and the Service that it governs.</span></td></tr><tr><td>Impacted By</td><td><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">A dependency relationship denoting that a Capability is affected by an advantageous for addressing enterprise Challenges.</span></td></tr><tr><td>Owns Value</td><td><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">An abstraction relationship denoting that an Actual Organizational Driver.</span></td></tr><tr><td>Resource Service</td><td><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">A services that a Resource Performer provides to support higher level Services or Operational Activities. Employee provisioning, backup and recovery, storage, self-service help desk are examples of Resource Services.</span></td></tr><tr><td>Resource Service Interface</td><td><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">A contract that defines the Resource Methods and Resource Signal receptions that the Resource Services realize.</span></td></tr><tr><td>Security Risk</td><td><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The level of impact on enterprise operations, assets, or individuals resulting from the operation of an information system given the potential impact of a threat and the likelihood of that threat occurring [NIST SP 800-65].</span></td></tr><tr><td>Sequence</td><td><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">A dependency relationship that asserts one Individual's temporal extent is completely before the temporal extent of another.</span></td></tr><tr><td>Service Architecture</td><td><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">An element used to denote a model of the Architecture, described from the Services perspective.</span></td></tr><tr><td>Service Contract</td><td><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">A constraint governing the use of one or more Services.</span></td></tr><tr><td>Service Control Flow</td><td><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">An Activity Edge that shows the flow of control between Service Function Actions.</span></td></tr><tr><td><p>Service Exchange</p></td><td><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Asserts that a flow can exist between Services (i.e. flows of information, people, materiel, or energy).</span></td></tr><tr><td>Service Exchange Item</td><td><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">An abstract grouping for elements that defines the types of elements that can be exchanged between Services and conveyed by a Service Exchange.</span></td></tr><tr><td>Service Exchange Kind</td><td><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">Enumeration of the possible kinds of Service Exchange.</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">The following are enumeration literals for Service Exchange Kind:</p><ul><li>Configuration Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where Capability Configurations flow from one Operational Performer to another.</li><li>Energy Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where energy is flowed from one Operational Performer to another.</li><li>Information Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where information is flowed from one Operational Performer to another.</li><li>Materiel Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow of materiel (artifacts) between Functions.</li><li>Organizational Exchange - Indicates that the Operational Exchange associated with the Operational Exchange Kind is a logical flow where human resources (Post Types, Role Types) flow between Operational Performers.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td>Service Object Flow</td><td><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">An Activity Edge that shows the flow of Resources (objects/information) between Service Function Actions.</span></td></tr><tr><td>Service Signal</td><td><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">A specification of a kind of communication between Services in which a reaction is asynchronously triggered in the receiver without a reply.</span></td></tr><tr><td>Service Signal Property</td><td><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">A property of a Service Signal typed by Service Exchange Item. It enables Service Exchange Item e.g. Operational Information to be passed as arguments of the Service Signal.</span></td></tr><tr><td>Strategic Constraint</td><td><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">A Rule governing a Capability.</span></td></tr><tr><td>Strategic Exchange</td><td><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Asserts that a flow can exist between Actual Strategic Phases (i.e. flows of information, people, materiel, or energy).</span></td></tr><tr><td>Strategic Information</td><td><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Knowledge communicated or received concerning a particular fact or circumstance that is strategic in nature that is important or essential in relation to a plan of action.</span></td></tr><tr><td><p>Value Item</p></td><td><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">An ideal, custom, or institution that an enterprise promotes or agrees with. It may be positive or negative, depending on point of view.</span></td></tr><tr><td>Value Item Kind</td><td><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">Enumeration of the possible kinds of Value Item. The following are enumeration literals for Value Item Kind: Benefit, Cost, KPI, Loss, Other, Quality, Revenue, and Time.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Value Stream</td><td><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">An end-to-end collection of activities that create a result for a customer, who may be the ultimate customer or an internal end-user of the value stream. Value stream nested within another value stream may represent Value Stream Stage - a distinct, identifiable phase or step within a value stream [The Business Architecture Metamodel Guide, 2020].</span></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227158073 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01177: UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2. Renamed and removed elements

- page_id: `227158073`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227158073/UAF+1.1+to+UAF+1.2.+Renamed+and+removed+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-05-27T07:25:52.622+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UAF projects migration manual > Migration from UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following table describes element which are renamed in UAF 1.2 profile.

| Element UAF 1.1 [name] | Element UAF 1.2 [name] |
| --- | --- |
| Data Model Kind | Information Model Kind |
| Data Model | Information Model |
| Information Element | Operational Information |
| Data Element | Resource Information |
| Service Specification | Service |
| Consumes* | Supports |
| Achieved Effect | Achieves |
| Desired Effect | Desires |
| Enterprise Phase | Strategic Phase |
| Organization In Enterprise | Organization In Phase |

The following table describes properties which are renamed in UAF 1.2 profile.

| Property UAF 1.1 [name] | Property UAF 1.2 [name] |
| --- | --- |
| Information Role | Operational Information Role |
| Data Role | Resource Information Role |
| Service Specification Role | Service Role |

****

**Removed properties:**

- Concern::enterprisePhase
- ActualEnterprisePhase:: resourceArchitectureOfEnterprisePhase
- ActualEnterprisePhase:: operationalArchitectureOfEnterprisePhase
- ActualEnterprisePhase:: vision
- ActualEnterprisePhase:: goal
- ActualEnterprisePhase:: concern
- AchievedEffect:: desiredEffect
- DesiredEffect::achievedEffect
- EnterpriseGoal::enterprisePhase
- EnterpriseVision::enterprisePhase
- Risk::riskOwner

**Removed elements:**

- Enduring Task*
- Capability For Task

* For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2. Mapping solutions and issues' space='UAF12P2024xR3'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227156229 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01178: UAF 1.2

- page_id: `227156229`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227156229/UAF+1.2
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-02T10:30:40.162+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

769321950

769321952

769321951

The Unified Architecture Framework (UAF) supports the capability to:

- model architectures for a broad range of complex systems, which may include hardware, software, data, personnel, and facility elements;
- model consistent architectures for system-of-systems (SoS) down to lower levels of design and implementation;
- support the analysis, specification, design, and verification of complex systems; and
- improve the ability to exchange architecture information among related tools that are SysML-based and tools that are based on other standards.

According to modeling needs, there are the following UAF templates for different purposes:

- UAF Project is designed to model architectures for a broad range of complex systems. The UAF provides an applicable model security controls, threat, risk, and risk mitigation. It allows for defining consistent architectures for System-of-Systems (SoS), including all creation phases from the design until implementation.
- EA with BPMN Project* - u sing this template, you will get a simplified Enterprise Architecture project with BPMN, which is based on the UAF framework and designed for enterprise and IT architecture modeling. Creating an EA with the BPMN project will switch the modeling tool to the EA-BPMN Architect graphical user interface and use the EA with the BPMN model template.
- **UAF Project Template with Process Guide** - using this template, you will get a new UAF project enhanced with a modeling guide. The modeling guide helps you to build architecture by providing summary information about the UAF structure and architecture creation process.
- **EA Project Template with Process Guide** - using this template, you will get a new UAF project enhanced with the Enterprise Architecture Guide for UAF (a non-normative component of the UAF specification). This modeling guide aids in building architecture by providing nine steps of workflow.
- **Blank UAF Project** - creates a blank UAF project.

Please make sure, that the UAF 1.2 Plugin is [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installing plugins' space='IL2024xR3'].

*Starting with the 2024x version, the **UAF Enterprise Architecture Project**was renamed to**EA with BPMN Project.**

****

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### UAF 1.2 Grid. The grid is a way of showing how the various view specifications (cells) correspond to viewpoints (prev. known as domains) (horizontal rows) and the aspects (prev. known as model kinds) (the columns) that describe the view specification.

The descriptions of all the viewpoints are provided in the following table:

| Viewpoint | Acronym | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Architecture Management | Am | Identifies the metadata required to develop a suitable architecture that is fit for its purpose. |
| Strategic | St | Capability management process. Describes the capability taxonomy, composition, dependencies, and evolution. |
| Operational | Op | Illustrates the Logical Architecture of the enterprise. Describes the requirements, operational behavior, structure, and exchanges required to support (exhibit) capabilities. Defines all operational elements in an implementation/solution-independent manner. |
| Services | Sv | The Service-Orientated View (SOV) is a description of services needed to directly support the operational domain as described in the Operational View. A service withinMODAF is understood in its broadest sense, as a unit of work through which a provider provides a useful result to a consumer.DoDAF: The Service Views within the Services Viewpoint describe the design for service-based solutions to support operational development processes (JCIDS) and Defense Acquisition System or capability development within the Joint Capability Areas. |
| Personnel | Ps | Defines and explores organizational resource types. Shows the taxonomy of types of organizational resources as well as connections, interaction, and growth over time. |
| Resources | Rs | Captures a solution architecture consisting of resources, e.g., organizational, software, artifacts, capability configurations, and natural resources that implement the operational requirements. Further design of a resource is typically detailed in SysML or UML. |
| Security | Sc | Security assets and security enclaves. Defines the hierarchy of security assets and asset owners, security constraints (policy, laws, and guidance), and details where they are located (security enclaves). |
| Projects | Pj | Describes projects and project milestones, how those projects deliver capabilities, the organizations contributing to the projects, and dependencies between projects. |
| Standards | Sd | MODAF: Technical Standards Views are extended from the core DoDAF views to include non-technical standards such as operational doctrine, industry process standards, etc.DoDAF: The Standards Views within the Standards Viewpoint are the set of rules governing the arrangement, interaction, and interdependence of solution parts or elements. |
| Actual Resources | Ar | The analysis, e.g., evaluation of different alternatives, what-if, trade-offs, V&V on the actual resource configurations. Illustrates the expected or achieved actual resource configurations. |

The descriptions of all the aspects are provided in the following table:

| Aspect | Acronym | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Motivation | Mv | Captures motivational elements e.g., challenges, opportunities, and concerns, that pertain to enterprise transformation efforts, and different types of requirements, e.g., operational, services, personnel, resources, or security controls. |
| Taxonomy | Tx | Presents all the elements as a standalone structure. Presents all the elements as a specialization hierarchy, provides a text definition for each one and references the source of the element. |
| Structure | Sr | Describes the breakdown of structural elements e.g. logical performers, systems, projects, etc. into their smaller parts. |
| Connectivity | Cn | Describes the connections, relationships, and interactions between the different elements. |
| Processes | Pr | Captures activity-based behavior and flows. It describes activities, their Inputs/Outputs, activity actions, and flows between them. |
| States | St | Captures state-based behavior of an element. It is a graphical representation of states of a structural element and how it responds to various events and actions. |
| Sequences | Sq | Expresses a time-ordered examination of the exchanges as a result of a particular scenario. Provides a time-ordered examination of the exchanges between participating elements as a result of a particular scenario. |
| Information | If | Address the information perspective on operational, service, and resource architectures. Allows analysis of an architecture’s information and data definition aspect, without consideration of implementation-specific issues. |
| Parameters | Pm | Shows the measurable properties of something in the physical world and elements and relationships that are involved in defining the environments applicable to capability, operational concept or set of systems. |
| Constraints | Ct | Details the measurements that set performance requirements constraining capabilities. Also defines the rules governing behavior and structure. |
| Roadmap | Rm | Addresses how elements in the architecture change over time. |
| Traceability | Tr | Describes the mapping between elements in the architecture. This can be between different viewpoints within domains as well as between domains. It can also be between structure and behaviors. |

769321949

**UAF 1.2 viewpoints**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

It can also be between structure and behaviors.</p></td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e9c81e85-1960-46e5-b272-fe5f846565cb"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">769321949</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>UAF 1.2 viewpoints</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="e448b33b-8517-4791-9a4c-0a2fe6f8dfdf" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227157363 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01182: UAF Behavior to structure synchronization

- page_id: `227157363`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227157363/UAF+Behavior+to+structure+synchronization
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-03-19T12:54:59.184+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The behavior to structure synchronization checks the consistency of the <viewpoint name>***** Internal Connectivity diagram with Activities, automatically detects inconsistencies, and enables users to handle each inconsistency individually by providing solver actions to ensure the coherence of the <viewpoint name>***** Internal Connectivity diagrams with Activities.

600400

The behavior to structure synchronization validates the Operational / Resource / Service Role displayed in the <viewpoint name>***** Internal Connectivity Diagram to check for compatible

- Operational / Resource / Service Ports
- Operational / Resource / Service Connectors
- Operational / Resource / Service Interfaces
- Operational / Resource / Service Exchanges

To verify whether or not the structural elements (e.g., ports, interfaces) and behavioral elements (e.g., flows) are compatible, validation rules are used. If any inconsistencies are found, they are highlighted in yellow in the Internal Connectivity Diagram and displayed in the**Validation Results** panel. Then, they can be individually handled using solvers.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Synchronizing behavior to the structure.

##### Checking behavior to structure synchronization

To initiate the behavior to structure synchronization check, ensure the Internal Connectivity Diagrams have

displayed,

).

To check the structure model consistency with the behavior model

1. In the toolbar of the <viewpoint name> * Internal Connectivity Diagram, click [ATTACHMENT filename='tools_button.png'] Tools.
2. In the open drop-down menu, select Behavior to Structure Synchronization. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

The behavior to structure synchronization check is initiated.

*** <Viewpoint name>** - Operational, Personnel, Resources, Services, Security (Operational and Resource).

**Continue reading**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227158040 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01183: UAF Element

- page_id: `227158040`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227158040/UAF+Element
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T13:25:00.065+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

Abstract super type for all of the UAF elements. It provides a way for all of the UAF elements to have a common set of properties.

**Architecture** **Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

Element

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227157380 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01184: UAF element numbering

- page_id: `227157380`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227157380/UAF+element+numbering
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-02T10:31:03.016+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Our modeling tools provide a generic numbering mechanism that can be applied to any elements created in the modeling tool. The generic numbering mechanism allows you to:

- Automatically assign a unique number to the elements in the model when they are created, moved, or your model is refactored.
- Easily identify and find an element in the model when communicating with colleagues or stakeholders.
- Show element's place in a hierarchy.

For more information about the generic numbering mechanism and how to use it, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generic numbering mechanism' space='MD2024xR3'] documentation.

The default numbering schemas can have two different numbering styles:

- conse **cutive** - this style is to number elements in one numbering level. Specifyonly one number part.
- multi-level - this style is to number elements in several numbering levels. Specify at least one number part for each numbering level. Top level elements will be numbered using the first number part. Elements in subsequent levels (directly or indirectly contained in the top level element) will be numbered using successive number parts.

To learn more about the numbering styles, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Numbering Scheme property values' space='MD2024xR3'] documentation.

UAF numbering uses **multi-level** numbering style schema property.

#### UAF hierarchical numbering

Numbering of UAF motivational elements and information elements is hierarchical.

A hierarchical structure can only contain elements of the same type. Different element types cannot be mixed within the same hierarchy.

Motivational Elements:

- Driver (prefix is D)
- Opportunity (prefix is O)
- Challenge (prefix is C)
- Effect (prefix is E)

Information Elements:

- Operational Information (prefix is OI)
- Resource Information (prefix is RI)
- Strategic Information (prefix is SI)

#### UAF hierarchical numbering with dynamic prefixes

Numbering of UAF elements based on SysML requirements is hierarchical or dynamic (when prefix differs, numbering adapts. numbers will change, but prefixes will be kept according to elements).

A hierarchical structure can contain elements of different type. For example, Enterprise Goal and Enterprise Objective can both be in the same hierarchical structure. The same is with the Security elements.

Strategic Elements:

- Enterprise Goal (prefix is EG)
- Enterprise Objective (prefix is EO)

Security Elements:

- Security Control (prefix is SC)
- Enhanced Security Control (prefix is ESC)
- Security Control Family (prefix is SCF)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generic numbering mechanism' space='MD2024xR3']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The generic numbering mechanism allows you to:</p><ul><li>Automatically assign a unique number to the elements in the model when they are created, moved, or your model is refactored.</li><li>Easily identify and find an element in the model when communicating with colleagues or stakeholders.</li><li>Show element's place in a hierarchy.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7a60c08f-6ebe-4283-a166-55e4cf8ae3de"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more information about the generic numbering mechanism and how to use it, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" ri:content-title="Generic numbering mechanism" /></ac:link> documentation.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The default <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="b837aa41-4b6a-43fa-8052-ea7293365d18">numbering</ac:inline-comment-marker> schemas can have two different numbering styles:</p><ul><li><strong>conse</strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="bef9ad5d-ed4a-4f76-8438-cd73baa3324d"><strong>cutive</strong> - this style is to <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">number elements in one numbering level. Specify<span> </span></span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">only one number part.</span></ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="bef9ad5d-ed4a-4f76-8438-cd73baa3324d">multi-le</ac:inline-comment-marker>vel</strong> - this style is <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">to number elements in several numbering levels. Specify at least one number part for each numbering level. </span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Top level elements will be numbered using the first number part. Elements in subsequent levels (directly or indirectly contained in the top level element) will be numbered using successive number parts.</span></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="148ffb90-68bf-4b29-be24-74712b4da5db"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To learn more about the numbering styles, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" ri:content-title="Numbering Scheme property values" /></ac:link> documentation.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">UAF numbering uses <strong>multi-level</strong> numbering style schema property.</span></p><h2>UAF hierarchical numbering</h2><p>Numbering of UAF motivational elements and information <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="8482ff7a-d0f3-4175-a938-7cd8dbbde8ef">elements is hierarchical. </ac:inline-comment-marker></p><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="8482ff7a-d0f3-4175-a938-7cd8dbbde8ef">A hierarchical structure can only contain elements of the same type. Different element types cannot be mixed within the same hierarchy.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Motivational Elements:</span></p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li>Driver (prefix is D)</li><li>Opportunity (prefix is O)</li><li>Challenge (prefix is C)</li><li>Effect (prefix is E)</li></ul><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Information Elements: </span></p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li>Operational Information (prefix is OI)</li><li>Resource Information (prefix is RI)</li><li>Strategic Information (prefix is SI)</li></ul><h2>UAF hierarchical numbering with dynamic prefixes</h2><p>Numbering of UAF <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="78a84ff1-a965-4790-b198-6a7c92402db0">elements based on SysML requirement</ac:inline-comment-marker>s is hierarchical or dynamic (when prefix differs, numbering adapts. numbers will change, but prefixes will be kept according to elements).</p><p>A hierarchical structure can contain elements of different type. For example, Enterprise Goal and Enterprise Objective can both be in the same hierarchical structure. The same is with the Security elements.</p><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Strategic Elements:</span></p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li>Enterprise Goal (prefix is EG)</li><li>Enterprise Objective (prefix is EO)</li></ul><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Security Elements:</span></p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li>Security Control (prefix is SC)</li><li>Enhanced Security Control (prefix is ESC)</li><li>Security Control Family (prefix is SCF)</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="hierarchical_element_numbering.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="UAF element numbering" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR3" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" ri:content-title="Generic numbering mechanism" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227158067 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01185: UAF projects migration manual

- page_id: `227158067`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`

- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides

This manual aims to help you understand the differences between UAF specifications and guide you along the data mapping process.

The following chapters describe how migration works.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=227158053 space=UAF12P2024xR3 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01186: UAF specific elements

- page_id: `227158053`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR3`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR3/pages/227158053/UAF+specific+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-04-30T07:44:47.582+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

| Metaclass | Name | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Association | Operational Association | A relationship summarizing Operational Exchanges between connected Operational Performers. |
| Resource Association | A relationship summarizing Resource Exchanges between connected Resources. |  |
| Call Behavior Action | Operational Action | A Call Behavior Action for which the default type will not be created. However if you need to create or set a type for this element, you can select or create only an Operational Activity. |
| Resource Action | A Call Behavior Action for which the default type will not be created. However if you need to create or set a type for this element, you can select or create only a Function. |  |
| Service Action | A Call Behavior Action for which the default type will not be created. However if you need to create or set a type for this element, you can select or create only a Service Function. |  |
| Class | Time Period | An element which contains Date elements. |
| Dependency | Arbitrary Relationship Dashed Left | A relationship which you can used in a Free Form diagram to show dependencies. |
| Arbitrary Relationship Dashed Right |  |  |
| Arbitrary Relationship Directed Left |  |  |
| Arbitrary Relationship Directed Right |  |  |
| Arbitrary Relationship Solid |  |  |
| Property | Agreement Concept Role | A Concept Role which type is Agreement. |
| Capability Configuration Concept Role | A Concept Role which type is Capability Configuration. |  |
| Condition Concept Role | A Concept Role which type is Condition. |  |
| Environment Concept Role | A Concept Role which type is Environment. |  |
| Location Concept Role | A Concept Role which type is Location. |  |
| Natural Resource Concept Role | A Concept Role which type is Natural Resource. |  |
| Operational Concept Role | A Concept Role which type is Operational Performer. |  |
| Organization Concept Role | A Concept Role which type is Organization. |  |
| Person Concept Role | A Concept Role which type is Person. |  |
| Post Concept Role | A Concept Role which type is Post. |  |
| Resource Artifact Concept Role | A Concept Role which type is Resource Artifact. |  |
| Software Concept Role | A Concept Role which type is Software. |  |
| Technology Concept Role | A Concept Role which type is Technology. |  |
